3467(1 row)
3468
3469-- User defined function for user defined aggregate, VARIADIC
3470create function least_accum(anyelement, variadic anyarray)
3471returns anyelement language sql as
3472  'select least($1, min($2[i])) from generate_subscripts($2,1) g(i)';
3473create aggregate least_agg(variadic items anyarray) (
3474  stype = anyelement, sfunc = least_accum
3475);
3476-- Disable hash aggregation for plan stability.
3477set enable_hashagg to false;
3478-- Not pushed down due to user defined aggregate
3479explain (verbose, costs off)
3480select c2, least_agg(c1) from ft1 group by c2 order by c2;
3481                                    QUERY PLAN                                    
3482----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3483 GroupAggregate
3484   Output: c2, least_agg(VARIADIC ARRAY[c1])
3485   Group Key: ft1.c2
3486   ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft1
3487         Output: c2, c1
3488         Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2 FROM "S 1"."T 1" ORDER BY c2 ASC NULLS LAST
3489(6 rows)
3490
3491-- Add function and aggregate into extension
3492alter extension postgres_fdw add function least_accum(anyelement, variadic anyarray);
3493alter extension postgres_fdw add aggregate least_agg(variadic items anyarray);
3494alter server loopback options (set extensions 'postgres_fdw');
3495-- Now aggregate will be pushed.  Aggregate will display VARIADIC argument.
3496explain (verbose, costs off)
3497select c2, least_agg(c1) from ft1 where c2 < 100 group by c2 order by c2;
3498                                                      QUERY PLAN                                                       
3499-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3500 Sort
3501   Output: c2, (least_agg(VARIADIC ARRAY[c1]))
3502   Sort Key: ft1.c2
3503   ->  Foreign Scan
3504         Output: c2, (least_agg(VARIADIC ARRAY[c1]))
3505         Relations: Aggregate on (public.ft1)
3506         Remote SQL: SELECT c2, public.least_agg(VARIADIC ARRAY["C 1"]) FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE ((c2 < 100)) GROUP BY 1
3507(7 rows)
3508
3509select c2, least_agg(c1) from ft1 where c2 < 100 group by c2 order by c2;
3510 c2 | least_agg 
3511----+-----------
3512  0 |        10
3513  1 |         1
3514  2 |         2
3515  3 |         3
3516  4 |         4
3517  5 |         5
3518  6 |         6
3519  7 |         7
3520  8 |         8
3521  9 |         9
3522(10 rows)
3523
3524-- Remove function and aggregate from extension
3525alter extension postgres_fdw drop function least_accum(anyelement, variadic anyarray);
3526alter extension postgres_fdw drop aggregate least_agg(variadic items anyarray);
3527alter server loopback options (set extensions 'postgres_fdw');
3528-- Not pushed down as we have dropped objects from extension.
3529explain (verbose, costs off)
3530select c2, least_agg(c1) from ft1 group by c2 order by c2;
3531                                    QUERY PLAN                                    
3532----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3533 GroupAggregate
3534   Output: c2, least_agg(VARIADIC ARRAY[c1])
3535   Group Key: ft1.c2
3536   ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft1
3537         Output: c2, c1
3538         Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2 FROM "S 1"."T 1" ORDER BY c2 ASC NULLS LAST
3539(6 rows)
3540
3541-- Cleanup
3542reset enable_hashagg;
3543drop aggregate least_agg(variadic items anyarray);
3544drop function least_accum(anyelement, variadic anyarray);
3545-- Testing USING OPERATOR() in ORDER BY within aggregate.
3546-- For this, we need user defined operators along with operator family and
3547-- operator class.  Create those and then add them in extension.  Note that
3548-- user defined objects are considered unshippable unless they are part of
3549-- the extension.
3550create operator public.<^ (
3551 leftarg = int4,
3552 rightarg = int4,
3553 procedure = int4eq
3554);
3555create operator public.=^ (
3556 leftarg = int4,
3557 rightarg = int4,
3558 procedure = int4lt
3559);
3560create operator public.>^ (
3561 leftarg = int4,
3562 rightarg = int4,
3563 procedure = int4gt
3564);
3565create operator family my_op_family using btree;
3566create function my_op_cmp(a int, b int) returns int as
3567  $$begin return btint4cmp(a, b); end $$ language plpgsql;
3568create operator class my_op_class for type int using btree family my_op_family as
3569 operator 1 public.<^,
3570 operator 3 public.=^,
3571 operator 5 public.>^,
3572 function 1 my_op_cmp(int, int);
3573-- This will not be pushed as user defined sort operator is not part of the
3574-- extension yet.
3575explain (verbose, costs off)
3576select array_agg(c1 order by c1 using operator(public.<^)) from ft2 where c2 = 6 and c1 < 100 group by c2;
3577                                            QUERY PLAN                                            
3578--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3579 GroupAggregate
3580   Output: array_agg(c1 ORDER BY c1 USING <^ NULLS LAST), c2
3581   ->  Sort
3582         Output: c1, c2
3583         Sort Key: ft2.c1 USING <^
3584         ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft2
3585               Output: c1, c2
3586               Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE (("C 1" < 100)) AND ((c2 = 6))
3587(8 rows)
3588
3589-- This should not be pushed either.
3590explain (verbose, costs off)
3591select * from ft2 order by c1 using operator(public.<^);
3592                                  QUERY PLAN                                   
3593-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3594 Sort
3595   Output: c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8
3596   Sort Key: ft2.c1 USING <^
3597   ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft2
3598         Output: c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8
3599         Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8 FROM "S 1"."T 1"
3600(6 rows)
3601
3602-- Update local stats on ft2
3603ANALYZE ft2;
3604-- Add into extension
3605alter extension postgres_fdw add operator class my_op_class using btree;
3606alter extension postgres_fdw add function my_op_cmp(a int, b int);
3607alter extension postgres_fdw add operator family my_op_family using btree;
3608alter extension postgres_fdw add operator public.<^(int, int);
3609alter extension postgres_fdw add operator public.=^(int, int);
3610alter extension postgres_fdw add operator public.>^(int, int);
3611alter server loopback options (set extensions 'postgres_fdw');
3612-- Now this will be pushed as sort operator is part of the extension.
3613alter server loopback options (add fdw_tuple_cost '0.5');
3614explain (verbose, costs off)
3615select array_agg(c1 order by c1 using operator(public.<^)) from ft2 where c2 = 6 and c1 < 100 group by c2;
3616                                                                           QUERY PLAN                                                                           
3617----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3618 Foreign Scan
3619   Output: (array_agg(c1 ORDER BY c1 USING <^ NULLS LAST)), c2
3620   Relations: Aggregate on (public.ft2)
3621   Remote SQL: SELECT array_agg("C 1" ORDER BY "C 1" USING OPERATOR(public.<^) NULLS LAST), c2 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE (("C 1" < 100)) AND ((c2 = 6)) GROUP BY 2
3622(4 rows)
3623
3624select array_agg(c1 order by c1 using operator(public.<^)) from ft2 where c2 = 6 and c1 < 100 group by c2;
3625           array_agg            
3626--------------------------------
3627 {6,16,26,36,46,56,66,76,86,96}
3628(1 row)
3629
3630alter server loopback options (drop fdw_tuple_cost);
3631-- This should be pushed too.
3632explain (verbose, costs off)
3633select * from ft2 order by c1 using operator(public.<^);
3634                                                         QUERY PLAN                                                          
3635-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3636 Foreign Scan on public.ft2
3637   Output: c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8
3638   Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8 FROM "S 1"."T 1" ORDER BY "C 1" USING OPERATOR(public.<^) NULLS LAST
3639(3 rows)
3640
3641-- Remove from extension
3642alter extension postgres_fdw drop operator class my_op_class using btree;
3643alter extension postgres_fdw drop function my_op_cmp(a int, b int);
3644alter extension postgres_fdw drop operator family my_op_family using btree;
3645alter extension postgres_fdw drop operator public.<^(int, int);
3646alter extension postgres_fdw drop operator public.=^(int, int);
3647alter extension postgres_fdw drop operator public.>^(int, int);
3648alter server loopback options (set extensions 'postgres_fdw');
3649-- This will not be pushed as sort operator is now removed from the extension.
3650explain (verbose, costs off)
3651select array_agg(c1 order by c1 using operator(public.<^)) from ft2 where c2 = 6 and c1 < 100 group by c2;
3652                                            QUERY PLAN                                            
3653--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3654 GroupAggregate
3655   Output: array_agg(c1 ORDER BY c1 USING <^ NULLS LAST), c2
3656   ->  Sort
3657         Output: c1, c2
3658         Sort Key: ft2.c1 USING <^
3659         ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft2
3660               Output: c1, c2
3661               Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE (("C 1" < 100)) AND ((c2 = 6))
3662(8 rows)
3663
3664-- Cleanup
3665drop operator class my_op_class using btree;
3666drop function my_op_cmp(a int, b int);
3667drop operator family my_op_family using btree;
3668drop operator public.>^(int, int);
3669drop operator public.=^(int, int);
3670drop operator public.<^(int, int);
3671-- Input relation to aggregate push down hook is not safe to pushdown and thus
3672-- the aggregate cannot be pushed down to foreign server.
3673explain (verbose, costs off)
3674select count(t1.c3) from ft2 t1 left join ft2 t2 on (t1.c1 = random() * t2.c2);
3675                                        QUERY PLAN                                         
3676-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3677 Aggregate
3678   Output: count(t1.c3)
3679   ->  Nested Loop Left Join
3680         Output: t1.c3
3681         Join Filter: ((t1.c1)::double precision = (random() * (t2.c2)::double precision))
3682         ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft2 t1
3683               Output: t1.c3, t1.c1
3684               Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c3 FROM "S 1"."T 1"
3685         ->  Materialize
3686               Output: t2.c2
3687               ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft2 t2
3688                     Output: t2.c2
3689                     Remote SQL: SELECT c2 FROM "S 1"."T 1"
3690(13 rows)
3691
3692-- Subquery in FROM clause having aggregate
3693explain (verbose, costs off)
3694select count(*), x.b from ft1, (select c2 a, sum(c1) b from ft1 group by c2) x where ft1.c2 = x.a group by x.b order by 1, 2;
3695                                          QUERY PLAN                                           
3696-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3697 Sort
3698   Output: (count(*)), x.b
3699   Sort Key: (count(*)), x.b
3700   ->  HashAggregate
3701         Output: count(*), x.b
3702         Group Key: x.b
3703         ->  Hash Join
3704               Output: x.b
3705               Inner Unique: true
3706               Hash Cond: (ft1.c2 = x.a)
3707               ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft1
3708                     Output: ft1.c2
3709                     Remote SQL: SELECT c2 FROM "S 1"."T 1"
3710               ->  Hash
3711                     Output: x.b, x.a
3712                     ->  Subquery Scan on x
3713                           Output: x.b, x.a
3714                           ->  Foreign Scan
3715                                 Output: ft1_1.c2, (sum(ft1_1.c1))
3716                                 Relations: Aggregate on (public.ft1 ft1_1)
3717                                 Remote SQL: SELECT c2, sum("C 1") FROM "S 1"."T 1" GROUP BY 1
3718(21 rows)
3719
3720select count(*), x.b from ft1, (select c2 a, sum(c1) b from ft1 group by c2) x where ft1.c2 = x.a group by x.b order by 1, 2;
3721 count |   b   
3722-------+-------
3723   100 | 49600
3724   100 | 49700
3725   100 | 49800
3726   100 | 49900
3727   100 | 50000
3728   100 | 50100
3729   100 | 50200
3730   100 | 50300
3731   100 | 50400
3732   100 | 50500
3733(10 rows)

3735-- FULL join with IS NULL check in HAVING
3736explain (verbose, costs off)
3737select avg(t1.c1), sum(t2.c1) from ft4 t1 full join ft5 t2 on (t1.c1 = t2.c1) group by t2.c1 having (avg(t1.c1) is null and sum(t2.c1) < 10) or sum(t2.c1) is null order by 1 nulls last, 2;
3738                                                                                                                                    QUERY PLAN                                                                                                                                     
3739-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3740 Foreign Scan
3741   Output: (avg(t1.c1)), (sum(t2.c1)), t2.c1
3742   Relations: Aggregate on ((public.ft4 t1) FULL JOIN (public.ft5 t2))
3743   Remote SQL: SELECT avg(r1.c1), sum(r2.c1), r2.c1 FROM ("S 1"."T 3" r1 FULL JOIN "S 1"."T 4" r2 ON (((r1.c1 = r2.c1)))) GROUP BY 3 HAVING ((((avg(r1.c1) IS NULL) AND (sum(r2.c1) < 10)) OR (sum(r2.c1) IS NULL))) ORDER BY avg(r1.c1) ASC NULLS LAST, sum(r2.c1) ASC NULLS LAST
3744(4 rows)
3745
3746select avg(t1.c1), sum(t2.c1) from ft4 t1 full join ft5 t2 on (t1.c1 = t2.c1) group by t2.c1 having (avg(t1.c1) is null and sum(t2.c1) < 10) or sum(t2.c1) is null order by 1 nulls last, 2;
3747         avg         | sum 
3748---------------------+-----
3749 51.0000000000000000 |    
3750                     |   3
3751                     |   9
3752(3 rows)
3753
3754-- Aggregate over FULL join needing to deparse the joining relations as
3755-- subqueries.
3756explain (verbose, costs off)
3757select count(*), sum(t1.c1), avg(t2.c1) from (select c1 from ft4 where c1 between 50 and 60) t1 full join (select c1 from ft5 where c1 between 50 and 60) t2 on (t1.c1 = t2.c1);
3758                                                                                                                  QUERY PLAN                                                                                                                   
3759-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3760 Foreign Scan
3761   Output: (count(*)), (sum(ft4.c1)), (avg(ft5.c1))
3762   Relations: Aggregate on ((public.ft4) FULL JOIN (public.ft5))
3763   Remote SQL: SELECT count(*), sum(s4.c1), avg(s5.c1) FROM ((SELECT c1 FROM "S 1"."T 3" WHERE ((c1 >= 50)) AND ((c1 <= 60))) s4(c1) FULL JOIN (SELECT c1 FROM "S 1"."T 4" WHERE ((c1 >= 50)) AND ((c1 <= 60))) s5(c1) ON (((s4.c1 = s5.c1))))
3764(4 rows)
3765
3766select count(*), sum(t1.c1), avg(t2.c1) from (select c1 from ft4 where c1 between 50 and 60) t1 full join (select c1 from ft5 where c1 between 50 and 60) t2 on (t1.c1 = t2.c1);
3767 count | sum |         avg         
3768-------+-----+---------------------
3769     8 | 330 | 55.5000000000000000
3770(1 row)
3771
3772-- ORDER BY expression is part of the target list but not pushed down to
3773-- foreign server.
3774explain (verbose, costs off)
3775select sum(c2) * (random() <= 1)::int as sum from ft1 order by 1;
3776                                   QUERY PLAN                                   
3777--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3778 Sort
3779   Output: (((sum(c2)) * ((random() <= '1'::double precision))::integer))
3780   Sort Key: (((sum(ft1.c2)) * ((random() <= '1'::double precision))::integer))
3781   ->  Foreign Scan
3782         Output: ((sum(c2)) * ((random() <= '1'::double precision))::integer)
3783         Relations: Aggregate on (public.ft1)
3784         Remote SQL: SELECT sum(c2) FROM "S 1"."T 1"
3785(7 rows)
3786
3787select sum(c2) * (random() <= 1)::int as sum from ft1 order by 1;
3788 sum  
3789------
3790 4500
3791(1 row)
3792
3793-- LATERAL join, with parameterization
3794set enable_hashagg to false;
3795explain (verbose, costs off)
3796select c2, sum from "S 1"."T 1" t1, lateral (select sum(t2.c1 + t1."C 1") sum from ft2 t2 group by t2.c1) qry where t1.c2 * 2 = qry.sum and t1.c2 < 3 and t1."C 1" < 100 order by 1;
3797                                              QUERY PLAN                                              
3798------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3799 Sort
3800   Output: t1.c2, qry.sum
3801   Sort Key: t1.c2
3802   ->  Nested Loop
3803         Output: t1.c2, qry.sum
3804         ->  Index Scan using t1_pkey on "S 1"."T 1" t1
3805               Output: t1."C 1", t1.c2, t1.c3, t1.c4, t1.c5, t1.c6, t1.c7, t1.c8
3806               Index Cond: (t1."C 1" < 100)
3807               Filter: (t1.c2 < 3)
3808         ->  Subquery Scan on qry
3809               Output: qry.sum, t2.c1
3810               Filter: ((t1.c2 * 2) = qry.sum)
3811               ->  Foreign Scan
3812                     Output: (sum((t2.c1 + t1."C 1"))), t2.c1
3813                     Relations: Aggregate on (public.ft2 t2)
3814                     Remote SQL: SELECT sum(("C 1" + $1::integer)), "C 1" FROM "S 1"."T 1" GROUP BY 2
3815(16 rows)
3816
3817select c2, sum from "S 1"."T 1" t1, lateral (select sum(t2.c1 + t1."C 1") sum from ft2 t2 group by t2.c1) qry where t1.c2 * 2 = qry.sum and t1.c2 < 3 and t1."C 1" < 100 order by 1;
3818 c2 | sum 
3819----+-----
3820  1 |   2
3821  2 |   4
3822(2 rows)
3823
3824reset enable_hashagg;
3825-- bug #15613: bad plan for foreign table scan with lateral reference
3826EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
3827SELECT ref_0.c2, subq_1.*
3828FROM
3829    "S 1"."T 1" AS ref_0,
3830    LATERAL (
3831        SELECT ref_0."C 1" c1, subq_0.*
3832        FROM (SELECT ref_0.c2, ref_1.c3
3833              FROM ft1 AS ref_1) AS subq_0
3834             RIGHT JOIN ft2 AS ref_3 ON (subq_0.c3 = ref_3.c3)
3835    ) AS subq_1
3836WHERE ref_0."C 1" < 10 AND subq_1.c3 = '00001'
3837ORDER BY ref_0."C 1";
3838                                               QUERY PLAN                                                
3839---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3840 Nested Loop
3841   Output: ref_0.c2, ref_0."C 1", (ref_0.c2), ref_1.c3, ref_0."C 1"
3842   ->  Nested Loop
3843         Output: ref_0.c2, ref_0."C 1", ref_1.c3, (ref_0.c2)
3844         ->  Index Scan using t1_pkey on "S 1"."T 1" ref_0
3845               Output: ref_0."C 1", ref_0.c2, ref_0.c3, ref_0.c4, ref_0.c5, ref_0.c6, ref_0.c7, ref_0.c8
3846               Index Cond: (ref_0."C 1" < 10)
3847         ->  Memoize
3848               Output: ref_1.c3, (ref_0.c2)
3849               Cache Key: ref_0.c2
3850               Cache Mode: binary
3851               ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft1 ref_1
3852                     Output: ref_1.c3, ref_0.c2
3853                     Remote SQL: SELECT c3 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE ((c3 = '00001'))
3854   ->  Materialize
3855         Output: ref_3.c3
3856         ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft2 ref_3
3857               Output: ref_3.c3
3858               Remote SQL: SELECT c3 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE ((c3 = '00001'))
3859(19 rows)
3860
3861SELECT ref_0.c2, subq_1.*
3862FROM
3863    "S 1"."T 1" AS ref_0,
3864    LATERAL (
3865        SELECT ref_0."C 1" c1, subq_0.*
3866        FROM (SELECT ref_0.c2, ref_1.c3
3867              FROM ft1 AS ref_1) AS subq_0
3868             RIGHT JOIN ft2 AS ref_3 ON (subq_0.c3 = ref_3.c3)
3869    ) AS subq_1
3870WHERE ref_0."C 1" < 10 AND subq_1.c3 = '00001'
3871ORDER BY ref_0."C 1";
3872 c2 | c1 | c2 |  c3   
3873----+----+----+-------
3874  1 |  1 |  1 | 00001
3875  2 |  2 |  2 | 00001
3876  3 |  3 |  3 | 00001
3877  4 |  4 |  4 | 00001
3878  5 |  5 |  5 | 00001
3879  6 |  6 |  6 | 00001
3880  7 |  7 |  7 | 00001
3881  8 |  8 |  8 | 00001
3882  9 |  9 |  9 | 00001
3883(9 rows)
3884
3885-- Check with placeHolderVars
3886explain (verbose, costs off)
3887select sum(q.a), count(q.b) from ft4 left join (select 13, avg(ft1.c1), sum(ft2.c1) from ft1 right join ft2 on (ft1.c1 = ft2.c1)) q(a, b, c) on (ft4.c1 <= q.b);
3888                                                                        QUERY PLAN                                                                        
3889----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3890 Aggregate
3891   Output: sum(q.a), count(q.b)
3892   ->  Nested Loop Left Join
3893         Output: q.a, q.b
3894         Inner Unique: true
3895         Join Filter: ((ft4.c1)::numeric <= q.b)
3896         ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft4
3897               Output: ft4.c1, ft4.c2, ft4.c3
3898               Remote SQL: SELECT c1 FROM "S 1"."T 3"
3899         ->  Materialize
3900               Output: q.a, q.b
3901               ->  Subquery Scan on q
3902                     Output: q.a, q.b
3903                     ->  Foreign Scan
3904                           Output: 13, (avg(ft1.c1)), NULL::bigint
3905                           Relations: Aggregate on ((public.ft2) LEFT JOIN (public.ft1))
3906                           Remote SQL: SELECT 13, avg(r1."C 1"), NULL::bigint FROM ("S 1"."T 1" r2 LEFT JOIN "S 1"."T 1" r1 ON (((r1."C 1" = r2."C 1"))))
3907(17 rows)
3908
3909select sum(q.a), count(q.b) from ft4 left join (select 13, avg(ft1.c1), sum(ft2.c1) from ft1 right join ft2 on (ft1.c1 = ft2.c1)) q(a, b, c) on (ft4.c1 <= q.b);
3910 sum | count 
3911-----+-------
3912 650 |    50
3913(1 row)

3915-- Not supported cases
3916-- Grouping sets
3917explain (verbose, costs off)
3918select c2, sum(c1) from ft1 where c2 < 3 group by rollup(c2) order by 1 nulls last;
3919                                  QUERY PLAN                                  
3920------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3921 Sort
3922   Output: c2, (sum(c1))
3923   Sort Key: ft1.c2
3924   ->  MixedAggregate
3925         Output: c2, sum(c1)
3926         Hash Key: ft1.c2
3927         Group Key: ()
3928         ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft1
3929               Output: c2, c1
3930               Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE ((c2 < 3))
3931(10 rows)
3932

3952         Group Key: ()

3977         Hash Key: ft1.c6
3978         ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft1

3981(10 rows)

4017-- DISTINCT itself is not pushed down, whereas underneath aggregate is pushed
4018explain (verbose, costs off)
4019select distinct sum(c1)/1000 s from ft2 where c2 < 6 group by c2 order by 1;
4020                                              QUERY PLAN                                               
4021-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4022 Unique
4023   Output: ((sum(c1) / 1000)), c2
4024   ->  Sort
4025         Output: ((sum(c1) / 1000)), c2
4026         Sort Key: ((sum(ft2.c1) / 1000))
4027         ->  Foreign Scan
4028               Output: ((sum(c1) / 1000)), c2
4029               Relations: Aggregate on (public.ft2)
4030               Remote SQL: SELECT (sum("C 1") / 1000), c2 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE ((c2 < 6)) GROUP BY 2
4031(9 rows)
4032
4033select distinct sum(c1)/1000 s from ft2 where c2 < 6 group by c2 order by 1;
4034 s  
4035----
4036 49
4037 50
4038(2 rows)
4039
4040-- WindowAgg
4041explain (verbose, costs off)
4042select c2, sum(c2), count(c2) over (partition by c2%2) from ft2 where c2 < 10 group by c2 order by 1;
4043                                                 QUERY PLAN                                                 
4044------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4045 Sort
4046   Output: c2, (sum(c2)), (count(c2) OVER w1), ((c2 % 2))
4047   Sort Key: ft2.c2
4048   ->  WindowAgg
4049         Output: c2, (sum(c2)), count(c2) OVER w1, ((c2 % 2))
4050         Window: w1 AS (PARTITION BY ((ft2.c2 % 2)))
4051         ->  Sort
4052               Output: c2, ((c2 % 2)), (sum(c2))
4053               Sort Key: ((ft2.c2 % 2))
4054               ->  Foreign Scan
4055                     Output: c2, ((c2 % 2)), (sum(c2))
4056                     Relations: Aggregate on (public.ft2)
4057                     Remote SQL: SELECT c2, (c2 % 2), sum(c2) FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE ((c2 < 10)) GROUP BY 1
4058(13 rows)
4059
4060select c2, sum(c2), count(c2) over (partition by c2%2) from ft2 where c2 < 10 group by c2 order by 1;
4061 c2 | sum | count 
4062----+-----+-------
4063  0 |   0 |     5
4064  1 | 100 |     5
4065  2 | 200 |     5
4066  3 | 300 |     5
4067  4 | 400 |     5
4068  5 | 500 |     5
4069  6 | 600 |     5
4070  7 | 700 |     5
4071  8 | 800 |     5
4072  9 | 900 |     5
4073(10 rows)
4074
4075explain (verbose, costs off)
4076select c2, array_agg(c2) over (partition by c2%2 order by c2 desc) from ft1 where c2 < 10 group by c2 order by 1;
4077                                            QUERY PLAN                                             
4078---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4079 Sort
4080   Output: c2, (array_agg(c2) OVER w1), ((c2 % 2))
4081   Sort Key: ft1.c2
4082   ->  WindowAgg
4083         Output: c2, array_agg(c2) OVER w1, ((c2 % 2))
4084         Window: w1 AS (PARTITION BY ((ft1.c2 % 2)) ORDER BY ft1.c2)
4085         ->  Sort
4086               Output: c2, ((c2 % 2))
4087               Sort Key: ((ft1.c2 % 2)), ft1.c2 DESC
4088               ->  Foreign Scan
4089                     Output: c2, ((c2 % 2))
4090                     Relations: Aggregate on (public.ft1)
4091                     Remote SQL: SELECT c2, (c2 % 2) FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE ((c2 < 10)) GROUP BY 1
4092(13 rows)
4093
4094select c2, array_agg(c2) over (partition by c2%2 order by c2 desc) from ft1 where c2 < 10 group by c2 order by 1;
4095 c2 |  array_agg  
4096----+-------------
4097  0 | {8,6,4,2,0}
4098  1 | {9,7,5,3,1}
4099  2 | {8,6,4,2}
4100  3 | {9,7,5,3}
4101  4 | {8,6,4}
4102  5 | {9,7,5}
4103  6 | {8,6}
4104  7 | {9,7}
4105  8 | {8}
4106  9 | {9}
4107(10 rows)
4108
4109explain (verbose, costs off)
4110select c2, array_agg(c2) over (partition by c2%2 order by c2 range between current row and unbounded following) from ft1 where c2 < 10 group by c2 order by 1;
4111                                                      QUERY PLAN                                                       
4112-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4113 Sort
4114   Output: c2, (array_agg(c2) OVER w1), ((c2 % 2))
4115   Sort Key: ft1.c2
4116   ->  WindowAgg
4117         Output: c2, array_agg(c2) OVER w1, ((c2 % 2))
4118         Window: w1 AS (PARTITION BY ((ft1.c2 % 2)) ORDER BY ft1.c2 RANGE BETWEEN CURRENT ROW AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING)
4119         ->  Sort
4120               Output: c2, ((c2 % 2))
4121               Sort Key: ((ft1.c2 % 2)), ft1.c2
4122               ->  Foreign Scan
4123                     Output: c2, ((c2 % 2))
4124                     Relations: Aggregate on (public.ft1)
4125                     Remote SQL: SELECT c2, (c2 % 2) FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE ((c2 < 10)) GROUP BY 1
4126(13 rows)
4127
4128select c2, array_agg(c2) over (partition by c2%2 order by c2 range between current row and unbounded following) from ft1 where c2 < 10 group by c2 order by 1;

4130----+-------------

4143-- ===================================================================
4144-- parameterized queries
4145-- ===================================================================
4146-- simple join
4147PREPARE st1(int, int) AS SELECT t1.c3, t2.c3 FROM ft1 t1, ft2 t2 WHERE t1.c1 = $1 AND t2.c1 = $2;
4148EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) EXECUTE st1(1, 2);
4149                                                          QUERY PLAN                                                          
4150------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4151 Foreign Scan
4152   Output: t1.c3, t2.c3
4153   Relations: (public.ft1 t1) INNER JOIN (public.ft2 t2)
4154   Remote SQL: SELECT r1.c3, r2.c3 FROM ("S 1"."T 1" r1 INNER JOIN "S 1"."T 1" r2 ON (((r2."C 1" = 2)) AND ((r1."C 1" = 1))))
4155(4 rows)
4156
4157EXECUTE st1(1, 1);
4158  c3   |  c3   
4159-------+-------
4160 00001 | 00001
4161(1 row)
4162
4163EXECUTE st1(101, 101);
4164  c3   |  c3   
4165-------+-------
4166 00101 | 00101
4167(1 row)
4168
4169SET enable_hashjoin TO off;
4170SET enable_sort TO off;
4171-- subquery using stable function (can't be sent to remote)
4172PREPARE st2(int) AS SELECT * FROM ft1 t1 WHERE t1.c1 < $2 AND t1.c3 IN (SELECT c3 FROM ft2 t2 WHERE c1 > $1 AND date(c4) = '1970-01-17'::date) ORDER BY c1;
4173EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) EXECUTE st2(10, 20);
4174                                                            QUERY PLAN                                                            
4175----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4176 Nested Loop Semi Join
4177   Output: t1.c1, t1.c2, t1.c3, t1.c4, t1.c5, t1.c6, t1.c7, t1.c8
4178   Join Filter: (t2.c3 = t1.c3)
4179   ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft1 t1
4180         Output: t1.c1, t1.c2, t1.c3, t1.c4, t1.c5, t1.c6, t1.c7, t1.c8
4181         Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE (("C 1" < 20)) ORDER BY "C 1" ASC NULLS LAST
4182   ->  Materialize
4183         Output: t2.c3
4184         ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft2 t2
4185               Output: t2.c3
4186               Filter: (date(t2.c4) = '01-17-1970'::date)
4187               Remote SQL: SELECT c3, c4 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE (("C 1" > 10))
4188(12 rows)
4189
4190EXECUTE st2(10, 20);
4191 c1 | c2 |  c3   |              c4              |            c5            | c6 |     c7     | c8  
4192----+----+-------+------------------------------+--------------------------+----+------------+-----
4193 16 |  6 | 00016 | Sat Jan 17 00:00:00 1970 PST | Sat Jan 17 00:00:00 1970 | 6  | 6          | foo
4194(1 row)
4195
4196EXECUTE st2(101, 121);
4197 c1  | c2 |  c3   |              c4              |            c5            | c6 |     c7     | c8  
4198-----+----+-------+------------------------------+--------------------------+----+------------+-----
4199 116 |  6 | 00116 | Sat Jan 17 00:00:00 1970 PST | Sat Jan 17 00:00:00 1970 | 6  | 6          | foo
4200(1 row)
4201
4202RESET enable_hashjoin;
4203RESET enable_sort;
4204-- subquery using immutable function (can be sent to remote)
4205PREPARE st3(int) AS SELECT * FROM ft1 t1 WHERE t1.c1 < $2 AND t1.c3 IN (SELECT c3 FROM ft2 t2 WHERE c1 > $1 AND date(c5) = '1970-01-17'::date) ORDER BY c1;
4206EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) EXECUTE st3(10, 20);
4207                                                                                                                              QUERY PLAN                                                                                                                              
4208----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4209 Sort
4210   Output: t1.c1, t1.c2, t1.c3, t1.c4, t1.c5, t1.c6, t1.c7, t1.c8
4211   Sort Key: t1.c1
4212   ->  Foreign Scan
4213         Output: t1.c1, t1.c2, t1.c3, t1.c4, t1.c5, t1.c6, t1.c7, t1.c8
4214         Relations: (public.ft1 t1) SEMI JOIN (public.ft2 t2)
4215         Remote SQL: SELECT r1."C 1", r1.c2, r1.c3, r1.c4, r1.c5, r1.c6, r1.c7, r1.c8 FROM "S 1"."T 1" r1 WHERE ((r1."C 1" < 20)) AND EXISTS (SELECT NULL FROM "S 1"."T 1" r3 WHERE ((r3."C 1" > 10)) AND ((date(r3.c5) = '1970-01-17'::date)) AND ((r3.c3 = r1.c3)))
4216(7 rows)
4217
4218EXECUTE st3(10, 20);
4219 c1 | c2 |  c3   |              c4              |            c5            | c6 |     c7     | c8  
4220----+----+-------+------------------------------+--------------------------+----+------------+-----
4221 16 |  6 | 00016 | Sat Jan 17 00:00:00 1970 PST | Sat Jan 17 00:00:00 1970 | 6  | 6          | foo
4222(1 row)
4223
4224EXECUTE st3(20, 30);
4225 c1 | c2 | c3 | c4 | c5 | c6 | c7 | c8 
4226----+----+----+----+----+----+----+----
4227(0 rows)
4228
4229-- custom plan should be chosen initially
4230PREPARE st4(int) AS SELECT * FROM ft1 t1 WHERE t1.c1 = $1;

4259   Output: c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8

4261(3 rows)

4263EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) EXECUTE st4(1);
4264                                         QUERY PLAN                                          
4265---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4266 Foreign Scan on public.ft1 t1
4267   Output: c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8
4268   Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE (("C 1" = 1))
4269(3 rows)
4270
4271-- once we try it enough times, should switch to generic plan
4272EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) EXECUTE st4(1);
4273                                              QUERY PLAN                                               
4274-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4275 Foreign Scan on public.ft1 t1
4276   Output: c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8
4277   Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE (("C 1" = $1::integer))
4278(3 rows)
4279
4280-- value of $1 should not be sent to remote
4281PREPARE st5(user_enum,int) AS SELECT * FROM ft1 t1 WHERE c8 = $1 and c1 = $2;
4282EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) EXECUTE st5('foo', 1);
4283                                         QUERY PLAN                                          
4284---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4285 Foreign Scan on public.ft1 t1
4286   Output: c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8
4287   Filter: (t1.c8 = 'foo'::user_enum)
4288   Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE (("C 1" = 1))
4289(4 rows)
4290
4291EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) EXECUTE st5('foo', 1);

4293---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

4341
4342-- altering FDW options requires replanning
4343PREPARE st6 AS SELECT * FROM ft1 t1 WHERE t1.c1 = t1.c2;
4344EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) EXECUTE st6;
4345                                          QUERY PLAN                                          
4346----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4347 Foreign Scan on public.ft1 t1
4348   Output: c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8
4349   Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE (("C 1" = c2))
4350(3 rows)
4351
4352PREPARE st7 AS INSERT INTO ft1 (c1,c2,c3) VALUES (1001,101,'foo');
4353EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) EXECUTE st7;
4354                                                                                             QUERY PLAN                                                                                              
4355-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4356 Insert on public.ft1
4357   Remote SQL: INSERT INTO "S 1"."T 1"("C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7, $8)
4358   Batch Size: 1
4359   ->  Result
4360         Output: NULL::integer, 1001, 101, 'foo'::text, NULL::timestamp with time zone, NULL::timestamp without time zone, NULL::character varying(10), 'ft1       '::character(10), NULL::user_enum
4361(5 rows)
4362

4364ALTER FOREIGN TABLE ft1 OPTIONS (SET table_name 'T 0');
4365EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) EXECUTE st6;
4366                                          QUERY PLAN                                          
4367----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4368 Foreign Scan on public.ft1 t1
4369   Output: c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8
4370   Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8 FROM "S 1"."T 0" WHERE (("C 1" = c2))
4371(3 rows)

4381  6 |  6 | 00006 | Wed Jan 07 00:00:00 1970 PST | Wed Jan 07 00:00:00 1970 | 6  | 6          | foo

4384  9 |  9 | 00009 | Sat Jan 10 00:00:00 1970 PST | Sat Jan 10 00:00:00 1970 | 9  | 9          | foo
4385(9 rows)
4386

4392   Batch Size: 1
4393   ->  Result
4394         Output: NULL::integer, 1001, 101, 'foo'::text, NULL::timestamp with time zone, NULL::timestamp without time zone, NULL::character varying(10), 'ft1       '::character(10), NULL::user_enum
4395(5 rows)
4396
4397ALTER TABLE "S 1"."T 0" RENAME TO "T 1";
4398ALTER FOREIGN TABLE ft1 OPTIONS (SET table_name 'T 1');
4399PREPARE st8 AS SELECT count(c3) FROM ft1 t1 WHERE t1.c1 === t1.c2;
4400EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) EXECUTE st8;
4401                                       QUERY PLAN                                        
4402-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4403 Foreign Scan
4404   Output: (count(c3))
4405   Relations: Aggregate on (public.ft1 t1)
4406   Remote SQL: SELECT count(c3) FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE (("C 1" OPERATOR(public.===) c2))
4407(4 rows)
4408
4409ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (DROP extensions);
4410EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) EXECUTE st8;
4411                        QUERY PLAN                         
4412-----------------------------------------------------------
4413 Aggregate
4414   Output: count(c3)
4415   ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft1 t1
4416         Output: c3
4417         Filter: (t1.c1 === t1.c2)
4418         Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3 FROM "S 1"."T 1"
4419(6 rows)
4420
4421EXECUTE st8;
4422 count 
4423-------
4424     9
4425(1 row)
4426
4427ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (ADD extensions 'postgres_fdw');
4428-- cleanup
4429DEALLOCATE st1;
4430DEALLOCATE st2;
4431DEALLOCATE st3;
4432DEALLOCATE st4;
4433DEALLOCATE st5;
4434DEALLOCATE st6;
4435DEALLOCATE st7;
4436DEALLOCATE st8;
4437-- System columns, except ctid and oid, should not be sent to remote
4438EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
4439SELECT * FROM ft1 t1 WHERE t1.tableoid = 'pg_class'::regclass LIMIT 1;
4440                                  QUERY PLAN                                   
4441-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4442 Limit
4443   Output: c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8
4444   ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft1 t1

4446         Filter: (t1.tableoid = '1259'::oid)
4447         Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8 FROM "S 1"."T 1"
4448(6 rows)
4449

4454(1 row)
4455

4500
4501-- ===================================================================
4502-- used in PL/pgSQL function
4503-- ===================================================================
4504CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION f_test(p_c1 int) RETURNS int AS $$
4505DECLARE
4506	v_c1 int;
4507BEGIN
4508    SELECT c1 INTO v_c1 FROM ft1 WHERE c1 = p_c1 LIMIT 1;
4509    PERFORM c1 FROM ft1 WHERE c1 = p_c1 AND p_c1 = v_c1 LIMIT 1;
4510    RETURN v_c1;
4511END;
4512$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
4513SELECT f_test(100);
4514 f_test 
4515--------
4516    100
4517(1 row)
4518
4519DROP FUNCTION f_test(int);
4520-- ===================================================================
4521-- REINDEX
4522-- ===================================================================
4523-- remote table is not created here
4524CREATE FOREIGN TABLE reindex_foreign (c1 int, c2 int)
4525  SERVER loopback2 OPTIONS (table_name 'reindex_local');
4526REINDEX TABLE reindex_foreign; -- error
4527ERROR:  "reindex_foreign" is not a table or materialized view
4528REINDEX TABLE CONCURRENTLY reindex_foreign; -- error
4529ERROR:  "reindex_foreign" is not a table or materialized view
4530DROP FOREIGN TABLE reindex_foreign;
4531-- partitions and foreign tables
4532CREATE TABLE reind_fdw_parent (c1 int) PARTITION BY RANGE (c1);
4533CREATE TABLE reind_fdw_0_10 PARTITION OF reind_fdw_parent
4534  FOR VALUES FROM (0) TO (10);
4535CREATE FOREIGN TABLE reind_fdw_10_20 PARTITION OF reind_fdw_parent
4536  FOR VALUES FROM (10) TO (20)
4537  SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'reind_local_10_20');
4538REINDEX TABLE reind_fdw_parent; -- ok
4539REINDEX TABLE CONCURRENTLY reind_fdw_parent; -- ok
4540DROP TABLE reind_fdw_parent;
4541-- ===================================================================
4542-- conversion error
4543-- ===================================================================
4544ALTER FOREIGN TABLE ft1 ALTER COLUMN c8 TYPE int;
4545SELECT * FROM ft1 ftx(x1,x2,x3,x4,x5,x6,x7,x8) WHERE x1 = 1;  -- ERROR
4546ERROR:  invalid input syntax for type integer: "foo"
4547CONTEXT:  column "x8" of foreign table "ftx"
4548SELECT ftx.x1, ft2.c2, ftx.x8 FROM ft1 ftx(x1,x2,x3,x4,x5,x6,x7,x8), ft2
4549  WHERE ftx.x1 = ft2.c1 AND ftx.x1 = 1; -- ERROR
4550ERROR:  invalid input syntax for type integer: "foo"
4551CONTEXT:  column "x8" of foreign table "ftx"
4552SELECT ftx.x1, ft2.c2, ftx FROM ft1 ftx(x1,x2,x3,x4,x5,x6,x7,x8), ft2
4553  WHERE ftx.x1 = ft2.c1 AND ftx.x1 = 1; -- ERROR
4554ERROR:  invalid input syntax for type integer: "foo"
4555CONTEXT:  whole-row reference to foreign table "ftx"
4556SELECT sum(c2), array_agg(c8) FROM ft1 GROUP BY c8; -- ERROR
4557ERROR:  invalid input syntax for type integer: "foo"
4558CONTEXT:  processing expression at position 2 in select list
4559ANALYZE ft1; -- ERROR
4560ERROR:  invalid input syntax for type integer: "foo"
4561CONTEXT:  column "c8" of foreign table "ft1"
4562ALTER FOREIGN TABLE ft1 ALTER COLUMN c8 TYPE user_enum;
4563-- ===================================================================
4564-- local type can be different from remote type in some cases,
4565-- in particular if similarly-named operators do equivalent things
4566-- ===================================================================
4567ALTER FOREIGN TABLE ft1 ALTER COLUMN c8 TYPE text;
4568EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
4569SELECT * FROM ft1 WHERE c8 = 'foo' LIMIT 1;
4570                                                  QUERY PLAN                                                  
4571--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4572 Foreign Scan on public.ft1
4573   Output: c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8
4574   Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE ((c8 = 'foo')) LIMIT 1::bigint
4575(3 rows)
4576
4577SELECT * FROM ft1 WHERE c8 = 'foo' LIMIT 1;
4578 c1 | c2 |  c3   |              c4              |            c5            | c6 |     c7     | c8  
4579----+----+-------+------------------------------+--------------------------+----+------------+-----
4580  1 |  1 | 00001 | Fri Jan 02 00:00:00 1970 PST | Fri Jan 02 00:00:00 1970 | 1  | 1          | foo
4581(1 row)
4582
4583EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
4584SELECT * FROM ft1 WHERE 'foo' = c8 LIMIT 1;
4585                                                  QUERY PLAN                                                  
4586--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4587 Foreign Scan on public.ft1
4588   Output: c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8
4589   Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE (('foo' = c8)) LIMIT 1::bigint
4590(3 rows)
4591
4592SELECT * FROM ft1 WHERE 'foo' = c8 LIMIT 1;
4593 c1 | c2 |  c3   |              c4              |            c5            | c6 |     c7     | c8  
4594----+----+-------+------------------------------+--------------------------+----+------------+-----
4595  1 |  1 | 00001 | Fri Jan 02 00:00:00 1970 PST | Fri Jan 02 00:00:00 1970 | 1  | 1          | foo
4596(1 row)
4597
4598-- we declared c8 to be text locally, but it's still the same type on
4599-- the remote which will balk if we try to do anything incompatible
4600-- with that remote type
4601SELECT * FROM ft1 WHERE c8 LIKE 'foo' LIMIT 1; -- ERROR
4602ERROR:  operator does not exist: public.user_enum ~~ unknown
4603HINT:  No operator matches the given name and argument types. You might need to add explicit type casts.
4604CONTEXT:  remote SQL command: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE ((c8 ~~ 'foo')) LIMIT 1::bigint
4605SELECT * FROM ft1 WHERE c8::text LIKE 'foo' LIMIT 1; -- ERROR; cast not pushed down
4606ERROR:  operator does not exist: public.user_enum ~~ unknown
4607HINT:  No operator matches the given name and argument types. You might need to add explicit type casts.
4608CONTEXT:  remote SQL command: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE ((c8 ~~ 'foo')) LIMIT 1::bigint
4609ALTER FOREIGN TABLE ft1 ALTER COLUMN c8 TYPE user_enum;
4610-- ===================================================================
4611-- subtransaction
4612--  + local/remote error doesn't break cursor
4613-- ===================================================================
4614BEGIN;
4615DECLARE c CURSOR FOR SELECT * FROM ft1 ORDER BY c1;
4616FETCH c;
4617 c1 | c2 |  c3   |              c4              |            c5            | c6 |     c7     | c8  
4618----+----+-------+------------------------------+--------------------------+----+------------+-----
4619  1 |  1 | 00001 | Fri Jan 02 00:00:00 1970 PST | Fri Jan 02 00:00:00 1970 | 1  | 1          | foo
4620(1 row)
4621
4622SAVEPOINT s;
4623ERROR OUT;          -- ERROR
4624ERROR:  syntax error at or near "ERROR"
4625LINE 1: ERROR OUT;
4626        ^
4627ROLLBACK TO s;
4628FETCH c;
4629 c1 | c2 |  c3   |              c4              |            c5            | c6 |     c7     | c8  
4630----+----+-------+------------------------------+--------------------------+----+------------+-----
4631  2 |  2 | 00002 | Sat Jan 03 00:00:00 1970 PST | Sat Jan 03 00:00:00 1970 | 2  | 2          | foo
4632(1 row)
4633
4634SAVEPOINT s;
4635SELECT * FROM ft1 WHERE 1 / (c1 - 1) > 0;  -- ERROR
4636ERROR:  division by zero
4637CONTEXT:  remote SQL command: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE (((1 / ("C 1" - 1)) > 0))
4638ROLLBACK TO s;
4639FETCH c;
4640 c1 | c2 |  c3   |              c4              |            c5            | c6 |     c7     | c8  
4641----+----+-------+------------------------------+--------------------------+----+------------+-----
4642  3 |  3 | 00003 | Sun Jan 04 00:00:00 1970 PST | Sun Jan 04 00:00:00 1970 | 3  | 3          | foo
4643(1 row)
4644
4645SELECT * FROM ft1 ORDER BY c1 LIMIT 1;
4646 c1 | c2 |  c3   |              c4              |            c5            | c6 |     c7     | c8  
4647----+----+-------+------------------------------+--------------------------+----+------------+-----
4648  1 |  1 | 00001 | Fri Jan 02 00:00:00 1970 PST | Fri Jan 02 00:00:00 1970 | 1  | 1          | foo
4649(1 row)
4650
4651COMMIT;
4652-- ===================================================================
4653-- test handling of collations
4654-- ===================================================================
4655create table loct3 (f1 text collate "C" unique, f2 text, f3 varchar(10) unique);
4656create foreign table ft3 (f1 text collate "C", f2 text, f3 varchar(10))
4657  server loopback options (table_name 'loct3', use_remote_estimate 'true');
4658-- can be sent to remote
4659explain (verbose, costs off) select * from ft3 where f1 = 'foo';
4660                               QUERY PLAN                               
4661------------------------------------------------------------------------
4662 Foreign Scan on public.ft3
4663   Output: f1, f2, f3
4664   Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3 FROM public.loct3 WHERE ((f1 = 'foo'))
4665(3 rows)
4666
4667explain (verbose, costs off) select * from ft3 where f1 COLLATE "C" = 'foo';
4668                               QUERY PLAN                               
4669------------------------------------------------------------------------
4670 Foreign Scan on public.ft3
4671   Output: f1, f2, f3
4672   Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3 FROM public.loct3 WHERE ((f1 = 'foo'))
4673(3 rows)
4674
4675explain (verbose, costs off) select * from ft3 where f2 = 'foo';
4676                               QUERY PLAN                               
4677------------------------------------------------------------------------
4678 Foreign Scan on public.ft3
4679   Output: f1, f2, f3
4680   Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3 FROM public.loct3 WHERE ((f2 = 'foo'))
4681(3 rows)
4682
4683explain (verbose, costs off) select * from ft3 where f3 = 'foo';
4684                               QUERY PLAN                               
4685------------------------------------------------------------------------
4686 Foreign Scan on public.ft3
4687   Output: f1, f2, f3
4688   Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3 FROM public.loct3 WHERE ((f3 = 'foo'))
4689(3 rows)
4690
4691explain (verbose, costs off) select * from ft3 f, loct3 l
4692  where f.f3 = l.f3 and l.f1 = 'foo';
4693                                            QUERY PLAN                                            
4694--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4695 Nested Loop
4696   Output: f.f1, f.f2, f.f3, l.f1, l.f2, l.f3
4697   ->  Index Scan using loct3_f1_key on public.loct3 l
4698         Output: l.f1, l.f2, l.f3
4699         Index Cond: (l.f1 = 'foo'::text)
4700   ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft3 f
4701         Output: f.f1, f.f2, f.f3
4702         Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3 FROM public.loct3 WHERE ((f3 = $1::character varying(10)))
4703(8 rows)
4704
4705-- can't be sent to remote
4706explain (verbose, costs off) select * from ft3 where f1 COLLATE "POSIX" = 'foo';
4707                    QUERY PLAN                     
4708---------------------------------------------------
4709 Foreign Scan on public.ft3

4869  (SELECT * FROM ft4 WHERE
4870  EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ft5 WHERE ft4.c1 = ft5.c1)) ft4

4873  EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ft4 WHERE ft4.c1 = ft5.c1)) ft5
4874  ON ft2.c2 <= ft5.c1

5003	ON ft2.c2 = ft4.c1
5004	INNER JOIN
5005	(SELECT * FROM ft2 WHERE EXISTS (
5006		SELECT 1 FROM ft4 WHERE ft2.c2 = ft4.c2)) ft21
5007	ON ft2.c2 = ft21.c2
5008	WHERE ft2.c1 > 900
5009	ORDER BY ft2.c1 LIMIT 10;
5010 c1 | c2 | c3 | c4 | c5 | c6 | c7 | c8 | c1 | c2 | c3 
5011----+----+----+----+----+----+----+----+----+----+----
5012(0 rows)
5013
5014-- Semi-join conditions shouldn't pop up as left/right join clauses.
5015SET enable_material TO off;
5016EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
5017SELECT x1.c1 FROM
5018		(SELECT * FROM ft2 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ft4 WHERE ft4.c1 = ft2.c1 AND ft2.c2 < 10)) x1
5019	RIGHT JOIN
5020		(SELECT * FROM ft2 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ft4 WHERE ft4.c1 = ft2.c1 AND ft2.c2 < 10)) x2
5021	ON (x1.c1 = x2.c1)
5022ORDER BY x1.c1 LIMIT 10;
5023                                                                                                                                                                                 QUERY PLAN                                                                                                                                                                                 
5024----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5025 Foreign Scan
5026   Output: ft2.c1
5027   Relations: ((public.ft2 ft2_1) LEFT JOIN ((public.ft2) SEMI JOIN (public.ft4))) SEMI JOIN (public.ft4 ft4_1)
5028   Remote SQL: SELECT s9.c1 FROM ("S 1"."T 1" r6 LEFT JOIN (SELECT r4."C 1" FROM "S 1"."T 1" r4 WHERE ((r4.c2 < 10)) AND EXISTS (SELECT NULL FROM "S 1"."T 3" r5 WHERE ((r4."C 1" = r5.c1)))) s9(c1) ON (((s9.c1 = r6."C 1")))) WHERE ((r6.c2 < 10)) AND EXISTS (SELECT NULL FROM "S 1"."T 3" r7 WHERE ((r6."C 1" = r7.c1))) ORDER BY s9.c1 ASC NULLS LAST LIMIT 10::bigint
5029(4 rows)
5030
5031SELECT x1.c1 FROM
5032		(SELECT * FROM ft2 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ft4 WHERE ft4.c1 = ft2.c1 AND ft2.c2 < 10)) x1
5033	RIGHT JOIN
5034		(SELECT * FROM ft2 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ft4 WHERE ft4.c1 = ft2.c1 AND ft2.c2 < 10)) x2
5035	ON (x1.c1 = x2.c1)
5036ORDER BY x1.c1 LIMIT 10;
5037 c1 
5038----
5039  2
5040  4
5041  6
5042  8
5043 10
5044 12
5045 14
5046 16
5047 18
5048 20
5049(10 rows)
5050
5051RESET enable_material;
5052-- Can't push down semi-join with inner rel vars in targetlist
5053EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
5054SELECT ft1.c1 FROM ft1 JOIN ft2 on ft1.c1 = ft2.c1 WHERE
5055	ft1.c1 IN (
5056		SELECT ft2.c1 FROM ft2 JOIN ft4 ON ft2.c1 = ft4.c1)
5057	ORDER BY ft1.c1 LIMIT 5;
5058                                                                            QUERY PLAN                                                                             
5059-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5060 Limit
5061   Output: ft1.c1
5062   ->  Merge Semi Join
5063         Output: ft1.c1
5064         Merge Cond: (ft1.c1 = ft2_1.c1)
5065         ->  Foreign Scan
5066               Output: ft1.c1, ft2.c1
5067               Relations: (public.ft1) INNER JOIN (public.ft2)
5068               Remote SQL: SELECT r1."C 1", r2."C 1" FROM ("S 1"."T 1" r1 INNER JOIN "S 1"."T 1" r2 ON (((r2."C 1" = r1."C 1")))) ORDER BY r1."C 1" ASC NULLS LAST
5069         ->  Foreign Scan
5070               Output: ft2_1.c1, ft4.c1
5071               Relations: (public.ft2 ft2_1) INNER JOIN (public.ft4)
5072               Remote SQL: SELECT r5."C 1", r6.c1 FROM ("S 1"."T 1" r5 INNER JOIN "S 1"."T 3" r6 ON (((r5."C 1" = r6.c1)))) ORDER BY r5."C 1" ASC NULLS LAST
5073(13 rows)
5074
5075-- ===================================================================
5076-- test writable foreign table stuff
5077-- ===================================================================
5078EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
5079INSERT INTO ft2 (c1,c2,c3) SELECT c1+1000,c2+100, c3 || c3 FROM ft2 LIMIT 20;
5080                                                                                                                      QUERY PLAN                                                                                                                      
5081------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5082 Insert on public.ft2
5083   Remote SQL: INSERT INTO "S 1"."T 1"("C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7, $8)
5084   Batch Size: 1
5085   ->  Subquery Scan on "*SELECT*"
5086         Output: "*SELECT*"."?column?", "*SELECT*"."?column?_1", NULL::integer, "*SELECT*"."?column?_2", NULL::timestamp with time zone, NULL::timestamp without time zone, NULL::character varying(10), 'ft2       '::character(10), NULL::user_enum
5087         ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft2 ft2_1
5088               Output: (ft2_1.c1 + 1000), (ft2_1.c2 + 100), (ft2_1.c3 || ft2_1.c3)
5089               Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3 FROM "S 1"."T 1" LIMIT 20::bigint
5090(8 rows)
5091
5092INSERT INTO ft2 (c1,c2,c3) SELECT c1+1000,c2+100, c3 || c3 FROM ft2 LIMIT 20;

5260 Update on public.ft2
5261   ->  Foreign Update
5262         Remote SQL: UPDATE "S 1"."T 1" r1 SET c2 = (r1.c2 + 500), c3 = (r1.c3 || '_update9'), c7 = 'ft2       '::character(10) FROM "S 1"."T 1" r2 WHERE ((r1.c2 = r2."C 1")) AND (((r2."C 1" % 10) = 9))
5263(3 rows)
5264
5265UPDATE ft2 SET c2 = ft2.c2 + 500, c3 = ft2.c3 || '_update9', c7 = DEFAULT
5266  FROM ft1 WHERE ft1.c1 = ft2.c2 AND ft1.c1 % 10 = 9;

5385BEGIN;
5386  EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
5387  DELETE FROM ft2 WHERE c1 % 10 = 6 AND c1 < 40 RETURNING old.c1, c4;               -- can't be pushed down
5388                                                QUERY PLAN                                                 
5389-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

5391   Output: old.c1, c4

6242EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
6243INSERT INTO ft2 (c1,c2,c3) VALUES (1200,999,'foo') RETURNING tableoid::regclass;
6244                                                                                             QUERY PLAN                                                                                              
6245-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6246 Insert on public.ft2
6247   Output: (ft2.tableoid)::regclass
6248   Remote SQL: INSERT INTO "S 1"."T 1"("C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7, $8)
6249   Batch Size: 1
6250   ->  Result
6251         Output: 1200, 999, NULL::integer, 'foo'::text, NULL::timestamp with time zone, NULL::timestamp without time zone, NULL::character varying(10), 'ft2       '::character(10), NULL::user_enum
6252(6 rows)

6261UPDATE ft2 SET c3 = 'bar' WHERE c1 = 1200 RETURNING tableoid::regclass;             -- can be pushed down

6287 tableoid

6289 ft2
6290(1 row)
6291
6292-- Test UPDATE/DELETE with RETURNING on a three-table join
6293INSERT INTO ft2 (c1,c2,c3)
6294  SELECT id, id - 1200, to_char(id, 'FM00000') FROM generate_series(1201, 1300) id;
6295EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
6296UPDATE ft2 SET c3 = 'foo'
6297  FROM ft4 INNER JOIN ft5 ON (ft4.c1 = ft5.c1)
6298  WHERE ft2.c1 > 1200 AND ft2.c2 = ft4.c1
6299  RETURNING ft2, ft2.*, ft4, ft4.*;       -- can be pushed down
6300                                                                                                                                                                          QUERY PLAN                                                                                                                                                                           
6301---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6302 Update on public.ft2
6303   Output: ft2.*, ft2.c1, ft2.c2, ft2.c3, ft2.c4, ft2.c5, ft2.c6, ft2.c7, ft2.c8, ft4.*, ft4.c1, ft4.c2, ft4.c3
6304   ->  Foreign Update
6305         Remote SQL: UPDATE "S 1"."T 1" r1 SET c3 = 'foo'::text FROM ("S 1"."T 3" r2 INNER JOIN "S 1"."T 4" r3 ON (TRUE)) WHERE ((r2.c1 = r3.c1)) AND ((r1.c2 = r2.c1)) AND ((r1."C 1" > 1200)) RETURNING r1."C 1", r1.c2, r1.c3, r1.c4, r1.c5, r1.c6, r1.c7, r1.c8, CASE WHEN (r2.*)::text IS NOT NULL THEN ROW(r2.c1, r2.c2, r2.c3) END, r2.c1, r2.c2, r2.c3
6306(4 rows)
6307
6308UPDATE ft2 SET c3 = 'foo'

6332BEGIN;
6333  EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
6334  UPDATE ft2 SET c3 = 'bar'
6335    FROM ft4 INNER JOIN ft5 ON (ft4.c1 = ft5.c1)
6336    WHERE ft2.c1 > 1200 AND ft2.c2 = ft4.c1
6337    RETURNING old, new, ft2, ft2.*, ft4, ft4.*;  -- can't be pushed down
6338                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     QUERY PLAN                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
6339------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6340 Update on public.ft2
6341   Output: old.*, new.*, ft2.*, ft2.c1, ft2.c2, ft2.c3, ft2.c4, ft2.c5, ft2.c6, ft2.c7, ft2.c8, ft4.*, ft4.c1, ft4.c2, ft4.c3
6342   Remote SQL: UPDATE "S 1"."T 1" SET c3 = $2 WHERE ctid = $1 RETURNING "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8
6343   ->  Foreign Scan
6344         Output: 'bar'::text, ft2.ctid, ft2.*, ft4.*, ft5.*, ft4.c1, ft4.c2, ft4.c3
6345         Relations: ((public.ft2) INNER JOIN (public.ft4)) INNER JOIN (public.ft5)
6346         Remote SQL: SELECT r1.ctid, CASE WHEN (r1.*)::text IS NOT NULL THEN ROW(r1."C 1", r1.c2, r1.c3, r1.c4, r1.c5, r1.c6, r1.c7, r1.c8) END, CASE WHEN (r2.*)::text IS NOT NULL THEN ROW(r2.c1, r2.c2, r2.c3) END, CASE WHEN (r3.*)::text IS NOT NULL THEN ROW(r3.c1, r3.c2, r3.c3) END, r2.c1, r2.c2, r2.c3 FROM (("S 1"."T 1" r1 INNER JOIN "S 1"."T 3" r2 ON (((r1.c2 = r2.c1)) AND ((r1."C 1" > 1200)))) INNER JOIN "S 1"."T 4" r3 ON (((r2.c1 = r3.c1)))) FOR UPDATE OF r1
6347         ->  Nested Loop
6348               Output: ft2.ctid, ft2.*, ft4.*, ft5.*, ft4.c1, ft4.c2, ft4.c3
6349               Join Filter: (ft4.c1 = ft5.c1)
6350               ->  Sort
6351                     Output: ft2.ctid, ft2.*, ft2.c2, ft4.*, ft4.c1, ft4.c2, ft4.c3
6352                     Sort Key: ft2.c2
6353                     ->  Hash Join
6354                           Output: ft2.ctid, ft2.*, ft2.c2, ft4.*, ft4.c1, ft4.c2, ft4.c3
6355                           Hash Cond: (ft2.c2 = ft4.c1)
6356                           ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft2
6357                                 Output: ft2.ctid, ft2.*, ft2.c2
6358                                 Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8, ctid FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE (("C 1" > 1200)) FOR UPDATE
6359                           ->  Hash
6360                                 Output: ft4.*, ft4.c1, ft4.c2, ft4.c3
6361                                 ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft4
6362                                       Output: ft4.*, ft4.c1, ft4.c2, ft4.c3
6363                                       Remote SQL: SELECT c1, c2, c3 FROM "S 1"."T 3"
6364               ->  Materialize
6365                     Output: ft5.*, ft5.c1
6366                     ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft5
6367                           Output: ft5.*, ft5.c1
6368                           Remote SQL: SELECT c1, c2, c3 FROM "S 1"."T 4"
6369(29 rows)

6395ROLLBACK;
6396EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
6397DELETE FROM ft2
6398  USING ft4 LEFT JOIN ft5 ON (ft4.c1 = ft5.c1)
6399  WHERE ft2.c1 > 1200 AND ft2.c1 % 10 = 0 AND ft2.c2 = ft4.c1
6400  RETURNING 100;                          -- can be pushed down
6401                                                                                            QUERY PLAN                                                                                             
6402---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6403 Delete on public.ft2
6404   Output: 100
6405   ->  Foreign Delete
6406         Remote SQL: DELETE FROM "S 1"."T 1" r1 USING ("S 1"."T 3" r2 LEFT JOIN "S 1"."T 4" r3 ON (((r2.c1 = r3.c1)))) WHERE ((r1.c2 = r2.c1)) AND ((r1."C 1" > 1200)) AND (((r1."C 1" % 10) = 0))
6407(4 rows)

6427DELETE FROM ft2 WHERE ft2.c1 > 1200;
6428-- Test UPDATE with a MULTIEXPR sub-select
6429-- (maybe someday this'll be remotely executable, but not today)
6430EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
6431UPDATE ft2 AS target SET (c2, c7) = (
6432    SELECT c2 * 10, c7
6433        FROM ft2 AS src
6434        WHERE target.c1 = src.c1
6435) WHERE c1 > 1100;
6436                                                      QUERY PLAN                                                       
6437-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6438 Update on public.ft2 target
6439   Remote SQL: UPDATE "S 1"."T 1" SET c2 = $2, c7 = $3 WHERE ctid = $1
6440   ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft2 target
6441         Output: (SubPlan 1).col1, (SubPlan 1).col2, (rescan SubPlan 1), target.ctid, target.*
6442         Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8, ctid FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE (("C 1" > 1100)) FOR UPDATE
6443         SubPlan 1
6444           ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft2 src
6445                 Output: (src.c2 * 10), src.c7
6446                 Remote SQL: SELECT c2, c7 FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE (($1::integer = "C 1"))
6447(9 rows)
6448
6449UPDATE ft2 AS target SET (c2, c7) = (
6450    SELECT c2 * 10, c7
6451        FROM ft2 AS src
6452        WHERE target.c1 = src.c1
6453) WHERE c1 > 1100;
6454UPDATE ft2 AS target SET (c2) = (
6455    SELECT c2 / 10
6456        FROM ft2 AS src
6457        WHERE target.c1 = src.c1
6458) WHERE c1 > 1100;
6459-- Test UPDATE involving a join that can be pushed down,
6460-- but a SET clause that can't be
6461EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
6462UPDATE ft2 d SET c2 = CASE WHEN random() >= 0 THEN d.c2 ELSE 0 END
6463  FROM ft2 AS t WHERE d.c1 = t.c1 AND d.c1 > 1000;
6464                                                                                                                                                                                       QUERY PLAN                                                                                                                                                                                        
6465-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6466 Update on public.ft2 d
6467   Remote SQL: UPDATE "S 1"."T 1" SET c2 = $2 WHERE ctid = $1
6468   ->  Foreign Scan
6469         Output: CASE WHEN (random() >= '0'::double precision) THEN d.c2 ELSE 0 END, d.ctid, d.*, t.*
6470         Relations: (public.ft2 d) INNER JOIN (public.ft2 t)
6471         Remote SQL: SELECT r1.c2, r1.ctid, CASE WHEN (r1.*)::text IS NOT NULL THEN ROW(r1."C 1", r1.c2, r1.c3, r1.c4, r1.c5, r1.c6, r1.c7, r1.c8) END, CASE WHEN (r2.*)::text IS NOT NULL THEN ROW(r2."C 1", r2.c2, r2.c3, r2.c4, r2.c5, r2.c6, r2.c7, r2.c8) END FROM ("S 1"."T 1" r1 INNER JOIN "S 1"."T 1" r2 ON (((r1."C 1" = r2."C 1")) AND ((r1."C 1" > 1000)))) FOR UPDATE OF r1
6472         ->  Hash Join
6473               Output: d.c2, d.ctid, d.*, t.*
6474               Hash Cond: (d.c1 = t.c1)
6475               ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft2 d
6476                     Output: d.c2, d.ctid, d.*, d.c1
6477                     Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8, ctid FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE (("C 1" > 1000)) ORDER BY "C 1" ASC NULLS LAST FOR UPDATE
6478               ->  Hash
6479                     Output: t.*, t.c1
6480                     ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft2 t
6481                           Output: t.*, t.c1
6482                           Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8 FROM "S 1"."T 1"
6483(17 rows)
6484
6485UPDATE ft2 d SET c2 = CASE WHEN random() >= 0 THEN d.c2 ELSE 0 END
6486  FROM ft2 AS t WHERE d.c1 = t.c1 AND d.c1 > 1000;
6487-- Test UPDATE/DELETE with WHERE or JOIN/ON conditions containing
6488-- user-defined operators/functions
6489ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (DROP extensions);
6490INSERT INTO ft2 (c1,c2,c3)
6491  SELECT id, id % 10, to_char(id, 'FM00000') FROM generate_series(2001, 2010) id;
6492-- this will do a remote seqscan, causing unstable result order, so sort
6493EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
6494WITH cte AS (
6495  UPDATE ft2 SET c3 = 'bar' WHERE postgres_fdw_abs(c1) > 2000 RETURNING *
6496) SELECT * FROM cte ORDER BY c1;          -- can't be pushed down
6497                                                    QUERY PLAN                                                    
6498------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6499 Sort
6500   Output: cte.c1, cte.c2, cte.c3, cte.c4, cte.c5, cte.c6, cte.c7, cte.c8
6501   Sort Key: cte.c1
6502   CTE cte
6503     ->  Update on public.ft2
6504           Output: ft2.c1, ft2.c2, ft2.c3, ft2.c4, ft2.c5, ft2.c6, ft2.c7, ft2.c8
6505           Remote SQL: UPDATE "S 1"."T 1" SET c3 = $2 WHERE ctid = $1 RETURNING "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8
6506           ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft2
6507                 Output: 'bar'::text, ft2.ctid, ft2.*
6508                 Filter: (postgres_fdw_abs(ft2.c1) > 2000)
6509                 Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8, ctid FROM "S 1"."T 1" FOR UPDATE
6510   ->  CTE Scan on cte
6511         Output: cte.c1, cte.c2, cte.c3, cte.c4, cte.c5, cte.c6, cte.c7, cte.c8
6512(13 rows)
6513
6514WITH cte AS (
6515  UPDATE ft2 SET c3 = 'bar' WHERE postgres_fdw_abs(c1) > 2000 RETURNING *
6516) SELECT * FROM cte ORDER BY c1;

6518------+----+-----+----+----+----+------------+----

6528 2010 |  0 | bar |    |    |    | ft2        |

6533  FROM ft4 INNER JOIN ft5 ON (ft4.c1 = ft5.c1)
6534  WHERE ft2.c1 > 2000 AND ft2.c2 === ft4.c1
6535  RETURNING ft2.*, ft4.*, ft5.*;                                                    -- can't be pushed down
6536                                                                                                                                          QUERY PLAN                                                                                                                                          
6537----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6538 Update on public.ft2
6539   Output: ft2.c1, ft2.c2, ft2.c3, ft2.c4, ft2.c5, ft2.c6, ft2.c7, ft2.c8, ft4.c1, ft4.c2, ft4.c3, ft5.c1, ft5.c2, ft5.c3

6541   ->  Nested Loop
6542         Output: 'baz'::text, ft2.ctid, ft2.*, ft4.*, ft5.*, ft4.c1, ft4.c2, ft4.c3, ft5.c1, ft5.c2, ft5.c3
6543         Join Filter: (ft2.c2 === ft4.c1)
6544         ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft2
6545               Output: ft2.ctid, ft2.*, ft2.c2
6546               Remote SQL: SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8, ctid FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE (("C 1" > 2000)) FOR UPDATE
6547         ->  Foreign Scan
6548               Output: ft4.*, ft4.c1, ft4.c2, ft4.c3, ft5.*, ft5.c1, ft5.c2, ft5.c3
6549               Relations: (public.ft4) INNER JOIN (public.ft5)
6550               Remote SQL: SELECT CASE WHEN (r2.*)::text IS NOT NULL THEN ROW(r2.c1, r2.c2, r2.c3) END, r2.c1, r2.c2, r2.c3, CASE WHEN (r3.*)::text IS NOT NULL THEN ROW(r3.c1, r3.c2, r3.c3) END, r3.c1, r3.c2, r3.c3 FROM ("S 1"."T 3" r2 INNER JOIN "S 1"."T 4" r3 ON (((r2.c1 = r3.c1))))
6551               ->  Hash Join
6552                     Output: ft4.*, ft4.c1, ft4.c2, ft4.c3, ft5.*, ft5.c1, ft5.c2, ft5.c3
6553                     Hash Cond: (ft4.c1 = ft5.c1)
6554                     ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft4
6555                           Output: ft4.*, ft4.c1, ft4.c2, ft4.c3
6556                           Remote SQL: SELECT c1, c2, c3 FROM "S 1"."T 3"
6557                     ->  Hash
6558                           Output: ft5.*, ft5.c1, ft5.c2, ft5.c3
6559                           ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft5
6560                                 Output: ft5.*, ft5.c1, ft5.c2, ft5.c3
6561                                 Remote SQL: SELECT c1, c2, c3 FROM "S 1"."T 4"
6562(24 rows)
6563
6564UPDATE ft2 SET c3 = 'baz'
6565  FROM ft4 INNER JOIN ft5 ON (ft4.c1 = ft5.c1)
6566  WHERE ft2.c1 > 2000 AND ft2.c2 === ft4.c1
6567  RETURNING ft2.*, ft4.*, ft5.*;
6568  c1  | c2 | c3  | c4 | c5 | c6 |     c7     | c8 | c1 | c2 |   c3   | c1 | c2 |   c3   
6569------+----+-----+----+----+----+------------+----+----+----+--------+----+----+--------
6570 2006 |  6 | baz |    |    |    | ft2        |    |  6 |  7 | AAA006 |  6 |  7 | AAA006
6571(1 row)
6572
6573EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
6574DELETE FROM ft2
6575  USING ft4 INNER JOIN ft5 ON (ft4.c1 === ft5.c1)
6576  WHERE ft2.c1 > 2000 AND ft2.c2 = ft4.c1
6577  RETURNING ft2.c1, ft2.c2, ft2.c3;       -- can't be pushed down
6578                                                                                                                                                                     QUERY PLAN                                                                                                                                                                     
6579----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6580 Delete on public.ft2
6581   Output: ft2.c1, ft2.c2, ft2.c3
6582   Remote SQL: DELETE FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE ctid = $1 RETURNING "C 1", c2, c3
6583   ->  Foreign Scan
6584         Output: ft2.ctid, ft4.*, ft5.*
6585         Filter: (ft4.c1 === ft5.c1)
6586         Relations: ((public.ft2) INNER JOIN (public.ft4)) INNER JOIN (public.ft5)
6587         Remote SQL: SELECT r1.ctid, CASE WHEN (r2.*)::text IS NOT NULL THEN ROW(r2.c1, r2.c2, r2.c3) END, CASE WHEN (r3.*)::text IS NOT NULL THEN ROW(r3.c1, r3.c2, r3.c3) END, r2.c1, r3.c1 FROM (("S 1"."T 1" r1 INNER JOIN "S 1"."T 3" r2 ON (((r1.c2 = r2.c1)) AND ((r1."C 1" > 2000)))) INNER JOIN "S 1"."T 4" r3 ON (TRUE)) FOR UPDATE OF r1
6588         ->  Nested Loop
6589               Output: ft2.ctid, ft4.*, ft5.*, ft4.c1, ft5.c1
6590               ->  Nested Loop
6591                     Output: ft2.ctid, ft4.*, ft4.c1
6592                     Join Filter: (ft2.c2 = ft4.c1)
6593                     ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft2
6594                           Output: ft2.ctid, ft2.c2
6595                           Remote SQL: SELECT c2, ctid FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE (("C 1" > 2000)) FOR UPDATE
6596                     ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft4
6597                           Output: ft4.*, ft4.c1
6598                           Remote SQL: SELECT c1, c2, c3 FROM "S 1"."T 3"
6599               ->  Foreign Scan on public.ft5
6600                     Output: ft5.*, ft5.c1
6601                     Remote SQL: SELECT c1, c2, c3 FROM "S 1"."T 4"
6602(22 rows)
6603
6604DELETE FROM ft2
6605  USING ft4 INNER JOIN ft5 ON (ft4.c1 === ft5.c1)
6606  WHERE ft2.c1 > 2000 AND ft2.c2 = ft4.c1
6607  RETURNING ft2.c1, ft2.c2, ft2.c3;
6608  c1  | c2 | c3  
6609------+----+-----
6610 2006 |  6 | baz
6611(1 row)
6612
6613DELETE FROM ft2 WHERE ft2.c1 > 2000;
6614ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (ADD extensions 'postgres_fdw');
6615-- Test that trigger on remote table works as expected
6616CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION "S 1".F_BRTRIG() RETURNS trigger AS $$
6617BEGIN
6618    NEW.c3 = NEW.c3 || '_trig_update';
6619    RETURN NEW;
6620END;
6621$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
6622CREATE TRIGGER t1_br_insert BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE
6623    ON "S 1"."T 1" FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE "S 1".F_BRTRIG();

6743-- Test errors thrown on remote side during update
6744ALTER TABLE "S 1"."T 1" ADD CONSTRAINT c2positive CHECK (c2 >= 0);
6745INSERT INTO ft1(c1, c2) VALUES(11, 12);  -- duplicate key
6746ERROR:  duplicate key value violates unique constraint "t1_pkey"
6747DETAIL:  Key ("C 1")=(11) already exists.
6748CONTEXT:  remote SQL command: INSERT INTO "S 1"."T 1"("C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7, $8)
6749INSERT INTO ft1(c1, c2) VALUES(11, 12) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; -- works
6750INSERT INTO ft1(c1, c2) VALUES(11, 12) ON CONFLICT (c1, c2) DO NOTHING; -- unsupported
6751ERROR:  there is no unique or exclusion constraint matching the ON CONFLICT specification
6752INSERT INTO ft1(c1, c2) VALUES(11, 12) ON CONFLICT (c1, c2) DO UPDATE SET c3 = 'ffg'; -- unsupported
6753ERROR:  there is no unique or exclusion constraint matching the ON CONFLICT specification
6754INSERT INTO ft1(c1, c2) VALUES(1111, -2);  -- c2positive
6755ERROR:  new row for relation "T 1" violates check constraint "c2positive"
6756DETAIL:  Failing row contains (1111, -2, null, null, null, null, ft1       , null).
6757CONTEXT:  remote SQL command: INSERT INTO "S 1"."T 1"("C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7, $8)
6758UPDATE ft1 SET c2 = -c2 WHERE c1 = 1;  -- c2positive
6759ERROR:  new row for relation "T 1" violates check constraint "c2positive"
6760DETAIL:  Failing row contains (1, -1, 00001_trig_update, 1970-01-02 08:00:00+00, 1970-01-02 00:00:00, 1, 1         , foo).
6761CONTEXT:  remote SQL command: UPDATE "S 1"."T 1" SET c2 = (- c2) WHERE (("C 1" = 1))

6974 403 |     2
6975 407 |   100
6976(13 rows)

7016-- Above DMLs add data with c6 as NULL in ft1, so test ORDER BY NULLS LAST and NULLs
7017-- FIRST behavior here.
7018-- ORDER BY DESC NULLS LAST options
7019EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) SELECT * FROM ft1 ORDER BY c6 DESC NULLS LAST, c1 OFFSET 795 LIMIT 10;

7090-- Test ReScan code path that recreates the cursor even when no parameters
7091-- change (bug #17889)
7092CREATE TABLE loct1 (c1 int);
7093CREATE TABLE loct2 (c1 int, c2 text);
7094INSERT INTO loct1 VALUES (1001);
7095INSERT INTO loct1 VALUES (1002);
7096INSERT INTO loct2 SELECT id, to_char(id, 'FM0000') FROM generate_series(1, 1000) id;
7097INSERT INTO loct2 VALUES (1001, 'foo');
7098INSERT INTO loct2 VALUES (1002, 'bar');
7099CREATE FOREIGN TABLE remt2 (c1 int, c2 text) SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'loct2');
7100ANALYZE loct1;
7101ANALYZE remt2;
7102SET enable_mergejoin TO false;
7103SET enable_hashjoin TO false;
7104SET enable_material TO false;
7105EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
7106UPDATE remt2 SET c2 = remt2.c2 || remt2.c2 FROM loct1 WHERE loct1.c1 = remt2.c1 RETURNING remt2.*;
7107                                   QUERY PLAN                                   
7108--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7109 Update on public.remt2
7110   Output: remt2.c1, remt2.c2
7111   Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loct2 SET c2 = $2 WHERE ctid = $1 RETURNING c1, c2
7112   ->  Nested Loop
7113         Output: (remt2.c2 || remt2.c2), remt2.ctid, remt2.*, loct1.ctid
7114         Join Filter: (remt2.c1 = loct1.c1)
7115         ->  Seq Scan on public.loct1
7116               Output: loct1.ctid, loct1.c1
7117         ->  Foreign Scan on public.remt2
7118               Output: remt2.c2, remt2.ctid, remt2.*, remt2.c1
7119               Remote SQL: SELECT c1, c2, ctid FROM public.loct2 FOR UPDATE
7120(11 rows)
7121
