7122UPDATE remt2 SET c2 = remt2.c2 || remt2.c2 FROM loct1 WHERE loct1.c1 = remt2.c1 RETURNING remt2.*;
7123  c1  |   c2   
7124------+--------
7125 1001 | foofoo
7126 1002 | barbar
7127(2 rows)
7128
7129RESET enable_mergejoin;
7130RESET enable_hashjoin;
7131RESET enable_material;
7132DROP FOREIGN TABLE remt2;
7133DROP TABLE loct1;
7134DROP TABLE loct2;
7135-- ===================================================================
7136-- test check constraints
7137-- ===================================================================
7138-- Consistent check constraints provide consistent results
7139ALTER FOREIGN TABLE ft1 ADD CONSTRAINT ft1_c2positive CHECK (c2 >= 0);
7140EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) SELECT count(*) FROM ft1 WHERE c2 < 0;
7141                           QUERY PLAN                            
7142-----------------------------------------------------------------
7143 Foreign Scan
7144   Output: (count(*))
7145   Relations: Aggregate on (public.ft1)
7146   Remote SQL: SELECT count(*) FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE ((c2 < 0))
7147(4 rows)
7148
7149SELECT count(*) FROM ft1 WHERE c2 < 0;
7150 count 
7151-------
7152     0
7153(1 row)
7154
7155SET constraint_exclusion = 'on';
7156EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) SELECT count(*) FROM ft1 WHERE c2 < 0;
7157           QUERY PLAN           
7158--------------------------------
7159 Aggregate
7160   Output: count(*)
7161   ->  Result
7162         One-Time Filter: false
7163(4 rows)
7164
7165SELECT count(*) FROM ft1 WHERE c2 < 0;
7166 count 
7167-------
7168     0
7169(1 row)
7170
7171RESET constraint_exclusion;
7172-- check constraint is enforced on the remote side, not locally
7173INSERT INTO ft1(c1, c2) VALUES(1111, -2);  -- c2positive
7174ERROR:  new row for relation "T 1" violates check constraint "c2positive"
7175DETAIL:  Failing row contains (1111, -2, null, null, null, null, ft1       , null).
7176CONTEXT:  remote SQL command: INSERT INTO "S 1"."T 1"("C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7, $8)
7177UPDATE ft1 SET c2 = -c2 WHERE c1 = 1;  -- c2positive
7178ERROR:  new row for relation "T 1" violates check constraint "c2positive"
7179DETAIL:  Failing row contains (1, -1, 00001_trig_update, 1970-01-02 08:00:00+00, 1970-01-02 00:00:00, 1, 1         , foo).
7180CONTEXT:  remote SQL command: UPDATE "S 1"."T 1" SET c2 = (- c2) WHERE (("C 1" = 1))
7181ALTER FOREIGN TABLE ft1 DROP CONSTRAINT ft1_c2positive;
7182-- But inconsistent check constraints provide inconsistent results
7183ALTER FOREIGN TABLE ft1 ADD CONSTRAINT ft1_c2negative CHECK (c2 < 0);
7184EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) SELECT count(*) FROM ft1 WHERE c2 >= 0;
7185                            QUERY PLAN                            
7186------------------------------------------------------------------
7187 Foreign Scan
7188   Output: (count(*))
7189   Relations: Aggregate on (public.ft1)
7190   Remote SQL: SELECT count(*) FROM "S 1"."T 1" WHERE ((c2 >= 0))
7191(4 rows)
7192
7193SELECT count(*) FROM ft1 WHERE c2 >= 0;
7194 count 
7195-------
7196   821
7197(1 row)
7198
7199SET constraint_exclusion = 'on';
7200EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) SELECT count(*) FROM ft1 WHERE c2 >= 0;
7201           QUERY PLAN           
7202--------------------------------
7203 Aggregate
7204   Output: count(*)
7205   ->  Result
7206         One-Time Filter: false
7207(4 rows)
7208
7209SELECT count(*) FROM ft1 WHERE c2 >= 0;
7210 count 
7211-------
7212     0
7213(1 row)
7214
7215RESET constraint_exclusion;
7216-- local check constraint is not actually enforced
7217INSERT INTO ft1(c1, c2) VALUES(1111, 2);
7218UPDATE ft1 SET c2 = c2 + 1 WHERE c1 = 1;
7219ALTER FOREIGN TABLE ft1 DROP CONSTRAINT ft1_c2negative;
7220-- ===================================================================
7221-- test WITH CHECK OPTION constraints
7222-- ===================================================================
7223CREATE FUNCTION row_before_insupd_trigfunc() RETURNS trigger AS $$BEGIN NEW.a := NEW.a + 10; RETURN NEW; END$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
7224CREATE TABLE base_tbl (a int, b int);
7225ALTER TABLE base_tbl SET (autovacuum_enabled = 'false');
7226CREATE TRIGGER row_before_insupd_trigger BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE ON base_tbl FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE row_before_insupd_trigfunc();
7227CREATE FOREIGN TABLE foreign_tbl (a int, b int)
7228  SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'base_tbl');
7229CREATE VIEW rw_view AS SELECT * FROM foreign_tbl
7230  WHERE a < b WITH CHECK OPTION;
7231\d+ rw_view
7232                           View "public.rw_view"
7233 Column |  Type   | Collation | Nullable | Default | Storage | Description 
7234--------+---------+-----------+----------+---------+---------+-------------
7235 a      | integer |           |          |         | plain   | 
7236 b      | integer |           |          |         | plain   | 
7237View definition:
7238 SELECT a,
7239    b
7240   FROM foreign_tbl
7241  WHERE a < b;
7242Options: check_option=cascaded
7243
7244EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
7245INSERT INTO rw_view VALUES (0, 5);
7246                                   QUERY PLAN                                   
7247--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7248 Insert on public.foreign_tbl
7249   Remote SQL: INSERT INTO public.base_tbl(a, b) VALUES ($1, $2) RETURNING a, b
7250   Batch Size: 1
7251   ->  Result
7252         Output: 0, 5
7253(5 rows)
7254
7255INSERT INTO rw_view VALUES (0, 5); -- should fail
7256ERROR:  new row violates check option for view "rw_view"
7257DETAIL:  Failing row contains (10, 5).
7258EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
7259INSERT INTO rw_view VALUES (0, 15);
7260                                   QUERY PLAN                                   
7261--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7262 Insert on public.foreign_tbl
7263   Remote SQL: INSERT INTO public.base_tbl(a, b) VALUES ($1, $2) RETURNING a, b
7264   Batch Size: 1
7265   ->  Result
7266         Output: 0, 15
7267(5 rows)
7268
7269INSERT INTO rw_view VALUES (0, 15); -- ok
7270SELECT * FROM foreign_tbl;
7271 a  | b  
7272----+----
7273 10 | 15
7274(1 row)
7275
7276EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
7277UPDATE rw_view SET b = b + 5;
7278                                      QUERY PLAN                                       
7279---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7280 Update on public.foreign_tbl
7281   Remote SQL: UPDATE public.base_tbl SET b = $2 WHERE ctid = $1 RETURNING a, b
7282   ->  Foreign Scan on public.foreign_tbl
7283         Output: (foreign_tbl.b + 5), foreign_tbl.ctid, foreign_tbl.*
7284         Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, ctid FROM public.base_tbl WHERE ((a < b)) FOR UPDATE
7285(5 rows)
7286
7287UPDATE rw_view SET b = b + 5; -- should fail
7288ERROR:  new row violates check option for view "rw_view"
7289DETAIL:  Failing row contains (20, 20).
7290EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
7291UPDATE rw_view SET b = b + 15;
7292                                      QUERY PLAN                                       
7293---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7294 Update on public.foreign_tbl
7295   Remote SQL: UPDATE public.base_tbl SET b = $2 WHERE ctid = $1 RETURNING a, b
7296   ->  Foreign Scan on public.foreign_tbl
7297         Output: (foreign_tbl.b + 15), foreign_tbl.ctid, foreign_tbl.*
7298         Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, ctid FROM public.base_tbl WHERE ((a < b)) FOR UPDATE
7299(5 rows)
7300
7301UPDATE rw_view SET b = b + 15; -- ok
7302SELECT * FROM foreign_tbl;
7303 a  | b  
7304----+----
7305 20 | 30
7306(1 row)
7307
7308-- We don't allow batch insert when there are any WCO constraints
7309ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (ADD batch_size '10');
7310EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
7311INSERT INTO rw_view VALUES (0, 15), (0, 5);
7312                                   QUERY PLAN                                   
7313--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7314 Insert on public.foreign_tbl
7315   Remote SQL: INSERT INTO public.base_tbl(a, b) VALUES ($1, $2) RETURNING a, b
7316   Batch Size: 1
7317   ->  Values Scan on "*VALUES*"
7318         Output: "*VALUES*".column1, "*VALUES*".column2
7319(5 rows)
7320
7321INSERT INTO rw_view VALUES (0, 15), (0, 5); -- should fail
7322ERROR:  new row violates check option for view "rw_view"
7323DETAIL:  Failing row contains (10, 5).
7324SELECT * FROM foreign_tbl;
7325 a  | b  
7326----+----
7327 20 | 30
7328(1 row)
7329
7330ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (DROP batch_size);
7331DROP FOREIGN TABLE foreign_tbl CASCADE;
7332NOTICE:  drop cascades to view rw_view
7333DROP TRIGGER row_before_insupd_trigger ON base_tbl;
7334DROP TABLE base_tbl;
7335-- test WCO for partitions
7336CREATE TABLE child_tbl (a int, b int);
7337ALTER TABLE child_tbl SET (autovacuum_enabled = 'false');
7338CREATE TRIGGER row_before_insupd_trigger BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE ON child_tbl FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE row_before_insupd_trigfunc();
7339CREATE FOREIGN TABLE foreign_tbl (a int, b int)
7340  SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'child_tbl');
7341CREATE TABLE parent_tbl (a int, b int) PARTITION BY RANGE(a);
7342ALTER TABLE parent_tbl ATTACH PARTITION foreign_tbl FOR VALUES FROM (0) TO (100);
7343-- Detach and re-attach once, to stress the concurrent detach case.
7344ALTER TABLE parent_tbl DETACH PARTITION foreign_tbl CONCURRENTLY;
7345ALTER TABLE parent_tbl ATTACH PARTITION foreign_tbl FOR VALUES FROM (0) TO (100);
7346CREATE VIEW rw_view AS SELECT * FROM parent_tbl
7347  WHERE a < b WITH CHECK OPTION;
7348\d+ rw_view
7349                           View "public.rw_view"
7350 Column |  Type   | Collation | Nullable | Default | Storage | Description 
7351--------+---------+-----------+----------+---------+---------+-------------
7352 a      | integer |           |          |         | plain   | 
7353 b      | integer |           |          |         | plain   | 
7354View definition:
7355 SELECT a,
7356    b
7357   FROM parent_tbl
7358  WHERE a < b;
7359Options: check_option=cascaded
7360
7361EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
7362INSERT INTO rw_view VALUES (0, 5);
7363         QUERY PLAN          
7364-----------------------------
7365 Insert on public.parent_tbl
7366   ->  Result
7367         Output: 0, 5
7368(3 rows)
7369
7370INSERT INTO rw_view VALUES (0, 5); -- should fail
7371ERROR:  new row violates check option for view "rw_view"
7372DETAIL:  Failing row contains (10, 5).
7373EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
7374INSERT INTO rw_view VALUES (0, 15);
7375         QUERY PLAN          
7376-----------------------------
7377 Insert on public.parent_tbl
7378   ->  Result
7379         Output: 0, 15
7380(3 rows)
7381
7382INSERT INTO rw_view VALUES (0, 15); -- ok
7383SELECT * FROM foreign_tbl;
7384 a  | b  
7385----+----
7386 10 | 15
7387(1 row)
7388
7389EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
7390UPDATE rw_view SET b = b + 5;
7391                                           QUERY PLAN                                           
7392------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7393 Update on public.parent_tbl
7394   Foreign Update on public.foreign_tbl parent_tbl_1
7395     Remote SQL: UPDATE public.child_tbl SET b = $2 WHERE ctid = $1 RETURNING a, b
7396   ->  Foreign Scan on public.foreign_tbl parent_tbl_1
7397         Output: (parent_tbl_1.b + 5), parent_tbl_1.tableoid, parent_tbl_1.ctid, parent_tbl_1.*
7398         Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, ctid FROM public.child_tbl WHERE ((a < b)) FOR UPDATE
7399(6 rows)
7400
7401UPDATE rw_view SET b = b + 5; -- should fail
7402ERROR:  new row violates check option for view "rw_view"
7403DETAIL:  Failing row contains (20, 20).
7404EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
7405UPDATE rw_view SET b = b + 15;
7406                                           QUERY PLAN                                            
7407-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7408 Update on public.parent_tbl
7409   Foreign Update on public.foreign_tbl parent_tbl_1
7410     Remote SQL: UPDATE public.child_tbl SET b = $2 WHERE ctid = $1 RETURNING a, b
7411   ->  Foreign Scan on public.foreign_tbl parent_tbl_1
7412         Output: (parent_tbl_1.b + 15), parent_tbl_1.tableoid, parent_tbl_1.ctid, parent_tbl_1.*
7413         Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, ctid FROM public.child_tbl WHERE ((a < b)) FOR UPDATE
7414(6 rows)
7415
7416UPDATE rw_view SET b = b + 15; -- ok
7417SELECT * FROM foreign_tbl;
7418 a  | b  
7419----+----
7420 20 | 30
7421(1 row)
7422
7423-- We don't allow batch insert when there are any WCO constraints
7424ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (ADD batch_size '10');
7425EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
7426INSERT INTO rw_view VALUES (0, 15), (0, 5);
7427                       QUERY PLAN                       
7428--------------------------------------------------------
7429 Insert on public.parent_tbl
7430   ->  Values Scan on "*VALUES*"
7431         Output: "*VALUES*".column1, "*VALUES*".column2
7432(3 rows)
7433
7434INSERT INTO rw_view VALUES (0, 15), (0, 5); -- should fail
7435ERROR:  new row violates check option for view "rw_view"
7436DETAIL:  Failing row contains (10, 5).
7437SELECT * FROM foreign_tbl;
7438 a  | b  
7439----+----
7440 20 | 30
7441(1 row)
7442
7443ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (DROP batch_size);
7444DROP FOREIGN TABLE foreign_tbl CASCADE;
7445DROP TRIGGER row_before_insupd_trigger ON child_tbl;
7446DROP TABLE parent_tbl CASCADE;
7447NOTICE:  drop cascades to view rw_view
7448DROP FUNCTION row_before_insupd_trigfunc;
7449-- Try a more complex permutation of WCO where there are multiple levels of
7450-- partitioned tables with columns not all in the same order
7451CREATE TABLE parent_tbl (a int, b text, c numeric) PARTITION BY RANGE(a);
7452CREATE TABLE sub_parent (c numeric, a int, b text) PARTITION BY RANGE(a);
7453ALTER TABLE parent_tbl ATTACH PARTITION sub_parent FOR VALUES FROM (1) TO (10);
7454CREATE TABLE child_local (b text, c numeric, a int);
7455CREATE FOREIGN TABLE child_foreign (b text, c numeric, a int)
7456  SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'child_local');
7457ALTER TABLE sub_parent ATTACH PARTITION child_foreign FOR VALUES FROM (1) TO (10);
7458CREATE VIEW rw_view AS SELECT * FROM parent_tbl WHERE a < 5 WITH CHECK OPTION;
7459INSERT INTO parent_tbl (a) VALUES(1),(5);
7460EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
7461UPDATE rw_view SET b = 'text', c = 123.456;
7462                                           QUERY PLAN                                            
7463-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7464 Update on public.parent_tbl
7465   Foreign Update on public.child_foreign parent_tbl_1
7466     Remote SQL: UPDATE public.child_local SET b = $2, c = $3 WHERE ctid = $1 RETURNING a
7467   ->  Foreign Scan on public.child_foreign parent_tbl_1
7468         Output: 'text'::text, 123.456, parent_tbl_1.tableoid, parent_tbl_1.ctid, parent_tbl_1.*
7469         Remote SQL: SELECT b, c, a, ctid FROM public.child_local WHERE ((a < 5)) FOR UPDATE
7470(6 rows)
7471
7472UPDATE rw_view SET b = 'text', c = 123.456;
7473SELECT * FROM parent_tbl ORDER BY a;
7474 a |  b   |    c    
7475---+------+---------
7476 1 | text | 123.456
7477 5 |      |        
7478(2 rows)
7479
7480DROP VIEW rw_view;
7481DROP TABLE child_local;
7482DROP FOREIGN TABLE child_foreign;
7483DROP TABLE sub_parent;
7484DROP TABLE parent_tbl;
7485-- ===================================================================
7486-- test serial columns (ie, sequence-based defaults)
7487-- ===================================================================
7488create table loc1 (f1 serial, f2 text);
7489alter table loc1 set (autovacuum_enabled = 'false');
7490create foreign table rem1 (f1 serial, f2 text)
7491  server loopback options(table_name 'loc1');
7492select pg_catalog.setval('rem1_f1_seq', 10, false);
7493 setval 
7494--------
7495     10
7496(1 row)
7497
7498insert into loc1(f2) values('hi');
7499insert into rem1(f2) values('hi remote');
7500insert into loc1(f2) values('bye');
7501insert into rem1(f2) values('bye remote');
7502select * from loc1;
7503 f1 |     f2     
7504----+------------
7505  1 | hi
7506 10 | hi remote
7507  2 | bye
7508 11 | bye remote
7509(4 rows)
7510
7511select * from rem1;
7512 f1 |     f2     
7513----+------------
7514  1 | hi
7515 10 | hi remote
7516  2 | bye
7517 11 | bye remote
7518(4 rows)
7519
7520-- ===================================================================
7521-- test generated columns
7522-- ===================================================================
7523create table gloc1 (
7524  a int,
7525  b int generated always as (a * 2) stored,
7526  c int
7527);
7528alter table gloc1 set (autovacuum_enabled = 'false');
7529create foreign table grem1 (
7530  a int,
7531  b int generated always as (a * 2) stored,
7532  c int generated always as (a * 3) virtual
7533) server loopback options(table_name 'gloc1');
7534explain (verbose, costs off)
7535insert into grem1 (a) values (1), (2);
7536                                  QUERY PLAN                                   
7537-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7538 Insert on public.grem1
7539   Remote SQL: INSERT INTO public.gloc1(a, b, c) VALUES ($1, DEFAULT, DEFAULT)
7540   Batch Size: 1
7541   ->  Values Scan on "*VALUES*"
7542         Output: "*VALUES*".column1, NULL::integer, NULL::integer
7543(5 rows)
7544
7545insert into grem1 (a) values (1), (2);
7546explain (verbose, costs off)
7547update grem1 set a = 22 where a = 2;
7548                                       QUERY PLAN                                       
7549----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7550 Update on public.grem1
7551   Remote SQL: UPDATE public.gloc1 SET a = $2, b = DEFAULT, c = DEFAULT WHERE ctid = $1
7552   ->  Foreign Scan on public.grem1
7553         Output: 22, ctid, grem1.*
7554         Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c, ctid FROM public.gloc1 WHERE ((a = 2)) FOR UPDATE
7555(5 rows)
7556
7557update grem1 set a = 22 where a = 2;
7558select * from gloc1;
7559 a  | b  | c 
7560----+----+---
7561  1 |  2 |  
7562 22 | 44 |  
7563(2 rows)
7564
7565select * from grem1;
7566 a  | b  | c  
7567----+----+----
7568  1 |  2 |  3
7569 22 | 44 | 66
7570(2 rows)
7571
7572delete from grem1;
7573-- test copy from
7574copy grem1 from stdin;
7575select * from gloc1;
7576 a | b | c 
7577---+---+---
7578 1 | 2 |  
7579 2 | 4 |  
7580(2 rows)
7581
7582select * from grem1;
7583 a | b | c 
7584---+---+---
7585 1 | 2 | 3
7586 2 | 4 | 6
7587(2 rows)
7588
7589delete from grem1;
7590-- test batch insert
7591alter server loopback options (add batch_size '10');
7592explain (verbose, costs off)
7593insert into grem1 (a) values (1), (2);
7594                                  QUERY PLAN                                   
7595-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7596 Insert on public.grem1
7597   Remote SQL: INSERT INTO public.gloc1(a, b, c) VALUES ($1, DEFAULT, DEFAULT)
7598   Batch Size: 10
7599   ->  Values Scan on "*VALUES*"
7600         Output: "*VALUES*".column1, NULL::integer, NULL::integer
7601(5 rows)
7602
7603insert into grem1 (a) values (1), (2);
7604select * from gloc1;
7605 a | b | c 
7606---+---+---
7607 1 | 2 |  
7608 2 | 4 |  
7609(2 rows)
7610
7611select * from grem1;
7612 a | b | c 
7613---+---+---
7614 1 | 2 | 3
7615 2 | 4 | 6
7616(2 rows)
7617
7618delete from grem1;
7619-- batch insert with foreign partitions.
7620-- This schema uses two partitions, one local and one remote with a modulo
7621-- to loop across all of them in batches.
7622create table tab_batch_local (id int, data text);
7623insert into tab_batch_local select i, 'test'|| i from generate_series(1, 45) i;
7624create table tab_batch_sharded (id int, data text) partition by hash(id);
7625create table tab_batch_sharded_p0 partition of tab_batch_sharded
7626  for values with (modulus 2, remainder 0);
7627create table tab_batch_sharded_p1_remote (id int, data text);
7628create foreign table tab_batch_sharded_p1 partition of tab_batch_sharded
7629  for values with (modulus 2, remainder 1)
7630  server loopback options (table_name 'tab_batch_sharded_p1_remote');
7631insert into tab_batch_sharded select * from tab_batch_local;
7632select count(*) from tab_batch_sharded;
7633 count 
7634-------
7635    45
7636(1 row)
7637
7638drop table tab_batch_local;
7639drop table tab_batch_sharded;
7640drop table tab_batch_sharded_p1_remote;
7641alter server loopback options (drop batch_size);
7642-- ===================================================================
7643-- test local triggers
7644-- ===================================================================
7645-- Trigger functions "borrowed" from triggers regress test.
7646CREATE FUNCTION trigger_func() RETURNS trigger LANGUAGE plpgsql AS $$
7647BEGIN
7648	RAISE NOTICE 'trigger_func(%) called: action = %, when = %, level = %',
7649		TG_ARGV[0], TG_OP, TG_WHEN, TG_LEVEL;
7650	RETURN NULL;
7651END;$$;
7652CREATE TRIGGER trig_stmt_before BEFORE DELETE OR INSERT OR UPDATE OR TRUNCATE ON rem1
7653	FOR EACH STATEMENT EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_func();
7654CREATE TRIGGER trig_stmt_after AFTER DELETE OR INSERT OR UPDATE OR TRUNCATE ON rem1
7655	FOR EACH STATEMENT EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_func();
7656CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION trigger_data()  RETURNS trigger
7657LANGUAGE plpgsql AS $$
7658
7659declare
7660	oldnew text[];
7661	relid text;
7662    argstr text;
7663begin
7664
7665	relid := TG_relid::regclass;
7666	argstr := '';
7667	for i in 0 .. TG_nargs - 1 loop
7668		if i > 0 then
7669			argstr := argstr || ', ';
7670		end if;
7671		argstr := argstr || TG_argv[i];
7672	end loop;
7673
7674    RAISE NOTICE '%(%) % % % ON %',
7675		tg_name, argstr, TG_when, TG_level, TG_OP, relid;
7676    oldnew := '{}'::text[];
7677	if TG_OP != 'INSERT' then
7678		oldnew := array_append(oldnew, format('OLD: %s', OLD));
7679	end if;
7680
7681	if TG_OP != 'DELETE' then
7682		oldnew := array_append(oldnew, format('NEW: %s', NEW));
7683	end if;
7684
7685    RAISE NOTICE '%', array_to_string(oldnew, ',');
7686
7687	if TG_OP = 'DELETE' then
7688		return OLD;
7689	else
7690		return NEW;
7691	end if;
7692end;
7693$$;
7694-- Test basic functionality
7695CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_before
7696BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE OR DELETE ON rem1
7697FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
7698CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_after
7699AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE OR DELETE ON rem1
7700FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
7701delete from rem1;
7702NOTICE:  trigger_func(<NULL>) called: action = DELETE, when = BEFORE, level = STATEMENT
7703NOTICE:  trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW DELETE ON rem1
7704NOTICE:  OLD: (1,hi)
7705NOTICE:  trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW DELETE ON rem1
7706NOTICE:  OLD: (10,"hi remote")
7707NOTICE:  trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW DELETE ON rem1
7708NOTICE:  OLD: (2,bye)
7709NOTICE:  trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW DELETE ON rem1
7710NOTICE:  OLD: (11,"bye remote")
7711NOTICE:  trig_row_after(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW DELETE ON rem1
7712NOTICE:  OLD: (1,hi)
7713NOTICE:  trig_row_after(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW DELETE ON rem1
7714NOTICE:  OLD: (10,"hi remote")
7715NOTICE:  trig_row_after(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW DELETE ON rem1
7716NOTICE:  OLD: (2,bye)
7717NOTICE:  trig_row_after(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW DELETE ON rem1
7718NOTICE:  OLD: (11,"bye remote")
7719NOTICE:  trigger_func(<NULL>) called: action = DELETE, when = AFTER, level = STATEMENT
7720insert into rem1 values(1,'insert');
7721NOTICE:  trigger_func(<NULL>) called: action = INSERT, when = BEFORE, level = STATEMENT
7722NOTICE:  trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW INSERT ON rem1
7723NOTICE:  NEW: (1,insert)
7724NOTICE:  trig_row_after(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW INSERT ON rem1
7725NOTICE:  NEW: (1,insert)
7726NOTICE:  trigger_func(<NULL>) called: action = INSERT, when = AFTER, level = STATEMENT
7727update rem1 set f2  = 'update' where f1 = 1;
7728NOTICE:  trigger_func(<NULL>) called: action = UPDATE, when = BEFORE, level = STATEMENT
7729NOTICE:  trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW UPDATE ON rem1
7730NOTICE:  OLD: (1,insert),NEW: (1,update)
7731NOTICE:  trig_row_after(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW UPDATE ON rem1
7732NOTICE:  OLD: (1,insert),NEW: (1,update)
7733NOTICE:  trigger_func(<NULL>) called: action = UPDATE, when = AFTER, level = STATEMENT
7734update rem1 set f2 = f2 || f2;
7735NOTICE:  trigger_func(<NULL>) called: action = UPDATE, when = BEFORE, level = STATEMENT
7736NOTICE:  trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW UPDATE ON rem1
7737NOTICE:  OLD: (1,update),NEW: (1,updateupdate)
7738NOTICE:  trig_row_after(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW UPDATE ON rem1
7739NOTICE:  OLD: (1,update),NEW: (1,updateupdate)
7740NOTICE:  trigger_func(<NULL>) called: action = UPDATE, when = AFTER, level = STATEMENT
7741truncate rem1;
7742NOTICE:  trigger_func(<NULL>) called: action = TRUNCATE, when = BEFORE, level = STATEMENT
7743NOTICE:  trigger_func(<NULL>) called: action = TRUNCATE, when = AFTER, level = STATEMENT
7744-- cleanup
7745DROP TRIGGER trig_row_before ON rem1;
7746DROP TRIGGER trig_row_after ON rem1;
7747DROP TRIGGER trig_stmt_before ON rem1;
7748DROP TRIGGER trig_stmt_after ON rem1;
7749DELETE from rem1;
7750-- Test multiple AFTER ROW triggers on a foreign table
7751CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_after1
7752AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE OR DELETE ON rem1
7753FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
7754CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_after2
7755AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE OR DELETE ON rem1
7756FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
7757insert into rem1 values(1,'insert');
7758NOTICE:  trig_row_after1(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW INSERT ON rem1
7759NOTICE:  NEW: (1,insert)
7760NOTICE:  trig_row_after2(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW INSERT ON rem1
7761NOTICE:  NEW: (1,insert)
7762update rem1 set f2  = 'update' where f1 = 1;
7763NOTICE:  trig_row_after1(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW UPDATE ON rem1
7764NOTICE:  OLD: (1,insert),NEW: (1,update)
7765NOTICE:  trig_row_after2(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW UPDATE ON rem1
7766NOTICE:  OLD: (1,insert),NEW: (1,update)
7767update rem1 set f2 = f2 || f2;
7768NOTICE:  trig_row_after1(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW UPDATE ON rem1
7769NOTICE:  OLD: (1,update),NEW: (1,updateupdate)
7770NOTICE:  trig_row_after2(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW UPDATE ON rem1
7771NOTICE:  OLD: (1,update),NEW: (1,updateupdate)
7772delete from rem1;
7773NOTICE:  trig_row_after1(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW DELETE ON rem1
7774NOTICE:  OLD: (1,updateupdate)
7775NOTICE:  trig_row_after2(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW DELETE ON rem1
7776NOTICE:  OLD: (1,updateupdate)
7777-- cleanup
7778DROP TRIGGER trig_row_after1 ON rem1;
7779DROP TRIGGER trig_row_after2 ON rem1;
7780-- Test WHEN conditions
7781CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_before_insupd
7782BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE ON rem1
7783FOR EACH ROW
7784WHEN (NEW.f2 like '%update%')
7785EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
7786CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_after_insupd
7787AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE ON rem1
7788FOR EACH ROW
7789WHEN (NEW.f2 like '%update%')
7790EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
7791-- Insert or update not matching: nothing happens
7792INSERT INTO rem1 values(1, 'insert');
7793UPDATE rem1 set f2 = 'test';
7794-- Insert or update matching: triggers are fired
7795INSERT INTO rem1 values(2, 'update');
7796NOTICE:  trig_row_before_insupd(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW INSERT ON rem1
7797NOTICE:  NEW: (2,update)
7798NOTICE:  trig_row_after_insupd(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW INSERT ON rem1
7799NOTICE:  NEW: (2,update)
7800UPDATE rem1 set f2 = 'update update' where f1 = '2';
7801NOTICE:  trig_row_before_insupd(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW UPDATE ON rem1
7802NOTICE:  OLD: (2,update),NEW: (2,"update update")
7803NOTICE:  trig_row_after_insupd(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW UPDATE ON rem1
7804NOTICE:  OLD: (2,update),NEW: (2,"update update")
7805CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_before_delete
7806BEFORE DELETE ON rem1
7807FOR EACH ROW
7808WHEN (OLD.f2 like '%update%')
7809EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
7810CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_after_delete
7811AFTER DELETE ON rem1
7812FOR EACH ROW
7813WHEN (OLD.f2 like '%update%')
7814EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
7815-- Trigger is fired for f1=2, not for f1=1
7816DELETE FROM rem1;
7817NOTICE:  trig_row_before_delete(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW DELETE ON rem1
7818NOTICE:  OLD: (2,"update update")
7819NOTICE:  trig_row_after_delete(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW DELETE ON rem1
7820NOTICE:  OLD: (2,"update update")
7821-- cleanup
7822DROP TRIGGER trig_row_before_insupd ON rem1;
7823DROP TRIGGER trig_row_after_insupd ON rem1;
7824DROP TRIGGER trig_row_before_delete ON rem1;
7825DROP TRIGGER trig_row_after_delete ON rem1;
7826-- Test various RETURN statements in BEFORE triggers.
7827CREATE FUNCTION trig_row_before_insupdate() RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
7828  BEGIN
7829    NEW.f2 := NEW.f2 || ' triggered !';
7830    RETURN NEW;
7831  END
7832$$ language plpgsql;
7833CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_before_insupd
7834BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE ON rem1
7835FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trig_row_before_insupdate();
7836-- The new values should have 'triggered' appended
7837INSERT INTO rem1 values(1, 'insert');
7838SELECT * from loc1;
7839 f1 |         f2         
7840----+--------------------
7841  1 | insert triggered !
7842(1 row)
7843
7844INSERT INTO rem1 values(2, 'insert') RETURNING f2;
7845         f2         
7846--------------------
7847 insert triggered !
7848(1 row)
7849
7850SELECT * from loc1;
7851 f1 |         f2         
7852----+--------------------
7853  1 | insert triggered !
7854  2 | insert triggered !
7855(2 rows)
7856
7857UPDATE rem1 set f2 = '';
7858SELECT * from loc1;
7859 f1 |      f2      
7860----+--------------
7861  1 |  triggered !
7862  2 |  triggered !
7863(2 rows)
7864
7865UPDATE rem1 set f2 = 'skidoo' RETURNING f2;
7866         f2         
7867--------------------
7868 skidoo triggered !
7869 skidoo triggered !
7870(2 rows)
7871
7872SELECT * from loc1;
7873 f1 |         f2         
7874----+--------------------
7875  1 | skidoo triggered !
7876  2 | skidoo triggered !
7877(2 rows)
7878
7879EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
7880UPDATE rem1 set f1 = 10;          -- all columns should be transmitted
7881                              QUERY PLAN                               
7882-----------------------------------------------------------------------
7883 Update on public.rem1
7884   Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loc1 SET f1 = $2, f2 = $3 WHERE ctid = $1
7885   ->  Foreign Scan on public.rem1
7886         Output: 10, ctid, rem1.*
7887         Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, ctid FROM public.loc1 FOR UPDATE
7888(5 rows)
7889
7890UPDATE rem1 set f1 = 10;
7891SELECT * from loc1;
7892 f1 |               f2               
7893----+--------------------------------
7894 10 | skidoo triggered ! triggered !
7895 10 | skidoo triggered ! triggered !
7896(2 rows)
7897
7898DELETE FROM rem1;
7899-- Add a second trigger, to check that the changes are propagated correctly
7900-- from trigger to trigger
7901CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_before_insupd2
7902BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE ON rem1
7903FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trig_row_before_insupdate();
7904INSERT INTO rem1 values(1, 'insert');
7905SELECT * from loc1;
7906 f1 |               f2               
7907----+--------------------------------
7908  1 | insert triggered ! triggered !
7909(1 row)
7910
7911INSERT INTO rem1 values(2, 'insert') RETURNING f2;
7912               f2               
7913--------------------------------
7914 insert triggered ! triggered !
7915(1 row)
7916
7917SELECT * from loc1;
7918 f1 |               f2               
7919----+--------------------------------
7920  1 | insert triggered ! triggered !
7921  2 | insert triggered ! triggered !
7922(2 rows)
7923
7924UPDATE rem1 set f2 = '';
7925SELECT * from loc1;
7926 f1 |            f2            
7927----+--------------------------
7928  1 |  triggered ! triggered !
7929  2 |  triggered ! triggered !
7930(2 rows)
7931
7932UPDATE rem1 set f2 = 'skidoo' RETURNING f2;
7933               f2               
7934--------------------------------
7935 skidoo triggered ! triggered !
7936 skidoo triggered ! triggered !
7937(2 rows)
7938
7939SELECT * from loc1;
7940 f1 |               f2               
7941----+--------------------------------
7942  1 | skidoo triggered ! triggered !
7943  2 | skidoo triggered ! triggered !
7944(2 rows)
7945
7946DROP TRIGGER trig_row_before_insupd ON rem1;
7947DROP TRIGGER trig_row_before_insupd2 ON rem1;
7948DELETE from rem1;
7949INSERT INTO rem1 VALUES (1, 'test');
7950-- Test with a trigger returning NULL
7951CREATE FUNCTION trig_null() RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
7952  BEGIN
7953    RETURN NULL;
7954  END
7955$$ language plpgsql;
7956CREATE TRIGGER trig_null
7957BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE OR DELETE ON rem1
7958FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trig_null();
7959-- Nothing should have changed.
7960INSERT INTO rem1 VALUES (2, 'test2');
7961SELECT * from loc1;
7962 f1 |  f2  
7963----+------
7964  1 | test
7965(1 row)
7966
7967UPDATE rem1 SET f2 = 'test2';
7968SELECT * from loc1;
7969 f1 |  f2  
7970----+------
7971  1 | test
7972(1 row)
7973
7974DELETE from rem1;
7975SELECT * from loc1;
7976 f1 |  f2  
7977----+------
7978  1 | test
7979(1 row)
7980
7981DROP TRIGGER trig_null ON rem1;
7982DELETE from rem1;
7983-- Test a combination of local and remote triggers
7984CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_before
7985BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE OR DELETE ON rem1
7986FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
7987CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_after
7988AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE OR DELETE ON rem1
7989FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
7990CREATE TRIGGER trig_local_before BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE ON loc1
7991FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trig_row_before_insupdate();
7992INSERT INTO rem1(f2) VALUES ('test');
7993NOTICE:  trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW INSERT ON rem1
7994NOTICE:  NEW: (12,test)
7995NOTICE:  trig_row_after(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW INSERT ON rem1
7996NOTICE:  NEW: (12,"test triggered !")
7997UPDATE rem1 SET f2 = 'testo';
7998NOTICE:  trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW UPDATE ON rem1
7999NOTICE:  OLD: (12,"test triggered !"),NEW: (12,testo)
8000NOTICE:  trig_row_after(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW UPDATE ON rem1
8001NOTICE:  OLD: (12,"test triggered !"),NEW: (12,"testo triggered !")
8002-- Test returning a system attribute
8003INSERT INTO rem1(f2) VALUES ('test') RETURNING ctid;
8004NOTICE:  trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW INSERT ON rem1
8005NOTICE:  NEW: (13,test)
8006NOTICE:  trig_row_after(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW INSERT ON rem1
8007NOTICE:  NEW: (13,"test triggered !")
8008  ctid  
8009--------
8010 (0,25)
8011(1 row)
8012
8013-- cleanup
8014DROP TRIGGER trig_row_before ON rem1;
8015DROP TRIGGER trig_row_after ON rem1;
8016DROP TRIGGER trig_local_before ON loc1;
8017-- Test direct foreign table modification functionality
8018EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
8019DELETE FROM rem1;                 -- can be pushed down
8020                 QUERY PLAN                  
8021---------------------------------------------
8022 Delete on public.rem1
8023   ->  Foreign Delete on public.rem1
8024         Remote SQL: DELETE FROM public.loc1
8025(3 rows)
8026
8027EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
8028DELETE FROM rem1 WHERE false;     -- currently can't be pushed down
8029                      QUERY PLAN                       
8030-------------------------------------------------------
8031 Delete on public.rem1
8032   Remote SQL: DELETE FROM public.loc1 WHERE ctid = $1
8033   ->  Result
8034         Output: ctid
8035         One-Time Filter: false
8036(5 rows)
8037
8038-- Test with statement-level triggers
8039CREATE TRIGGER trig_stmt_before
8040	BEFORE DELETE OR INSERT OR UPDATE ON rem1
8041	FOR EACH STATEMENT EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_func();
8042EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
8043UPDATE rem1 set f2 = '';          -- can be pushed down
8044                        QUERY PLAN                        
8045----------------------------------------------------------
8046 Update on public.rem1
8047   ->  Foreign Update on public.rem1
8048         Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loc1 SET f2 = ''::text
8049(3 rows)
8050
8051EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
8052DELETE FROM rem1;                 -- can be pushed down
8053                 QUERY PLAN                  
8054---------------------------------------------
8055 Delete on public.rem1
8056   ->  Foreign Delete on public.rem1
8057         Remote SQL: DELETE FROM public.loc1
8058(3 rows)
8059
8060DROP TRIGGER trig_stmt_before ON rem1;
8061CREATE TRIGGER trig_stmt_after
8062	AFTER DELETE OR INSERT OR UPDATE ON rem1
8063	FOR EACH STATEMENT EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_func();
8064EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
8065UPDATE rem1 set f2 = '';          -- can be pushed down
8066                        QUERY PLAN                        
8067----------------------------------------------------------
8068 Update on public.rem1
8069   ->  Foreign Update on public.rem1
8070         Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loc1 SET f2 = ''::text
8071(3 rows)
8072
8073EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
8074DELETE FROM rem1;                 -- can be pushed down
8075                 QUERY PLAN                  
8076---------------------------------------------
8077 Delete on public.rem1
8078   ->  Foreign Delete on public.rem1
8079         Remote SQL: DELETE FROM public.loc1
8080(3 rows)
8081
8082DROP TRIGGER trig_stmt_after ON rem1;
8083-- Test with row-level ON INSERT triggers
8084CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_before_insert
8085BEFORE INSERT ON rem1
8086FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
8087EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
8088UPDATE rem1 set f2 = '';          -- can be pushed down
8089                        QUERY PLAN                        
8090----------------------------------------------------------
8091 Update on public.rem1
8092   ->  Foreign Update on public.rem1
8093         Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loc1 SET f2 = ''::text
8094(3 rows)
8095
8096EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
8097DELETE FROM rem1;                 -- can be pushed down
8098                 QUERY PLAN                  
8099---------------------------------------------
8100 Delete on public.rem1
8101   ->  Foreign Delete on public.rem1
8102         Remote SQL: DELETE FROM public.loc1
8103(3 rows)
8104
8105DROP TRIGGER trig_row_before_insert ON rem1;
8106CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_after_insert
8107AFTER INSERT ON rem1
8108FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
8109EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
8110UPDATE rem1 set f2 = '';          -- can be pushed down
8111                        QUERY PLAN                        
8112----------------------------------------------------------
8113 Update on public.rem1
8114   ->  Foreign Update on public.rem1
8115         Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loc1 SET f2 = ''::text
8116(3 rows)
8117
8118EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
8119DELETE FROM rem1;                 -- can be pushed down
8120                 QUERY PLAN                  
8121---------------------------------------------
8122 Delete on public.rem1
8123   ->  Foreign Delete on public.rem1
8124         Remote SQL: DELETE FROM public.loc1
8125(3 rows)
8126
8127DROP TRIGGER trig_row_after_insert ON rem1;
8128-- Test with row-level ON UPDATE triggers
8129CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_before_update
8130BEFORE UPDATE ON rem1
8131FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
8132EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
8133UPDATE rem1 set f2 = '';          -- can't be pushed down
8134                              QUERY PLAN                               
8135-----------------------------------------------------------------------
8136 Update on public.rem1
8137   Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loc1 SET f1 = $2, f2 = $3 WHERE ctid = $1
8138   ->  Foreign Scan on public.rem1
8139         Output: ''::text, ctid, rem1.*
8140         Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, ctid FROM public.loc1 FOR UPDATE
8141(5 rows)
8142
8143EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
8144DELETE FROM rem1;                 -- can be pushed down
8145                 QUERY PLAN                  
8146---------------------------------------------
8147 Delete on public.rem1
8148   ->  Foreign Delete on public.rem1
8149         Remote SQL: DELETE FROM public.loc1
8150(3 rows)
8151
8152DROP TRIGGER trig_row_before_update ON rem1;
8153CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_after_update
8154AFTER UPDATE ON rem1
8155FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
8156EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
8157UPDATE rem1 set f2 = '';          -- can't be pushed down
8158                                  QUERY PLAN                                   
8159-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8160 Update on public.rem1
8161   Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loc1 SET f2 = $2 WHERE ctid = $1 RETURNING f1, f2
8162   ->  Foreign Scan on public.rem1
8163         Output: ''::text, ctid, rem1.*
8164         Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, ctid FROM public.loc1 FOR UPDATE
8165(5 rows)
8166
8167EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
8168DELETE FROM rem1;                 -- can be pushed down
8169                 QUERY PLAN                  
8170---------------------------------------------
8171 Delete on public.rem1
8172   ->  Foreign Delete on public.rem1
8173         Remote SQL: DELETE FROM public.loc1
8174(3 rows)
8175
8176DROP TRIGGER trig_row_after_update ON rem1;
8177-- Test with row-level ON DELETE triggers
8178CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_before_delete
8179BEFORE DELETE ON rem1
8180FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
8181EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
8182UPDATE rem1 set f2 = '';          -- can be pushed down
8183                        QUERY PLAN                        
8184----------------------------------------------------------
8185 Update on public.rem1
8186   ->  Foreign Update on public.rem1
8187         Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loc1 SET f2 = ''::text
8188(3 rows)
8189
8190EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
8191DELETE FROM rem1;                 -- can't be pushed down
8192                             QUERY PLAN                              
8193---------------------------------------------------------------------
8194 Delete on public.rem1
8195   Remote SQL: DELETE FROM public.loc1 WHERE ctid = $1
8196   ->  Foreign Scan on public.rem1
8197         Output: ctid, rem1.*
8198         Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, ctid FROM public.loc1 FOR UPDATE
8199(5 rows)
8200
8201DROP TRIGGER trig_row_before_delete ON rem1;
8202CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_after_delete
8203AFTER DELETE ON rem1
8204FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
8205EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
8206UPDATE rem1 set f2 = '';          -- can be pushed down
8207                        QUERY PLAN                        
8208----------------------------------------------------------
8209 Update on public.rem1
8210   ->  Foreign Update on public.rem1
8211         Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loc1 SET f2 = ''::text
8212(3 rows)
8213
8214EXPLAIN (verbose, costs off)
8215DELETE FROM rem1;                 -- can't be pushed down
8216                               QUERY PLAN                               
8217------------------------------------------------------------------------
8218 Delete on public.rem1
8219   Remote SQL: DELETE FROM public.loc1 WHERE ctid = $1 RETURNING f1, f2
8220   ->  Foreign Scan on public.rem1
8221         Output: ctid, rem1.*
8222         Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, ctid FROM public.loc1 FOR UPDATE
8223(5 rows)
8224
8225DROP TRIGGER trig_row_after_delete ON rem1;
8226-- We are allowed to create transition-table triggers on both kinds of
8227-- inheritance even if they contain foreign tables as children, but currently
8228-- collecting transition tuples from such foreign tables is not supported.
8229CREATE TABLE local_tbl (a text, b int);
8230CREATE FOREIGN TABLE foreign_tbl (a text, b int)
8231  SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'local_tbl');
8232INSERT INTO foreign_tbl VALUES ('AAA', 42);
8233-- Test case for partition hierarchy
8234CREATE TABLE parent_tbl (a text, b int) PARTITION BY LIST (a);
8235ALTER TABLE parent_tbl ATTACH PARTITION foreign_tbl FOR VALUES IN ('AAA');
8236CREATE TRIGGER parent_tbl_insert_trig
8237  AFTER INSERT ON parent_tbl REFERENCING NEW TABLE AS new_table
8238  FOR EACH STATEMENT EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_func();
8239CREATE TRIGGER parent_tbl_update_trig
8240  AFTER UPDATE ON parent_tbl REFERENCING OLD TABLE AS old_table NEW TABLE AS new_table
8241  FOR EACH STATEMENT EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_func();
8242CREATE TRIGGER parent_tbl_delete_trig
8243  AFTER DELETE ON parent_tbl REFERENCING OLD TABLE AS old_table
8244  FOR EACH STATEMENT EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_func();
8245INSERT INTO parent_tbl VALUES ('AAA', 42);
8246ERROR:  cannot collect transition tuples from child foreign tables
8247COPY parent_tbl (a, b) FROM stdin;
8248ERROR:  cannot collect transition tuples from child foreign tables
8249CONTEXT:  COPY parent_tbl, line 1: "AAA	42"
8250ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (ADD batch_size '10');
8251INSERT INTO parent_tbl VALUES ('AAA', 42);
8252ERROR:  cannot collect transition tuples from child foreign tables
8253COPY parent_tbl (a, b) FROM stdin;
8254ERROR:  cannot collect transition tuples from child foreign tables
8255CONTEXT:  COPY parent_tbl, line 1: "AAA	42"
8256ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (DROP batch_size);
8257EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
8258UPDATE parent_tbl SET b = b + 1;
8259                                           QUERY PLAN                                           
8260------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8261 Update on public.parent_tbl
8262   Foreign Update on public.foreign_tbl parent_tbl_1
8263     Remote SQL: UPDATE public.local_tbl SET b = $2 WHERE ctid = $1
8264   ->  Foreign Scan on public.foreign_tbl parent_tbl_1
8265         Output: (parent_tbl_1.b + 1), parent_tbl_1.tableoid, parent_tbl_1.ctid, parent_tbl_1.*
8266         Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, ctid FROM public.local_tbl FOR UPDATE
8267(6 rows)
8268
8269UPDATE parent_tbl SET b = b + 1;
8270ERROR:  cannot collect transition tuples from child foreign tables
8271EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
8272DELETE FROM parent_tbl;
8273                            QUERY PLAN                            
8274------------------------------------------------------------------
8275 Delete on public.parent_tbl
8276   Foreign Delete on public.foreign_tbl parent_tbl_1
8277     Remote SQL: DELETE FROM public.local_tbl WHERE ctid = $1
8278   ->  Foreign Scan on public.foreign_tbl parent_tbl_1
8279         Output: parent_tbl_1.tableoid, parent_tbl_1.ctid
8280         Remote SQL: SELECT ctid FROM public.local_tbl FOR UPDATE
8281(6 rows)
8282
8283DELETE FROM parent_tbl;
8284ERROR:  cannot collect transition tuples from child foreign tables
8285ALTER TABLE parent_tbl DETACH PARTITION foreign_tbl;
8286DROP TABLE parent_tbl;
8287-- Test case for non-partition hierarchy
8288CREATE TABLE parent_tbl (a text, b int);
8289ALTER FOREIGN TABLE foreign_tbl INHERIT parent_tbl;
8290CREATE TRIGGER parent_tbl_update_trig
8291  AFTER UPDATE ON parent_tbl REFERENCING OLD TABLE AS old_table NEW TABLE AS new_table
8292  FOR EACH STATEMENT EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_func();
8293CREATE TRIGGER parent_tbl_delete_trig
8294  AFTER DELETE ON parent_tbl REFERENCING OLD TABLE AS old_table
8295  FOR EACH STATEMENT EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_func();
8296EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
8297UPDATE parent_tbl SET b = b + 1;
8298                                              QUERY PLAN                                              
8299------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8300 Update on public.parent_tbl
8301   Update on public.parent_tbl parent_tbl_1
8302   Foreign Update on public.foreign_tbl parent_tbl_2
8303     Remote SQL: UPDATE public.local_tbl SET b = $2 WHERE ctid = $1
8304   ->  Result
8305         Output: (parent_tbl.b + 1), parent_tbl.tableoid, parent_tbl.ctid, (NULL::record)
8306         ->  Append
8307               ->  Seq Scan on public.parent_tbl parent_tbl_1
8308                     Output: parent_tbl_1.b, parent_tbl_1.tableoid, parent_tbl_1.ctid, NULL::record
8309               ->  Foreign Scan on public.foreign_tbl parent_tbl_2
8310                     Output: parent_tbl_2.b, parent_tbl_2.tableoid, parent_tbl_2.ctid, parent_tbl_2.*
8311                     Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, ctid FROM public.local_tbl FOR UPDATE
8312(12 rows)
8313
8314UPDATE parent_tbl SET b = b + 1;
8315ERROR:  cannot collect transition tuples from child foreign tables
8316EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
8317DELETE FROM parent_tbl;
8318                               QUERY PLAN                               
8319------------------------------------------------------------------------
8320 Delete on public.parent_tbl
8321   Delete on public.parent_tbl parent_tbl_1
8322   Foreign Delete on public.foreign_tbl parent_tbl_2
8323     Remote SQL: DELETE FROM public.local_tbl WHERE ctid = $1
8324   ->  Append
8325         ->  Seq Scan on public.parent_tbl parent_tbl_1
8326               Output: parent_tbl_1.tableoid, parent_tbl_1.ctid
8327         ->  Foreign Scan on public.foreign_tbl parent_tbl_2
8328               Output: parent_tbl_2.tableoid, parent_tbl_2.ctid
8329               Remote SQL: SELECT ctid FROM public.local_tbl FOR UPDATE
8330(10 rows)
8331
8332DELETE FROM parent_tbl;
8333ERROR:  cannot collect transition tuples from child foreign tables
8334ALTER FOREIGN TABLE foreign_tbl NO INHERIT parent_tbl;
8335DROP TABLE parent_tbl;
8336-- Cleanup
8337DROP FOREIGN TABLE foreign_tbl;
8338DROP TABLE local_tbl;
8339-- ===================================================================
8340-- test inheritance features
8341-- ===================================================================
8342CREATE TABLE a (aa TEXT);
8343CREATE TABLE loct (aa TEXT, bb TEXT);
8344ALTER TABLE a SET (autovacuum_enabled = 'false');
8345ALTER TABLE loct SET (autovacuum_enabled = 'false');
8346CREATE FOREIGN TABLE b (bb TEXT) INHERITS (a)
8347  SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'loct');
8348INSERT INTO a(aa) VALUES('aaa');
8349INSERT INTO a(aa) VALUES('aaaa');
8350INSERT INTO a(aa) VALUES('aaaaa');
8351INSERT INTO b(aa) VALUES('bbb');
8352INSERT INTO b(aa) VALUES('bbbb');
8353INSERT INTO b(aa) VALUES('bbbbb');
8354SELECT tableoid::regclass, * FROM a;
8355 tableoid |  aa   
8356----------+-------
8357 a        | aaa
8358 a        | aaaa
8359 a        | aaaaa
8360 b        | bbb
8361 b        | bbbb
8362 b        | bbbbb
8363(6 rows)
8364
8365SELECT tableoid::regclass, * FROM b;
8366 tableoid |  aa   | bb 
8367----------+-------+----
8368 b        | bbb   | 
8369 b        | bbbb  | 
8370 b        | bbbbb | 
8371(3 rows)
8372
8373SELECT tableoid::regclass, * FROM ONLY a;
8374 tableoid |  aa   
8375----------+-------
8376 a        | aaa
8377 a        | aaaa
8378 a        | aaaaa
8379(3 rows)
8380
8381UPDATE a SET aa = 'zzzzzz' WHERE aa LIKE 'aaaa%';
8382SELECT tableoid::regclass, * FROM a;
8383 tableoid |   aa   
8384----------+--------
8385 a        | aaa
8386 a        | zzzzzz
8387 a        | zzzzzz
8388 b        | bbb
8389 b        | bbbb
8390 b        | bbbbb
8391(6 rows)
8392
8393SELECT tableoid::regclass, * FROM b;
8394 tableoid |  aa   | bb 
8395----------+-------+----
8396 b        | bbb   | 
8397 b        | bbbb  | 
8398 b        | bbbbb | 
8399(3 rows)
8400
8401SELECT tableoid::regclass, * FROM ONLY a;
8402 tableoid |   aa   
8403----------+--------
8404 a        | aaa
8405 a        | zzzzzz
8406 a        | zzzzzz
8407(3 rows)
8408
8409UPDATE b SET aa = 'new';
8410SELECT tableoid::regclass, * FROM a;
8411 tableoid |   aa   
8412----------+--------
8413 a        | aaa
8414 a        | zzzzzz
8415 a        | zzzzzz
8416 b        | new
8417 b        | new
8418 b        | new
8419(6 rows)
8420
8421SELECT tableoid::regclass, * FROM b;
8422 tableoid | aa  | bb 
8423----------+-----+----
8424 b        | new | 
8425 b        | new | 
8426 b        | new | 
8427(3 rows)
8428
8429SELECT tableoid::regclass, * FROM ONLY a;
8430 tableoid |   aa   
8431----------+--------
8432 a        | aaa
8433 a        | zzzzzz
8434 a        | zzzzzz
8435(3 rows)
8436
8437UPDATE a SET aa = 'newtoo';
8438SELECT tableoid::regclass, * FROM a;
8439 tableoid |   aa   
8440----------+--------
8441 a        | newtoo
8442 a        | newtoo
8443 a        | newtoo
8444 b        | newtoo
8445 b        | newtoo
8446 b        | newtoo
8447(6 rows)
8448
8449SELECT tableoid::regclass, * FROM b;
8450 tableoid |   aa   | bb 
8451----------+--------+----
8452 b        | newtoo | 
8453 b        | newtoo | 
8454 b        | newtoo | 
8455(3 rows)
8456
8457SELECT tableoid::regclass, * FROM ONLY a;
8458 tableoid |   aa   
8459----------+--------
8460 a        | newtoo
8461 a        | newtoo
8462 a        | newtoo
8463(3 rows)
8464
8465DELETE FROM a;
8466SELECT tableoid::regclass, * FROM a;
8467 tableoid | aa 
8468----------+----
8469(0 rows)
8470
8471SELECT tableoid::regclass, * FROM b;
8472 tableoid | aa | bb 
8473----------+----+----
8474(0 rows)
8475
8476SELECT tableoid::regclass, * FROM ONLY a;
8477 tableoid | aa 
8478----------+----
8479(0 rows)
8480
8481DROP TABLE a CASCADE;
8482NOTICE:  drop cascades to foreign table b
8483DROP TABLE loct;
8484-- Check SELECT FOR UPDATE/SHARE with an inherited source table
8485create table loct1 (f1 int, f2 int, f3 int);
8486create table loct2 (f1 int, f2 int, f3 int);
8487alter table loct1 set (autovacuum_enabled = 'false');
8488alter table loct2 set (autovacuum_enabled = 'false');
8489create table foo (f1 int, f2 int);
8490create foreign table foo2 (f3 int) inherits (foo)
8491  server loopback options (table_name 'loct1');
8492create table bar (f1 int, f2 int);
8493create foreign table bar2 (f3 int) inherits (bar)
8494  server loopback options (table_name 'loct2');
8495alter table foo set (autovacuum_enabled = 'false');
8496alter table bar set (autovacuum_enabled = 'false');
8497insert into foo values(1,1);
8498insert into foo values(3,3);
8499insert into foo2 values(2,2,2);
8500insert into foo2 values(4,4,4);
8501insert into bar values(1,11);
8502insert into bar values(2,22);
8503insert into bar values(6,66);
8504insert into bar2 values(3,33,33);
8505insert into bar2 values(4,44,44);
8506insert into bar2 values(7,77,77);
8507explain (verbose, costs off)
8508select * from bar where f1 in (select f1 from foo) for update;
8509                                          QUERY PLAN                                          
8510----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8511 LockRows
8512   Output: bar.f1, bar.f2, bar.ctid, foo.ctid, bar.*, bar.tableoid, foo.*, foo.tableoid
8513   ->  Hash Join
8514         Output: bar.f1, bar.f2, bar.ctid, foo.ctid, bar.*, bar.tableoid, foo.*, foo.tableoid
8515         Inner Unique: true
8516         Hash Cond: (bar.f1 = foo.f1)
8517         ->  Append
8518               ->  Seq Scan on public.bar bar_1
8519                     Output: bar_1.f1, bar_1.f2, bar_1.ctid, bar_1.*, bar_1.tableoid
8520               ->  Foreign Scan on public.bar2 bar_2
8521                     Output: bar_2.f1, bar_2.f2, bar_2.ctid, bar_2.*, bar_2.tableoid
8522                     Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3, ctid FROM public.loct2 FOR UPDATE
8523         ->  Hash
8524               Output: foo.ctid, foo.f1, foo.*, foo.tableoid
8525               ->  HashAggregate
8526                     Output: foo.ctid, foo.f1, foo.*, foo.tableoid
8527                     Group Key: foo.f1
8528                     ->  Append
8529                           ->  Seq Scan on public.foo foo_1
8530                                 Output: foo_1.ctid, foo_1.f1, foo_1.*, foo_1.tableoid
8531                           ->  Foreign Scan on public.foo2 foo_2
8532                                 Output: foo_2.ctid, foo_2.f1, foo_2.*, foo_2.tableoid
8533                                 Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3, ctid FROM public.loct1
8534(23 rows)
8535
8536select * from bar where f1 in (select f1 from foo) for update;
8537 f1 | f2 
8538----+----
8539  1 | 11
8540  2 | 22
8541  3 | 33
8542  4 | 44
8543(4 rows)
8544
8545explain (verbose, costs off)
8546select * from bar where f1 in (select f1 from foo) for share;
8547                                          QUERY PLAN                                          
8548----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8549 LockRows
8550   Output: bar.f1, bar.f2, bar.ctid, foo.ctid, bar.*, bar.tableoid, foo.*, foo.tableoid
8551   ->  Hash Join
8552         Output: bar.f1, bar.f2, bar.ctid, foo.ctid, bar.*, bar.tableoid, foo.*, foo.tableoid
8553         Inner Unique: true
8554         Hash Cond: (bar.f1 = foo.f1)
8555         ->  Append
8556               ->  Seq Scan on public.bar bar_1
8557                     Output: bar_1.f1, bar_1.f2, bar_1.ctid, bar_1.*, bar_1.tableoid
8558               ->  Foreign Scan on public.bar2 bar_2
8559                     Output: bar_2.f1, bar_2.f2, bar_2.ctid, bar_2.*, bar_2.tableoid
8560                     Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3, ctid FROM public.loct2 FOR SHARE
8561         ->  Hash
8562               Output: foo.ctid, foo.f1, foo.*, foo.tableoid
8563               ->  HashAggregate
8564                     Output: foo.ctid, foo.f1, foo.*, foo.tableoid
8565                     Group Key: foo.f1
8566                     ->  Append
8567                           ->  Seq Scan on public.foo foo_1
8568                                 Output: foo_1.ctid, foo_1.f1, foo_1.*, foo_1.tableoid
8569                           ->  Foreign Scan on public.foo2 foo_2
8570                                 Output: foo_2.ctid, foo_2.f1, foo_2.*, foo_2.tableoid
8571                                 Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3, ctid FROM public.loct1
8572(23 rows)
8573
8574select * from bar where f1 in (select f1 from foo) for share;
8575 f1 | f2 
8576----+----
8577  1 | 11
8578  2 | 22
8579  3 | 33
8580  4 | 44
8581(4 rows)
8582
8583-- Now check SELECT FOR UPDATE/SHARE with an inherited source table,
8584-- where the parent is itself a foreign table
8585create table loct4 (f1 int, f2 int, f3 int);
8586create foreign table foo2child (f3 int) inherits (foo2)
8587  server loopback options (table_name 'loct4');
8588NOTICE:  moving and merging column "f3" with inherited definition
8589DETAIL:  User-specified column moved to the position of the inherited column.
8590explain (verbose, costs off)
8591select * from bar where f1 in (select f1 from foo2) for share;
8592                                      QUERY PLAN                                      
8593--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8594 LockRows
8595   Output: bar.f1, bar.f2, bar.ctid, foo2.*, bar.*, bar.tableoid, foo2.tableoid
8596   ->  Hash Join
8597         Output: bar.f1, bar.f2, bar.ctid, foo2.*, bar.*, bar.tableoid, foo2.tableoid
8598         Inner Unique: true
8599         Hash Cond: (bar.f1 = foo2.f1)
8600         ->  Append
8601               ->  Seq Scan on public.bar bar_1
8602                     Output: bar_1.f1, bar_1.f2, bar_1.ctid, bar_1.*, bar_1.tableoid
8603               ->  Foreign Scan on public.bar2 bar_2
8604                     Output: bar_2.f1, bar_2.f2, bar_2.ctid, bar_2.*, bar_2.tableoid
8605                     Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3, ctid FROM public.loct2 FOR SHARE
8606         ->  Hash
8607               Output: foo2.*, foo2.f1, foo2.tableoid
8608               ->  HashAggregate
8609                     Output: foo2.*, foo2.f1, foo2.tableoid
8610                     Group Key: foo2.f1
8611                     ->  Append
8612                           ->  Foreign Scan on public.foo2 foo2_1
8613                                 Output: foo2_1.*, foo2_1.f1, foo2_1.tableoid
8614                                 Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3 FROM public.loct1
8615                           ->  Foreign Scan on public.foo2child foo2_2
8616                                 Output: foo2_2.*, foo2_2.f1, foo2_2.tableoid
8617                                 Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3 FROM public.loct4
8618(24 rows)
8619
8620select * from bar where f1 in (select f1 from foo2) for share;
8621 f1 | f2 
8622----+----
8623  2 | 22
8624  4 | 44
8625(2 rows)
8626
8627drop foreign table foo2child;
8628-- And with a local child relation of the foreign table parent
8629create table foo2child (f3 int) inherits (foo2);
8630NOTICE:  moving and merging column "f3" with inherited definition
8631DETAIL:  User-specified column moved to the position of the inherited column.
8632explain (verbose, costs off)
8633select * from bar where f1 in (select f1 from foo2) for share;
8634                                           QUERY PLAN                                            
8635-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8636 LockRows
8637   Output: bar.f1, bar.f2, bar.ctid, foo2.*, bar.*, bar.tableoid, foo2.ctid, foo2.tableoid
8638   ->  Hash Join
8639         Output: bar.f1, bar.f2, bar.ctid, foo2.*, bar.*, bar.tableoid, foo2.ctid, foo2.tableoid
8640         Inner Unique: true
8641         Hash Cond: (bar.f1 = foo2.f1)
8642         ->  Append
8643               ->  Seq Scan on public.bar bar_1
8644                     Output: bar_1.f1, bar_1.f2, bar_1.ctid, bar_1.*, bar_1.tableoid
8645               ->  Foreign Scan on public.bar2 bar_2
8646                     Output: bar_2.f1, bar_2.f2, bar_2.ctid, bar_2.*, bar_2.tableoid
8647                     Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3, ctid FROM public.loct2 FOR SHARE
8648         ->  Hash
8649               Output: foo2.*, foo2.f1, foo2.ctid, foo2.tableoid
8650               ->  HashAggregate
8651                     Output: foo2.*, foo2.f1, foo2.ctid, foo2.tableoid
8652                     Group Key: foo2.f1
8653                     ->  Append
8654                           ->  Foreign Scan on public.foo2 foo2_1
8655                                 Output: foo2_1.*, foo2_1.f1, foo2_1.ctid, foo2_1.tableoid
8656                                 Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3, ctid FROM public.loct1
8657                           ->  Seq Scan on public.foo2child foo2_2
8658                                 Output: foo2_2.*, foo2_2.f1, foo2_2.ctid, foo2_2.tableoid
8659(23 rows)
8660
8661select * from bar where f1 in (select f1 from foo2) for share;
8662 f1 | f2 
8663----+----
8664  2 | 22
8665  4 | 44
8666(2 rows)
8667
8668drop table foo2child;
8669-- Check UPDATE with inherited target and an inherited source table
8670explain (verbose, costs off)
8671update bar set f2 = f2 + 100 where f1 in (select f1 from foo);
8672                                              QUERY PLAN                                               
8673-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8674 Update on public.bar
8675   Update on public.bar bar_1
8676   Foreign Update on public.bar2 bar_2
8677     Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loct2 SET f2 = $2 WHERE ctid = $1
8678   ->  Hash Join
8679         Output: (bar.f2 + 100), foo.ctid, bar.tableoid, bar.ctid, (NULL::record), foo.*, foo.tableoid
8680         Inner Unique: true
8681         Hash Cond: (bar.f1 = foo.f1)
8682         ->  Append
8683               ->  Seq Scan on public.bar bar_1
8684                     Output: bar_1.f2, bar_1.f1, bar_1.tableoid, bar_1.ctid, NULL::record
8685               ->  Foreign Scan on public.bar2 bar_2
8686                     Output: bar_2.f2, bar_2.f1, bar_2.tableoid, bar_2.ctid, bar_2.*
8687                     Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3, ctid FROM public.loct2 FOR UPDATE
8688         ->  Hash
8689               Output: foo.ctid, foo.f1, foo.*, foo.tableoid
8690               ->  HashAggregate
8691                     Output: foo.ctid, foo.f1, foo.*, foo.tableoid
8692                     Group Key: foo.f1
8693                     ->  Append
8694                           ->  Seq Scan on public.foo foo_1
8695                                 Output: foo_1.ctid, foo_1.f1, foo_1.*, foo_1.tableoid
8696                           ->  Foreign Scan on public.foo2 foo_2
8697                                 Output: foo_2.ctid, foo_2.f1, foo_2.*, foo_2.tableoid
8698                                 Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3, ctid FROM public.loct1
8699(25 rows)
8700
8701update bar set f2 = f2 + 100 where f1 in (select f1 from foo);
8702select tableoid::regclass, * from bar order by 1,2;
8703 tableoid | f1 | f2  
8704----------+----+-----
8705 bar      |  1 | 111
8706 bar      |  2 | 122
8707 bar      |  6 |  66
8708 bar2     |  3 | 133
8709 bar2     |  4 | 144
8710 bar2     |  7 |  77
8711(6 rows)
8712
8713-- Check UPDATE with inherited target and an appendrel subquery
8714explain (verbose, costs off)
8715update bar set f2 = f2 + 100
8716from
8717  ( select f1 from foo union all select f1+3 from foo ) ss
8718where bar.f1 = ss.f1;
8719                                           QUERY PLAN                                           
8720------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8721 Update on public.bar
8722   Update on public.bar bar_1
8723   Foreign Update on public.bar2 bar_2
8724     Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loct2 SET f2 = $2 WHERE ctid = $1
8725   ->  Merge Join
8726         Output: (bar.f2 + 100), (ROW(foo.f1)), bar.tableoid, bar.ctid, (NULL::record)
8727         Merge Cond: (bar.f1 = foo.f1)
8728         ->  Sort
8729               Output: bar.f2, bar.f1, bar.tableoid, bar.ctid, (NULL::record)
8730               Sort Key: bar.f1
8731               ->  Append
8732                     ->  Seq Scan on public.bar bar_1
8733                           Output: bar_1.f2, bar_1.f1, bar_1.tableoid, bar_1.ctid, NULL::record
8734                     ->  Foreign Scan on public.bar2 bar_2
8735                           Output: bar_2.f2, bar_2.f1, bar_2.tableoid, bar_2.ctid, bar_2.*
8736                           Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3, ctid FROM public.loct2 FOR UPDATE
8737         ->  Sort
8738               Output: (ROW(foo.f1)), foo.f1
8739               Sort Key: foo.f1
8740               ->  Append
8741                     ->  Seq Scan on public.foo
8742                           Output: ROW(foo.f1), foo.f1
8743                     ->  Foreign Scan on public.foo2 foo_1
8744                           Output: ROW(foo_1.f1), foo_1.f1
8745                           Remote SQL: SELECT f1 FROM public.loct1
8746                     ->  Seq Scan on public.foo foo_2
8747                           Output: ROW((foo_2.f1 + 3)), (foo_2.f1 + 3)
8748                     ->  Foreign Scan on public.foo2 foo_3
8749                           Output: ROW((foo_3.f1 + 3)), (foo_3.f1 + 3)
8750                           Remote SQL: SELECT f1 FROM public.loct1
8751(30 rows)
8752
8753update bar set f2 = f2 + 100
8754from
8755  ( select f1 from foo union all select f1+3 from foo ) ss
8756where bar.f1 = ss.f1;
8757select tableoid::regclass, * from bar order by 1,2;
8758 tableoid | f1 | f2  
8759----------+----+-----
8760 bar      |  1 | 211
8761 bar      |  2 | 222
8762 bar      |  6 | 166
8763 bar2     |  3 | 233
8764 bar2     |  4 | 244
8765 bar2     |  7 | 177
8766(6 rows)
8767
8768-- Test forcing the remote server to produce sorted data for a merge join,
8769-- but the foreign table is an inheritance child.
8770truncate table loct1;
8771truncate table only foo;
8772\set num_rows_foo 2000
8773insert into loct1 select generate_series(0, :num_rows_foo, 2), generate_series(0, :num_rows_foo, 2), generate_series(0, :num_rows_foo, 2);
8774insert into foo select generate_series(1, :num_rows_foo, 2), generate_series(1, :num_rows_foo, 2);
8775SET enable_hashjoin to false;
8776SET enable_nestloop to false;
8777alter foreign table foo2 options (use_remote_estimate 'true');
8778create index i_loct1_f1 on loct1(f1);
8779create index i_foo_f1 on foo(f1);
8780analyze foo;
8781analyze loct1;
8782-- inner join; expressions in the clauses appear in the equivalence class list
8783explain (verbose, costs off)
8784	select foo.f1, loct1.f1 from foo join loct1 on (foo.f1 = loct1.f1) order by foo.f2 offset 10 limit 10;
8785                                            QUERY PLAN                                            
8786--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8787 Limit
8788   Output: foo.f1, loct1.f1, foo.f2
8789   ->  Sort
8790         Output: foo.f1, loct1.f1, foo.f2
8791         Sort Key: foo.f2
8792         ->  Merge Join
8793               Output: foo.f1, loct1.f1, foo.f2
8794               Merge Cond: (foo.f1 = loct1.f1)
8795               ->  Merge Append
8796                     Sort Key: foo.f1
8797                     ->  Index Scan using i_foo_f1 on public.foo foo_1
8798                           Output: foo_1.f1, foo_1.f2
8799                     ->  Foreign Scan on public.foo2 foo_2
8800                           Output: foo_2.f1, foo_2.f2
8801                           Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2 FROM public.loct1 ORDER BY f1 ASC NULLS LAST
8802               ->  Index Only Scan using i_loct1_f1 on public.loct1
8803                     Output: loct1.f1
8804(17 rows)
8805
8806select foo.f1, loct1.f1 from foo join loct1 on (foo.f1 = loct1.f1) order by foo.f2 offset 10 limit 10;
8807 f1 | f1 
8808----+----
8809 20 | 20
8810 22 | 22
8811 24 | 24
8812 26 | 26
8813 28 | 28
8814 30 | 30
8815 32 | 32
8816 34 | 34
8817 36 | 36
8818 38 | 38
8819(10 rows)
8820
8821-- outer join; expressions in the clauses do not appear in equivalence class
8822-- list but no output change as compared to the previous query
8823explain (verbose, costs off)
8824	select foo.f1, loct1.f1 from foo left join loct1 on (foo.f1 = loct1.f1) order by foo.f2 offset 10 limit 10;
8825                                            QUERY PLAN                                            
8826--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8827 Limit
8828   Output: foo.f1, loct1.f1, foo.f2
8829   ->  Sort
8830         Output: foo.f1, loct1.f1, foo.f2
8831         Sort Key: foo.f2
8832         ->  Merge Left Join
8833               Output: foo.f1, loct1.f1, foo.f2
8834               Merge Cond: (foo.f1 = loct1.f1)
8835               ->  Merge Append
8836                     Sort Key: foo.f1
8837                     ->  Index Scan using i_foo_f1 on public.foo foo_1
8838                           Output: foo_1.f1, foo_1.f2
8839                     ->  Foreign Scan on public.foo2 foo_2
8840                           Output: foo_2.f1, foo_2.f2
8841                           Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2 FROM public.loct1 ORDER BY f1 ASC NULLS LAST
8842               ->  Index Only Scan using i_loct1_f1 on public.loct1
8843                     Output: loct1.f1
8844(17 rows)
8845
8846select foo.f1, loct1.f1 from foo left join loct1 on (foo.f1 = loct1.f1) order by foo.f2 offset 10 limit 10;
8847 f1 | f1 
8848----+----
8849 10 | 10
8850 11 |   
8851 12 | 12
8852 13 |   
8853 14 | 14
8854 15 |   
8855 16 | 16
8856 17 |   
8857 18 | 18
8858 19 |   
8859(10 rows)
8860
8861RESET enable_hashjoin;
8862RESET enable_nestloop;
8863-- Test that WHERE CURRENT OF is not supported
8864begin;
8865declare c cursor for select * from bar where f1 = 7;
8866fetch from c;
8867 f1 | f2  
8868----+-----
8869  7 | 177
8870(1 row)
8871
8872update bar set f2 = null where current of c;
8873ERROR:  WHERE CURRENT OF is not supported for this table type
8874rollback;
8875explain (verbose, costs off)
8876delete from foo where f1 < 5 returning *;
8877                                      QUERY PLAN                                      
8878--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8879 Delete on public.foo
8880   Output: foo_1.f1, foo_1.f2
8881   Delete on public.foo foo_1
8882   Foreign Delete on public.foo2 foo_2
8883   ->  Append
8884         ->  Index Scan using i_foo_f1 on public.foo foo_1
8885               Output: foo_1.tableoid, foo_1.ctid
8886               Index Cond: (foo_1.f1 < 5)
8887         ->  Foreign Delete on public.foo2 foo_2
8888               Remote SQL: DELETE FROM public.loct1 WHERE ((f1 < 5)) RETURNING f1, f2
8889(10 rows)
8890
8891delete from foo where f1 < 5 returning *;
8892 f1 | f2 
8893----+----
8894  1 |  1
8895  3 |  3
8896  0 |  0
8897  2 |  2
8898  4 |  4
8899(5 rows)
8900
8901explain (verbose, costs off)
8902update bar set f2 = f2 + 100 returning *;
8903                                        QUERY PLAN                                        
8904------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8905 Update on public.bar
8906   Output: bar_1.f1, bar_1.f2
8907   Update on public.bar bar_1
8908   Foreign Update on public.bar2 bar_2
8909   ->  Result
8910         Output: (bar.f2 + 100), bar.tableoid, bar.ctid, (NULL::record)
8911         ->  Append
8912               ->  Seq Scan on public.bar bar_1
8913                     Output: bar_1.f2, bar_1.tableoid, bar_1.ctid, NULL::record
8914               ->  Foreign Update on public.bar2 bar_2
8915                     Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loct2 SET f2 = (f2 + 100) RETURNING f1, f2
8916(11 rows)
8917
8918update bar set f2 = f2 + 100 returning *;
8919 f1 | f2  
8920----+-----
8921  1 | 311
8922  2 | 322
8923  6 | 266
8924  3 | 333
8925  4 | 344
8926  7 | 277
8927(6 rows)
8928
8929-- Test that UPDATE/DELETE with inherited target works with row-level triggers
8930CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_before
8931BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE ON bar2
8932FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
8933CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_after
8934AFTER UPDATE OR DELETE ON bar2
8935FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
8936explain (verbose, costs off)
8937update bar set f2 = f2 + 100;
8938                                               QUERY PLAN                                               
8939--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8940 Update on public.bar
8941   Update on public.bar bar_1
8942   Foreign Update on public.bar2 bar_2
8943     Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loct2 SET f1 = $2, f2 = $3, f3 = $4 WHERE ctid = $1 RETURNING f1, f2, f3
8944   ->  Result
8945         Output: (bar.f2 + 100), bar.tableoid, bar.ctid, (NULL::record)
8946         ->  Append
8947               ->  Seq Scan on public.bar bar_1
8948                     Output: bar_1.f2, bar_1.tableoid, bar_1.ctid, NULL::record
8949               ->  Foreign Scan on public.bar2 bar_2
8950                     Output: bar_2.f2, bar_2.tableoid, bar_2.ctid, bar_2.*
8951                     Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3, ctid FROM public.loct2 FOR UPDATE
8952(12 rows)
8953
8954update bar set f2 = f2 + 100;
8955NOTICE:  trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW UPDATE ON bar2
8956NOTICE:  OLD: (3,333,33),NEW: (3,433,33)
8957NOTICE:  trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW UPDATE ON bar2
8958NOTICE:  OLD: (4,344,44),NEW: (4,444,44)
8959NOTICE:  trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW UPDATE ON bar2
8960NOTICE:  OLD: (7,277,77),NEW: (7,377,77)
8961NOTICE:  trig_row_after(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW UPDATE ON bar2
8962NOTICE:  OLD: (3,333,33),NEW: (3,433,33)
8963NOTICE:  trig_row_after(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW UPDATE ON bar2
8964NOTICE:  OLD: (4,344,44),NEW: (4,444,44)
8965NOTICE:  trig_row_after(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW UPDATE ON bar2
8966NOTICE:  OLD: (7,277,77),NEW: (7,377,77)
8967explain (verbose, costs off)
8968delete from bar where f2 < 400;
8969                                            QUERY PLAN                                             
8970---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8971 Delete on public.bar
8972   Delete on public.bar bar_1
8973   Foreign Delete on public.bar2 bar_2
8974     Remote SQL: DELETE FROM public.loct2 WHERE ctid = $1 RETURNING f1, f2, f3
8975   ->  Append
8976         ->  Seq Scan on public.bar bar_1
8977               Output: bar_1.tableoid, bar_1.ctid, NULL::record
8978               Filter: (bar_1.f2 < 400)
8979         ->  Foreign Scan on public.bar2 bar_2
8980               Output: bar_2.tableoid, bar_2.ctid, bar_2.*
8981               Remote SQL: SELECT f1, f2, f3, ctid FROM public.loct2 WHERE ((f2 < 400)) FOR UPDATE
8982(11 rows)
8983
8984delete from bar where f2 < 400;
8985NOTICE:  trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW DELETE ON bar2
8986NOTICE:  OLD: (7,377,77)
8987NOTICE:  trig_row_after(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW DELETE ON bar2
8988NOTICE:  OLD: (7,377,77)
8989-- cleanup
8990drop table foo cascade;
8991NOTICE:  drop cascades to foreign table foo2
8992drop table bar cascade;
8993NOTICE:  drop cascades to foreign table bar2
8994drop table loct1;
8995drop table loct2;
8996-- Test pushing down UPDATE/DELETE joins to the remote server
8997create table parent (a int, b text);
8998create table loct1 (a int, b text);
8999create table loct2 (a int, b text);
9000create foreign table remt1 (a int, b text)
9001  server loopback options (table_name 'loct1');
9002create foreign table remt2 (a int, b text)
9003  server loopback options (table_name 'loct2');
9004alter foreign table remt1 inherit parent;
9005insert into remt1 values (1, 'foo');
9006insert into remt1 values (2, 'bar');
9007insert into remt2 values (1, 'foo');
9008insert into remt2 values (2, 'bar');
9009analyze remt1;
9010analyze remt2;
9011explain (verbose, costs off)
9012update parent set b = parent.b || remt2.b from remt2 where parent.a = remt2.a returning *;
9013                                                   QUERY PLAN                                                   
9014----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9015 Update on public.parent
9016   Output: parent_1.a, parent_1.b, remt2.a, remt2.b
9017   Update on public.parent parent_1
9018   Foreign Update on public.remt1 parent_2
9019     Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loct1 SET b = $2 WHERE ctid = $1 RETURNING a, b
9020   ->  Nested Loop
9021         Output: (parent.b || remt2.b), remt2.*, remt2.a, remt2.b, parent.tableoid, parent.ctid, (NULL::record)
9022         Join Filter: (parent.a = remt2.a)
9023         ->  Append
9024               ->  Seq Scan on public.parent parent_1
9025                     Output: parent_1.b, parent_1.a, parent_1.tableoid, parent_1.ctid, NULL::record
9026               ->  Foreign Scan on public.remt1 parent_2
9027                     Output: parent_2.b, parent_2.a, parent_2.tableoid, parent_2.ctid, parent_2.*
9028                     Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, ctid FROM public.loct1 FOR UPDATE
9029         ->  Materialize
9030               Output: remt2.b, remt2.*, remt2.a
9031               ->  Foreign Scan on public.remt2
9032                     Output: remt2.b, remt2.*, remt2.a
9033                     Remote SQL: SELECT a, b FROM public.loct2
9034(19 rows)
9035
9036update parent set b = parent.b || remt2.b from remt2 where parent.a = remt2.a returning *;
9037 a |   b    | a |  b  
9038---+--------+---+-----
9039 1 | foofoo | 1 | foo
9040 2 | barbar | 2 | bar
9041(2 rows)
9042
9043explain (verbose, costs off)
9044delete from parent using remt2 where parent.a = remt2.a returning parent;
9045                                 QUERY PLAN                                  
9046-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
9047 Delete on public.parent
9048   Output: parent_1.*
9049   Delete on public.parent parent_1
9050   Foreign Delete on public.remt1 parent_2
9051     Remote SQL: DELETE FROM public.loct1 WHERE ctid = $1 RETURNING a, b
9052   ->  Nested Loop
9053         Output: remt2.*, parent.tableoid, parent.ctid
9054         Join Filter: (parent.a = remt2.a)
9055         ->  Append
9056               ->  Seq Scan on public.parent parent_1
9057                     Output: parent_1.a, parent_1.tableoid, parent_1.ctid
9058               ->  Foreign Scan on public.remt1 parent_2
