9059                     Output: parent_2.a, parent_2.tableoid, parent_2.ctid
9060                     Remote SQL: SELECT a, ctid FROM public.loct1 FOR UPDATE
9061         ->  Materialize
9062               Output: remt2.*, remt2.a
9063               ->  Foreign Scan on public.remt2
9064                     Output: remt2.*, remt2.a
9065                     Remote SQL: SELECT a, b FROM public.loct2
9066(19 rows)
9067
9068delete from parent using remt2 where parent.a = remt2.a returning parent;
9069   parent   
9070------------
9071 (1,foofoo)
9072 (2,barbar)
9073(2 rows)
9074
9075-- cleanup
9076drop foreign table remt1;
9077drop foreign table remt2;
9078drop table loct1;
9079drop table loct2;
9080drop table parent;
9081-- ===================================================================
9082-- test tuple routing for foreign-table partitions
9083-- ===================================================================
9084-- Test insert tuple routing
9085create table itrtest (a int, b text) partition by list (a);
9086create table loct1 (a int check (a in (1)), b text);
9087create foreign table remp1 (a int check (a in (1)), b text) server loopback options (table_name 'loct1');
9088create table loct2 (a int check (a in (2)), b text);
9089create foreign table remp2 (b text, a int check (a in (2))) server loopback options (table_name 'loct2');
9090alter table itrtest attach partition remp1 for values in (1);
9091alter table itrtest attach partition remp2 for values in (2);
9092insert into itrtest values (1, 'foo');
9093insert into itrtest values (1, 'bar') returning *;
9094 a |  b  
9095---+-----
9096 1 | bar
9097(1 row)
9098
9099insert into itrtest values (2, 'baz');
9100insert into itrtest values (2, 'qux') returning *;
9101 a |  b  
9102---+-----
9103 2 | qux
9104(1 row)
9105
9106insert into itrtest values (1, 'test1'), (2, 'test2') returning *;
9107 a |   b   
9108---+-------
9109 1 | test1
9110 2 | test2
9111(2 rows)
9112
9113select tableoid::regclass, * FROM itrtest;
9114 tableoid | a |   b   
9115----------+---+-------
9116 remp1    | 1 | foo
9117 remp1    | 1 | bar
9118 remp1    | 1 | test1
9119 remp2    | 2 | baz
9120 remp2    | 2 | qux
9121 remp2    | 2 | test2
9122(6 rows)
9123
9124select tableoid::regclass, * FROM remp1;
9125 tableoid | a |   b   
9126----------+---+-------
9127 remp1    | 1 | foo
9128 remp1    | 1 | bar
9129 remp1    | 1 | test1
9130(3 rows)
9131
9132select tableoid::regclass, * FROM remp2;
9133 tableoid |   b   | a 
9134----------+-------+---
9135 remp2    | baz   | 2
9136 remp2    | qux   | 2
9137 remp2    | test2 | 2
9138(3 rows)
9139
9140delete from itrtest;
9141-- MERGE ought to fail cleanly
9142merge into itrtest using (select 1, 'foo') as source on (true)
9143  when matched then do nothing;
9144ERROR:  cannot execute MERGE on relation "remp1"
9145DETAIL:  This operation is not supported for foreign tables.
9146create unique index loct1_idx on loct1 (a);
9147-- DO NOTHING without an inference specification is supported
9148insert into itrtest values (1, 'foo') on conflict do nothing returning *;
9149 a |  b  
9150---+-----
9151 1 | foo
9152(1 row)
9153
9154insert into itrtest values (1, 'foo') on conflict do nothing returning *;
9155 a | b 
9156---+---
9157(0 rows)
9158
9159-- But other cases are not supported
9160insert into itrtest values (1, 'bar') on conflict (a) do nothing;
9161ERROR:  there is no unique or exclusion constraint matching the ON CONFLICT specification
9162insert into itrtest values (1, 'bar') on conflict (a) do update set b = excluded.b;
9163ERROR:  there is no unique or exclusion constraint matching the ON CONFLICT specification
9164select tableoid::regclass, * FROM itrtest;
9165 tableoid | a |  b  
9166----------+---+-----
9167 remp1    | 1 | foo
9168(1 row)
9169
9170delete from itrtest;
9171drop index loct1_idx;
9172-- Test that remote triggers work with insert tuple routing
9173create function br_insert_trigfunc() returns trigger as $$
9174begin
9175	new.b := new.b || ' triggered !';
9176	return new;
9177end
9178$$ language plpgsql;
9179create trigger loct1_br_insert_trigger before insert on loct1
9180	for each row execute procedure br_insert_trigfunc();
9181create trigger loct2_br_insert_trigger before insert on loct2
9182	for each row execute procedure br_insert_trigfunc();
9183-- The new values are concatenated with ' triggered !'
9184insert into itrtest values (1, 'foo') returning *;
9185 a |        b        
9186---+-----------------
9187 1 | foo triggered !
9188(1 row)
9189
9190insert into itrtest values (2, 'qux') returning *;
9191 a |        b        
9192---+-----------------
9193 2 | qux triggered !
9194(1 row)
9195
9196insert into itrtest values (1, 'test1'), (2, 'test2') returning *;
9197 a |         b         
9198---+-------------------
9199 1 | test1 triggered !
9200 2 | test2 triggered !
9201(2 rows)
9202
9203with result as (insert into itrtest values (1, 'test1'), (2, 'test2') returning *) select * from result;
9204 a |         b         
9205---+-------------------
9206 1 | test1 triggered !
9207 2 | test2 triggered !
9208(2 rows)
9209
9210drop trigger loct1_br_insert_trigger on loct1;
9211drop trigger loct2_br_insert_trigger on loct2;
9212drop table itrtest;
9213drop table loct1;
9214drop table loct2;
9215-- Test update tuple routing
9216create table utrtest (a int, b text) partition by list (a);
9217create table loct (a int check (a in (1)), b text);
9218create foreign table remp (a int check (a in (1)), b text) server loopback options (table_name 'loct');
9219create table locp (a int check (a in (2)), b text);
9220alter table utrtest attach partition remp for values in (1);
9221alter table utrtest attach partition locp for values in (2);
9222insert into utrtest values (1, 'foo');
9223insert into utrtest values (2, 'qux');
9224select tableoid::regclass, * FROM utrtest;
9225 tableoid | a |  b  
9226----------+---+-----
9227 remp     | 1 | foo
9228 locp     | 2 | qux
9229(2 rows)
9230
9231select tableoid::regclass, * FROM remp;
9232 tableoid | a |  b  
9233----------+---+-----
9234 remp     | 1 | foo
9235(1 row)
9236
9237select tableoid::regclass, * FROM locp;
9238 tableoid | a |  b  
9239----------+---+-----
9240 locp     | 2 | qux
9241(1 row)
9242
9243-- It's not allowed to move a row from a partition that is foreign to another
9244update utrtest set a = 2 where b = 'foo' returning *;
9245ERROR:  new row for relation "loct" violates check constraint "loct_a_check"
9246DETAIL:  Failing row contains (2, foo).
9247CONTEXT:  remote SQL command: UPDATE public.loct SET a = 2 WHERE ((b = 'foo')) RETURNING a, b
9248-- But the reverse is allowed
9249update utrtest set a = 1 where b = 'qux' returning *;
9250ERROR:  cannot route tuples into foreign table to be updated "remp"
9251select tableoid::regclass, * FROM utrtest;
9252 tableoid | a |  b  
9253----------+---+-----
9254 remp     | 1 | foo
9255 locp     | 2 | qux
9256(2 rows)
9257
9258select tableoid::regclass, * FROM remp;
9259 tableoid | a |  b  
9260----------+---+-----
9261 remp     | 1 | foo
9262(1 row)
9263
9264select tableoid::regclass, * FROM locp;
9265 tableoid | a |  b  
9266----------+---+-----
9267 locp     | 2 | qux
9268(1 row)
9269
9270-- The executor should not let unexercised FDWs shut down
9271update utrtest set a = 1 where b = 'foo';
9272-- Test that remote triggers work with update tuple routing
9273create trigger loct_br_insert_trigger before insert on loct
9274	for each row execute procedure br_insert_trigfunc();
9275delete from utrtest;
9276insert into utrtest values (2, 'qux');
9277-- Check case where the foreign partition is a subplan target rel
9278explain (verbose, costs off)
9279update utrtest set a = 1 where a = 1 or a = 2 returning *;
9280                                             QUERY PLAN                                             
9281----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9282 Update on public.utrtest
9283   Output: utrtest_1.a, utrtest_1.b
9284   Foreign Update on public.remp utrtest_1
9285   Update on public.locp utrtest_2
9286   ->  Append
9287         ->  Foreign Update on public.remp utrtest_1
9288               Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loct SET a = 1 WHERE (((a = 1) OR (a = 2))) RETURNING a, b
9289         ->  Seq Scan on public.locp utrtest_2
9290               Output: 1, utrtest_2.tableoid, utrtest_2.ctid, NULL::record
9291               Filter: ((utrtest_2.a = 1) OR (utrtest_2.a = 2))
9292(10 rows)
9293
9294-- The new values are concatenated with ' triggered !'
9295update utrtest set a = 1 where a = 1 or a = 2 returning *;
9296ERROR:  cannot route tuples into foreign table to be updated "remp"
9297delete from utrtest;
9298insert into utrtest values (2, 'qux');
9299-- Check case where the foreign partition isn't a subplan target rel
9300explain (verbose, costs off)
9301update utrtest set a = 1 where a = 2 returning *;
9302                      QUERY PLAN                       
9303-------------------------------------------------------
9304 Update on public.utrtest
9305   Output: utrtest_1.a, utrtest_1.b
9306   Update on public.locp utrtest_1
9307   ->  Seq Scan on public.locp utrtest_1
9308         Output: 1, utrtest_1.tableoid, utrtest_1.ctid
9309         Filter: (utrtest_1.a = 2)
9310(6 rows)
9311
9312-- The new values are concatenated with ' triggered !'
9313update utrtest set a = 1 where a = 2 returning *;
9314 a |        b        
9315---+-----------------
9316 1 | qux triggered !
9317(1 row)
9318
9319drop trigger loct_br_insert_trigger on loct;
9320-- We can move rows to a foreign partition that has been updated already,
9321-- but can't move rows to a foreign partition that hasn't been updated yet
9322delete from utrtest;
9323insert into utrtest values (1, 'foo');
9324insert into utrtest values (2, 'qux');
9325-- Test the former case:
9326-- with a direct modification plan
9327explain (verbose, costs off)
9328update utrtest set a = 1 returning *;
9329                                QUERY PLAN                                 
9330---------------------------------------------------------------------------
9331 Update on public.utrtest
9332   Output: utrtest_1.a, utrtest_1.b
9333   Foreign Update on public.remp utrtest_1
9334   Update on public.locp utrtest_2
9335   ->  Append
9336         ->  Foreign Update on public.remp utrtest_1
9337               Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loct SET a = 1 RETURNING a, b
9338         ->  Seq Scan on public.locp utrtest_2
9339               Output: 1, utrtest_2.tableoid, utrtest_2.ctid, NULL::record
9340(9 rows)
9341
9342update utrtest set a = 1 returning *;
9343ERROR:  cannot route tuples into foreign table to be updated "remp"
9344delete from utrtest;
9345insert into utrtest values (1, 'foo');
9346insert into utrtest values (2, 'qux');
9347-- with a non-direct modification plan
9348explain (verbose, costs off)
9349update utrtest set a = 1 from (values (1), (2)) s(x) where a = s.x returning *;
9350                                           QUERY PLAN                                           
9351------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9352 Update on public.utrtest
9353   Output: utrtest_1.a, utrtest_1.b, "*VALUES*".column1
9354   Foreign Update on public.remp utrtest_1
9355     Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loct SET a = $2 WHERE ctid = $1 RETURNING a, b
9356   Update on public.locp utrtest_2
9357   ->  Hash Join
9358         Output: 1, "*VALUES*".*, "*VALUES*".column1, utrtest.tableoid, utrtest.ctid, utrtest.*
9359         Hash Cond: (utrtest.a = "*VALUES*".column1)
9360         ->  Append
9361               ->  Foreign Scan on public.remp utrtest_1
9362                     Output: utrtest_1.a, utrtest_1.tableoid, utrtest_1.ctid, utrtest_1.*
9363                     Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, ctid FROM public.loct FOR UPDATE
9364               ->  Seq Scan on public.locp utrtest_2
9365                     Output: utrtest_2.a, utrtest_2.tableoid, utrtest_2.ctid, NULL::record
9366         ->  Hash
9367               Output: "*VALUES*".*, "*VALUES*".column1
9368               ->  Values Scan on "*VALUES*"
9369                     Output: "*VALUES*".*, "*VALUES*".column1
9370(18 rows)
9371
9372update utrtest set a = 1 from (values (1), (2)) s(x) where a = s.x returning *;
9373ERROR:  cannot route tuples into foreign table to be updated "remp"
9374-- Change the definition of utrtest so that the foreign partition get updated
9375-- after the local partition
9376delete from utrtest;
9377alter table utrtest detach partition remp;
9378drop foreign table remp;
9379alter table loct drop constraint loct_a_check;
9380alter table loct add check (a in (3));
9381create foreign table remp (a int check (a in (3)), b text) server loopback options (table_name 'loct');
9382alter table utrtest attach partition remp for values in (3);
9383insert into utrtest values (2, 'qux');
9384insert into utrtest values (3, 'xyzzy');
9385-- Test the latter case:
9386-- with a direct modification plan
9387explain (verbose, costs off)
9388update utrtest set a = 3 returning *;
9389                                QUERY PLAN                                 
9390---------------------------------------------------------------------------
9391 Update on public.utrtest
9392   Output: utrtest_1.a, utrtest_1.b
9393   Update on public.locp utrtest_1
9394   Foreign Update on public.remp utrtest_2
9395   ->  Append
9396         ->  Seq Scan on public.locp utrtest_1
9397               Output: 3, utrtest_1.tableoid, utrtest_1.ctid, NULL::record
9398         ->  Foreign Update on public.remp utrtest_2
9399               Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loct SET a = 3 RETURNING a, b
9400(9 rows)
9401
9402update utrtest set a = 3 returning *; -- ERROR
9403ERROR:  cannot route tuples into foreign table to be updated "remp"
9404-- with a non-direct modification plan
9405explain (verbose, costs off)
9406update utrtest set a = 3 from (values (2), (3)) s(x) where a = s.x returning *;
9407                                             QUERY PLAN                                              
9408-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9409 Update on public.utrtest
9410   Output: utrtest_1.a, utrtest_1.b, "*VALUES*".column1
9411   Update on public.locp utrtest_1
9412   Foreign Update on public.remp utrtest_2
9413     Remote SQL: UPDATE public.loct SET a = $2 WHERE ctid = $1 RETURNING a, b
9414   ->  Hash Join
9415         Output: 3, "*VALUES*".*, "*VALUES*".column1, utrtest.tableoid, utrtest.ctid, (NULL::record)
9416         Hash Cond: (utrtest.a = "*VALUES*".column1)
9417         ->  Append
9418               ->  Seq Scan on public.locp utrtest_1
9419                     Output: utrtest_1.a, utrtest_1.tableoid, utrtest_1.ctid, NULL::record
9420               ->  Foreign Scan on public.remp utrtest_2
9421                     Output: utrtest_2.a, utrtest_2.tableoid, utrtest_2.ctid, utrtest_2.*
9422                     Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, ctid FROM public.loct FOR UPDATE
9423         ->  Hash
9424               Output: "*VALUES*".*, "*VALUES*".column1
9425               ->  Values Scan on "*VALUES*"
9426                     Output: "*VALUES*".*, "*VALUES*".column1
9427(18 rows)
9428
9429update utrtest set a = 3 from (values (2), (3)) s(x) where a = s.x returning *; -- ERROR
9430ERROR:  cannot route tuples into foreign table to be updated "remp"
9431drop table utrtest;
9432drop table loct;
9433-- Test copy tuple routing
9434create table ctrtest (a int, b text) partition by list (a);
9435create table loct1 (a int check (a in (1)), b text);
9436create foreign table remp1 (a int check (a in (1)), b text) server loopback options (table_name 'loct1');
9437create table loct2 (a int check (a in (2)), b text);
9438create foreign table remp2 (b text, a int check (a in (2))) server loopback options (table_name 'loct2');
9439alter table ctrtest attach partition remp1 for values in (1);
9440alter table ctrtest attach partition remp2 for values in (2);
9441copy ctrtest from stdin;
9442select tableoid::regclass, * FROM ctrtest;
9443 tableoid | a |  b  
9444----------+---+-----
9445 remp1    | 1 | foo
9446 remp2    | 2 | qux
9447(2 rows)
9448
9449select tableoid::regclass, * FROM remp1;
9450 tableoid | a |  b  
9451----------+---+-----
9452 remp1    | 1 | foo
9453(1 row)
9454
9455select tableoid::regclass, * FROM remp2;
9456 tableoid |  b  | a 
9457----------+-----+---
9458 remp2    | qux | 2
9459(1 row)
9460
9461-- Copying into foreign partitions directly should work as well
9462copy remp1 from stdin;
9463select tableoid::regclass, * FROM remp1;
9464 tableoid | a |  b  
9465----------+---+-----
9466 remp1    | 1 | foo
9467 remp1    | 1 | bar
9468(2 rows)
9469
9470delete from ctrtest;
9471-- Test copy tuple routing with the batch_size option enabled
9472alter server loopback options (add batch_size '2');
9473copy ctrtest from stdin;
9474select tableoid::regclass, * FROM ctrtest;
9475 tableoid | a |   b   
9476----------+---+-------
9477 remp1    | 1 | foo
9478 remp1    | 1 | bar
9479 remp1    | 1 | test1
9480 remp2    | 2 | baz
9481 remp2    | 2 | qux
9482 remp2    | 2 | test2
9483(6 rows)
9484
9485select tableoid::regclass, * FROM remp1;
9486 tableoid | a |   b   
9487----------+---+-------
9488 remp1    | 1 | foo
9489 remp1    | 1 | bar
9490 remp1    | 1 | test1
9491(3 rows)
9492
9493select tableoid::regclass, * FROM remp2;
9494 tableoid |   b   | a 
9495----------+-------+---
9496 remp2    | baz   | 2
9497 remp2    | qux   | 2
9498 remp2    | test2 | 2
9499(3 rows)
9500
9501delete from ctrtest;
9502alter server loopback options (drop batch_size);
9503drop table ctrtest;
9504drop table loct1;
9505drop table loct2;
9506-- ===================================================================
9507-- test COPY FROM
9508-- ===================================================================
9509create table loc2 (f1 int, f2 text);
9510alter table loc2 set (autovacuum_enabled = 'false');
9511create foreign table rem2 (f1 int, f2 text) server loopback options(table_name 'loc2');
9512-- Test basic functionality
9513copy rem2 from stdin;
9514select * from rem2;
9515 f1 | f2  
9516----+-----
9517  1 | foo
9518  2 | bar
9519(2 rows)
9520
9521delete from rem2;
9522-- Test check constraints
9523alter table loc2 add constraint loc2_f1positive check (f1 >= 0);
9524alter foreign table rem2 add constraint rem2_f1positive check (f1 >= 0);
9525-- check constraint is enforced on the remote side, not locally
9526copy rem2 from stdin;
9527copy rem2 from stdin; -- ERROR
9528ERROR:  new row for relation "loc2" violates check constraint "loc2_f1positive"
9529DETAIL:  Failing row contains (-1, xyzzy).
9530CONTEXT:  remote SQL command: INSERT INTO public.loc2(f1, f2) VALUES ($1, $2)
9531COPY rem2, line 1: "-1	xyzzy"
9532select * from rem2;
9533 f1 | f2  
9534----+-----
9535  1 | foo
9536  2 | bar
9537(2 rows)
9538
9539alter foreign table rem2 drop constraint rem2_f1positive;
9540alter table loc2 drop constraint loc2_f1positive;
9541delete from rem2;
9542-- Test local triggers
9543create trigger trig_stmt_before before insert on rem2
9544	for each statement execute procedure trigger_func();
9545create trigger trig_stmt_after after insert on rem2
9546	for each statement execute procedure trigger_func();
9547create trigger trig_row_before before insert on rem2
9548	for each row execute procedure trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
9549create trigger trig_row_after after insert on rem2
9550	for each row execute procedure trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
9551copy rem2 from stdin;
9552NOTICE:  trigger_func(<NULL>) called: action = INSERT, when = BEFORE, level = STATEMENT
9553NOTICE:  trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW INSERT ON rem2
9554NOTICE:  NEW: (1,foo)
9555NOTICE:  trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW INSERT ON rem2
9556NOTICE:  NEW: (2,bar)
9557NOTICE:  trig_row_after(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW INSERT ON rem2
9558NOTICE:  NEW: (1,foo)
9559NOTICE:  trig_row_after(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW INSERT ON rem2
9560NOTICE:  NEW: (2,bar)
9561NOTICE:  trigger_func(<NULL>) called: action = INSERT, when = AFTER, level = STATEMENT
9562select * from rem2;
9563 f1 | f2  
9564----+-----
9565  1 | foo
9566  2 | bar
9567(2 rows)
9568
9569drop trigger trig_row_before on rem2;
9570drop trigger trig_row_after on rem2;
9571drop trigger trig_stmt_before on rem2;
9572drop trigger trig_stmt_after on rem2;
9573delete from rem2;
9574create trigger trig_row_before_insert before insert on rem2
9575	for each row execute procedure trig_row_before_insupdate();
9576-- The new values are concatenated with ' triggered !'
9577copy rem2 from stdin;
9578select * from rem2;
9579 f1 |       f2        
9580----+-----------------
9581  1 | foo triggered !
9582  2 | bar triggered !
9583(2 rows)
9584
9585drop trigger trig_row_before_insert on rem2;
9586delete from rem2;
9587create trigger trig_null before insert on rem2
9588	for each row execute procedure trig_null();
9589-- Nothing happens
9590copy rem2 from stdin;
9591select * from rem2;
9592 f1 | f2 
9593----+----
9594(0 rows)
9595
9596drop trigger trig_null on rem2;
9597delete from rem2;
9598-- Test remote triggers
9599create trigger trig_row_before_insert before insert on loc2
9600	for each row execute procedure trig_row_before_insupdate();
9601-- The new values are concatenated with ' triggered !'
9602copy rem2 from stdin;
9603select * from rem2;
9604 f1 |       f2        
9605----+-----------------
9606  1 | foo triggered !
9607  2 | bar triggered !
9608(2 rows)
9609
9610drop trigger trig_row_before_insert on loc2;
9611delete from rem2;
9612create trigger trig_null before insert on loc2
9613	for each row execute procedure trig_null();
9614-- Nothing happens
9615copy rem2 from stdin;
9616select * from rem2;
9617 f1 | f2 
9618----+----
9619(0 rows)
9620
9621drop trigger trig_null on loc2;
9622delete from rem2;
9623-- Test a combination of local and remote triggers
9624create trigger rem2_trig_row_before before insert on rem2
9625	for each row execute procedure trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
9626create trigger rem2_trig_row_after after insert on rem2
9627	for each row execute procedure trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
9628create trigger loc2_trig_row_before_insert before insert on loc2
9629	for each row execute procedure trig_row_before_insupdate();
9630copy rem2 from stdin;
9631NOTICE:  rem2_trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW INSERT ON rem2
9632NOTICE:  NEW: (1,foo)
9633NOTICE:  rem2_trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW INSERT ON rem2
9634NOTICE:  NEW: (2,bar)
9635NOTICE:  rem2_trig_row_after(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW INSERT ON rem2
9636NOTICE:  NEW: (1,"foo triggered !")
9637NOTICE:  rem2_trig_row_after(23, skidoo) AFTER ROW INSERT ON rem2
9638NOTICE:  NEW: (2,"bar triggered !")
9639select * from rem2;
9640 f1 |       f2        
9641----+-----------------
9642  1 | foo triggered !
9643  2 | bar triggered !
9644(2 rows)
9645
9646drop trigger rem2_trig_row_before on rem2;
9647drop trigger rem2_trig_row_after on rem2;
9648drop trigger loc2_trig_row_before_insert on loc2;
9649delete from rem2;
9650-- test COPY FROM with foreign table created in the same transaction
9651create table loc3 (f1 int, f2 text);
9652begin;
9653create foreign table rem3 (f1 int, f2 text)
9654	server loopback options(table_name 'loc3');
9655copy rem3 from stdin;
9656commit;
9657select * from rem3;
9658 f1 | f2  
9659----+-----
9660  1 | foo
9661  2 | bar
9662(2 rows)
9663
9664drop foreign table rem3;
9665drop table loc3;
9666-- Test COPY FROM with the batch_size option enabled
9667alter server loopback options (add batch_size '2');
9668-- Test basic functionality
9669copy rem2 from stdin;
9670select * from rem2;
9671 f1 | f2  
9672----+-----
9673  1 | foo
9674  2 | bar
9675  3 | baz
9676(3 rows)
9677
9678delete from rem2;
9679-- Test check constraints
9680alter table loc2 add constraint loc2_f1positive check (f1 >= 0);
9681alter foreign table rem2 add constraint rem2_f1positive check (f1 >= 0);
9682-- check constraint is enforced on the remote side, not locally
9683copy rem2 from stdin;
9684copy rem2 from stdin; -- ERROR
9685ERROR:  new row for relation "loc2" violates check constraint "loc2_f1positive"
9686DETAIL:  Failing row contains (-1, xyzzy).
9687CONTEXT:  remote SQL command: INSERT INTO public.loc2(f1, f2) VALUES ($1, $2)
9688COPY rem2
9689select * from rem2;
9690 f1 | f2  
9691----+-----
9692  1 | foo
9693  2 | bar
9694  3 | baz
9695(3 rows)
9696
9697alter foreign table rem2 drop constraint rem2_f1positive;
9698alter table loc2 drop constraint loc2_f1positive;
9699delete from rem2;
9700-- Test remote triggers
9701create trigger trig_row_before_insert before insert on loc2
9702	for each row execute procedure trig_row_before_insupdate();
9703-- The new values are concatenated with ' triggered !'
9704copy rem2 from stdin;
9705select * from rem2;
9706 f1 |       f2        
9707----+-----------------
9708  1 | foo triggered !
9709  2 | bar triggered !
9710  3 | baz triggered !
9711(3 rows)
9712
9713drop trigger trig_row_before_insert on loc2;
9714delete from rem2;
9715create trigger trig_null before insert on loc2
9716	for each row execute procedure trig_null();
9717-- Nothing happens
9718copy rem2 from stdin;
9719select * from rem2;
9720 f1 | f2 
9721----+----
9722(0 rows)
9723
9724drop trigger trig_null on loc2;
9725delete from rem2;
9726-- Check with zero-column foreign table; batch insert will be disabled
9727alter table loc2 drop column f1;
9728alter table loc2 drop column f2;
9729alter table rem2 drop column f1;
9730alter table rem2 drop column f2;
9731copy rem2 from stdin;
9732select * from rem2;
9733--
9734(3 rows)
9735
9736delete from rem2;
9737alter server loopback options (drop batch_size);
9738-- ===================================================================
9739-- test for TRUNCATE
9740-- ===================================================================
9741CREATE TABLE tru_rtable0 (id int primary key);
9742CREATE FOREIGN TABLE tru_ftable (id int)
9743       SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'tru_rtable0');
9744INSERT INTO tru_rtable0 (SELECT x FROM generate_series(1,10) x);
9745CREATE TABLE tru_ptable (id int) PARTITION BY HASH(id);
9746CREATE TABLE tru_ptable__p0 PARTITION OF tru_ptable
9747                            FOR VALUES WITH (MODULUS 2, REMAINDER 0);
9748CREATE TABLE tru_rtable1 (id int primary key);
9749CREATE FOREIGN TABLE tru_ftable__p1 PARTITION OF tru_ptable
9750                                    FOR VALUES WITH (MODULUS 2, REMAINDER 1)
9751       SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'tru_rtable1');
9752INSERT INTO tru_ptable (SELECT x FROM generate_series(11,20) x);
9753CREATE TABLE tru_pk_table(id int primary key);
9754CREATE TABLE tru_fk_table(fkey int references tru_pk_table(id));
9755INSERT INTO tru_pk_table (SELECT x FROM generate_series(1,10) x);
9756INSERT INTO tru_fk_table (SELECT x % 10 + 1 FROM generate_series(5,25) x);
9757CREATE FOREIGN TABLE tru_pk_ftable (id int)
9758       SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'tru_pk_table');
9759CREATE TABLE tru_rtable_parent (id int);
9760CREATE TABLE tru_rtable_child (id int);
9761CREATE FOREIGN TABLE tru_ftable_parent (id int)
9762       SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'tru_rtable_parent');
9763CREATE FOREIGN TABLE tru_ftable_child () INHERITS (tru_ftable_parent)
9764       SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'tru_rtable_child');
9765INSERT INTO tru_rtable_parent (SELECT x FROM generate_series(1,8) x);
9766INSERT INTO tru_rtable_child  (SELECT x FROM generate_series(10, 18) x);
9767-- normal truncate
9768SELECT sum(id) FROM tru_ftable;        -- 55
9769 sum 
9770-----
9771  55
9772(1 row)
9773
9774TRUNCATE tru_ftable;
9775SELECT count(*) FROM tru_rtable0;		-- 0
9776 count 
9777-------
9778     0
9779(1 row)
9780
9781SELECT count(*) FROM tru_ftable;		-- 0
9782 count 
9783-------
9784     0
9785(1 row)
9786
9787-- 'truncatable' option
9788ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (ADD truncatable 'false');
9789TRUNCATE tru_ftable;			-- error
9790ERROR:  foreign table "tru_ftable" does not allow truncates
9791ALTER FOREIGN TABLE tru_ftable OPTIONS (ADD truncatable 'true');
9792TRUNCATE tru_ftable;			-- accepted
9793ALTER FOREIGN TABLE tru_ftable OPTIONS (SET truncatable 'false');
9794TRUNCATE tru_ftable;			-- error
9795ERROR:  foreign table "tru_ftable" does not allow truncates
9796ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (DROP truncatable);
9797ALTER FOREIGN TABLE tru_ftable OPTIONS (SET truncatable 'false');
9798TRUNCATE tru_ftable;			-- error
9799ERROR:  foreign table "tru_ftable" does not allow truncates
9800ALTER FOREIGN TABLE tru_ftable OPTIONS (SET truncatable 'true');
9801TRUNCATE tru_ftable;			-- accepted
9802-- partitioned table with both local and foreign tables as partitions
9803SELECT sum(id) FROM tru_ptable;        -- 155
9804 sum 
9805-----
9806 155
9807(1 row)
9808
9809TRUNCATE tru_ptable;
9810SELECT count(*) FROM tru_ptable;		-- 0
9811 count 
9812-------
9813     0
9814(1 row)
9815
9816SELECT count(*) FROM tru_ptable__p0;	-- 0
9817 count 
9818-------
9819     0
9820(1 row)
9821
9822SELECT count(*) FROM tru_ftable__p1;	-- 0
9823 count 
9824-------
9825     0
9826(1 row)
9827
9828SELECT count(*) FROM tru_rtable1;		-- 0
9829 count 
9830-------
9831     0
9832(1 row)
9833
9834-- 'CASCADE' option
9835SELECT sum(id) FROM tru_pk_ftable;      -- 55
9836 sum 
9837-----
9838  55
9839(1 row)
9840
9841TRUNCATE tru_pk_ftable;	-- failed by FK reference
9842ERROR:  cannot truncate a table referenced in a foreign key constraint
9843DETAIL:  Table "tru_fk_table" references "tru_pk_table".
9844HINT:  Truncate table "tru_fk_table" at the same time, or use TRUNCATE ... CASCADE.
9845CONTEXT:  remote SQL command: TRUNCATE public.tru_pk_table CONTINUE IDENTITY RESTRICT
9846TRUNCATE tru_pk_ftable CASCADE;
9847SELECT count(*) FROM tru_pk_ftable;    -- 0
9848 count 
9849-------
9850     0
9851(1 row)
9852
9853SELECT count(*) FROM tru_fk_table;		-- also truncated,0
9854 count 
9855-------
9856     0
9857(1 row)
9858
9859-- truncate two tables at a command
9860INSERT INTO tru_ftable (SELECT x FROM generate_series(1,8) x);
9861INSERT INTO tru_pk_ftable (SELECT x FROM generate_series(3,10) x);
9862SELECT count(*) from tru_ftable; -- 8
9863 count 
9864-------
9865     8
9866(1 row)
9867
9868SELECT count(*) from tru_pk_ftable; -- 8
9869 count 
9870-------
9871     8
9872(1 row)
9873
9874TRUNCATE tru_ftable, tru_pk_ftable CASCADE;
9875SELECT count(*) from tru_ftable; -- 0
9876 count 
9877-------
9878     0
9879(1 row)
9880
9881SELECT count(*) from tru_pk_ftable; -- 0
9882 count 
9883-------
9884     0
9885(1 row)
9886
9887-- truncate with ONLY clause
9888-- Since ONLY is specified, the table tru_ftable_child that inherits
9889-- tru_ftable_parent locally is not truncated.
9890TRUNCATE ONLY tru_ftable_parent;
9891SELECT sum(id) FROM tru_ftable_parent;  -- 126
9892 sum 
9893-----
9894 126
9895(1 row)
9896
9897TRUNCATE tru_ftable_parent;
9898SELECT count(*) FROM tru_ftable_parent; -- 0
9899 count 
9900-------
9901     0
9902(1 row)
9903
9904-- in case when remote table has inherited children
9905CREATE TABLE tru_rtable0_child () INHERITS (tru_rtable0);
9906INSERT INTO tru_rtable0 (SELECT x FROM generate_series(5,9) x);
9907INSERT INTO tru_rtable0_child (SELECT x FROM generate_series(10,14) x);
9908SELECT sum(id) FROM tru_ftable;   -- 95
9909 sum 
9910-----
9911  95
9912(1 row)
9913
9914-- Both parent and child tables in the foreign server are truncated
9915-- even though ONLY is specified because ONLY has no effect
9916-- when truncating a foreign table.
9917TRUNCATE ONLY tru_ftable;
9918SELECT count(*) FROM tru_ftable;   -- 0
9919 count 
9920-------
9921     0
9922(1 row)
9923
9924INSERT INTO tru_rtable0 (SELECT x FROM generate_series(21,25) x);
9925INSERT INTO tru_rtable0_child (SELECT x FROM generate_series(26,30) x);
9926SELECT sum(id) FROM tru_ftable;		-- 255
9927 sum 
9928-----
9929 255
9930(1 row)
9931
9932TRUNCATE tru_ftable;			-- truncate both of parent and child
9933SELECT count(*) FROM tru_ftable;    -- 0
9934 count 
9935-------
9936     0
9937(1 row)
9938
9939-- cleanup
9940DROP FOREIGN TABLE tru_ftable_parent, tru_ftable_child, tru_pk_ftable,tru_ftable__p1,tru_ftable;
9941DROP TABLE tru_rtable0, tru_rtable1, tru_ptable, tru_ptable__p0, tru_pk_table, tru_fk_table,
9942tru_rtable_parent,tru_rtable_child, tru_rtable0_child;
9943-- ===================================================================
9944-- test IMPORT FOREIGN SCHEMA
9945-- ===================================================================
9946CREATE SCHEMA import_source;
9947CREATE TABLE import_source.t1 (c1 int, c2 varchar NOT NULL);
9948CREATE TABLE import_source.t2 (c1 int default 42, c2 varchar NULL, c3 text collate "POSIX");
9949CREATE TYPE typ1 AS (m1 int, m2 varchar);
9950CREATE TABLE import_source.t3 (c1 timestamptz default now(), c2 typ1);
9951CREATE TABLE import_source."x 4" (c1 float8, "C 2" text, c3 varchar(42));
9952CREATE TABLE import_source."x 5" (c1 float8);
9953ALTER TABLE import_source."x 5" DROP COLUMN c1;
9954CREATE TABLE import_source."x 6" (c1 int, c2 int generated always as (c1 * 2) stored);
9955CREATE TABLE import_source.t4 (c1 int) PARTITION BY RANGE (c1);
9956CREATE TABLE import_source.t4_part PARTITION OF import_source.t4
9957  FOR VALUES FROM (1) TO (100);
9958CREATE TABLE import_source.t4_part2 PARTITION OF import_source.t4
9959  FOR VALUES FROM (100) TO (200);
9960CREATE SCHEMA import_dest1;
9961IMPORT FOREIGN SCHEMA import_source FROM SERVER loopback INTO import_dest1;
9962\det+ import_dest1.*
9963                                     List of foreign tables
9964    Schema    | Table |  Server  |                   FDW options                   | Description 
9965--------------+-------+----------+-------------------------------------------------+-------------
9966 import_dest1 | t1    | loopback | (schema_name 'import_source', table_name 't1')  | 
9967 import_dest1 | t2    | loopback | (schema_name 'import_source', table_name 't2')  | 
9968 import_dest1 | t3    | loopback | (schema_name 'import_source', table_name 't3')  | 
9969 import_dest1 | t4    | loopback | (schema_name 'import_source', table_name 't4')  | 
9970 import_dest1 | x 4   | loopback | (schema_name 'import_source', table_name 'x 4') | 
9971 import_dest1 | x 5   | loopback | (schema_name 'import_source', table_name 'x 5') | 
9972 import_dest1 | x 6   | loopback | (schema_name 'import_source', table_name 'x 6') | 
9973(7 rows)
