10176-- Check LIMIT TO and EXCEPT
10177CREATE SCHEMA import_dest4;
10178IMPORT FOREIGN SCHEMA import_source LIMIT TO (t1, nonesuch, t4_part)
10179  FROM SERVER loopback INTO import_dest4;
10180\det+ import_dest4.*
10181                                        List of foreign tables
10182    Schema    |  Table  |  Server  |                     FDW options                     | Description 
10183--------------+---------+----------+-----------------------------------------------------+-------------
10184 import_dest4 | t1      | loopback | (schema_name 'import_source', table_name 't1')      | 
10185 import_dest4 | t4_part | loopback | (schema_name 'import_source', table_name 't4_part') | 
10186(2 rows)
10187
10188IMPORT FOREIGN SCHEMA import_source EXCEPT (t1, "x 4", nonesuch, t4_part)
10189  FROM SERVER loopback INTO import_dest4;
10190\det+ import_dest4.*
10191                                        List of foreign tables
10192    Schema    |  Table  |  Server  |                     FDW options                     | Description 
10193--------------+---------+----------+-----------------------------------------------------+-------------
10194 import_dest4 | t1      | loopback | (schema_name 'import_source', table_name 't1')      | 
10195 import_dest4 | t2      | loopback | (schema_name 'import_source', table_name 't2')      | 
10196 import_dest4 | t3      | loopback | (schema_name 'import_source', table_name 't3')      | 
10197 import_dest4 | t4      | loopback | (schema_name 'import_source', table_name 't4')      | 
10198 import_dest4 | t4_part | loopback | (schema_name 'import_source', table_name 't4_part') | 
10199 import_dest4 | x 5     | loopback | (schema_name 'import_source', table_name 'x 5')     | 
10200 import_dest4 | x 6     | loopback | (schema_name 'import_source', table_name 'x 6')     | 
10201(7 rows)
10202
10203-- Assorted error cases
10204IMPORT FOREIGN SCHEMA import_source FROM SERVER loopback INTO import_dest4;
10205ERROR:  relation "t1" already exists
10206CONTEXT:  importing foreign table "t1"
10207IMPORT FOREIGN SCHEMA nonesuch FROM SERVER loopback INTO import_dest4;
10208ERROR:  schema "nonesuch" is not present on foreign server "loopback"
10209IMPORT FOREIGN SCHEMA nonesuch FROM SERVER loopback INTO notthere;
10210ERROR:  schema "notthere" does not exist
10211IMPORT FOREIGN SCHEMA nonesuch FROM SERVER nowhere INTO notthere;
10212ERROR:  server "nowhere" does not exist
10213-- Check case of a type present only on the remote server.
10214-- We can fake this by dropping the type locally in our transaction.
10215CREATE TYPE "Colors" AS ENUM ('red', 'green', 'blue');
10216CREATE TABLE import_source.t5 (c1 int, c2 text collate "C", "Col" "Colors");
10217CREATE SCHEMA import_dest5;
10218BEGIN;
10219DROP TYPE "Colors" CASCADE;
10220NOTICE:  drop cascades to column Col of table import_source.t5
10221IMPORT FOREIGN SCHEMA import_source LIMIT TO (t5)
10222  FROM SERVER loopback INTO import_dest5;  -- ERROR
10223ERROR:  type "public.Colors" does not exist
10224LINE 4:   "Col" public."Colors" OPTIONS (column_name 'Col')
10225                ^
10226QUERY:  CREATE FOREIGN TABLE t5 (
10227  c1 integer OPTIONS (column_name 'c1'),
10228  c2 text OPTIONS (column_name 'c2') COLLATE pg_catalog."C",
10229  "Col" public."Colors" OPTIONS (column_name 'Col')
10230) SERVER loopback
10231OPTIONS (schema_name 'import_source', table_name 't5');
10232CONTEXT:  importing foreign table "t5"
10233ROLLBACK;
10234BEGIN;
10235CREATE SERVER fetch101 FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER postgres_fdw OPTIONS( fetch_size '101' );
10236SELECT count(*)
10237FROM pg_foreign_server
10238WHERE srvname = 'fetch101'
10239AND srvoptions @> array['fetch_size=101'];
10240 count 
10241-------
10242     1
10243(1 row)
10244
10245ALTER SERVER fetch101 OPTIONS( SET fetch_size '202' );
10246SELECT count(*)
10247FROM pg_foreign_server
10248WHERE srvname = 'fetch101'
10249AND srvoptions @> array['fetch_size=101'];
10250 count 
10251-------
10252     0
10253(1 row)
10254
10255SELECT count(*)
10256FROM pg_foreign_server
10257WHERE srvname = 'fetch101'
10258AND srvoptions @> array['fetch_size=202'];
10259 count 
10260-------
10261     1
10262(1 row)
10263
10264CREATE FOREIGN TABLE table30000 ( x int ) SERVER fetch101 OPTIONS ( fetch_size '30000' );
10265SELECT COUNT(*)
10266FROM pg_foreign_table
10267WHERE ftrelid = 'table30000'::regclass
10268AND ftoptions @> array['fetch_size=30000'];
10269 count 
10270-------
10271     1
10272(1 row)
10273
10274ALTER FOREIGN TABLE table30000 OPTIONS ( SET fetch_size '60000');
10275SELECT COUNT(*)
10276FROM pg_foreign_table
10277WHERE ftrelid = 'table30000'::regclass
10278AND ftoptions @> array['fetch_size=30000'];
10279 count 
10280-------
10281     0
10282(1 row)
10283
10284SELECT COUNT(*)
10285FROM pg_foreign_table
10286WHERE ftrelid = 'table30000'::regclass
10287AND ftoptions @> array['fetch_size=60000'];
10288 count 
10289-------
10290     1
10291(1 row)
10292
10293ROLLBACK;
10294-- ===================================================================
10295-- test partitionwise joins
10296-- ===================================================================
10297SET enable_partitionwise_join=on;
10298CREATE TABLE fprt1 (a int, b int, c varchar) PARTITION BY RANGE(a);
10299CREATE TABLE fprt1_p1 (LIKE fprt1);
10300CREATE TABLE fprt1_p2 (LIKE fprt1);
10301ALTER TABLE fprt1_p1 SET (autovacuum_enabled = 'false');
10302ALTER TABLE fprt1_p2 SET (autovacuum_enabled = 'false');
10303INSERT INTO fprt1_p1 SELECT i, i, to_char(i/50, 'FM0000') FROM generate_series(0, 249, 2) i;
10304INSERT INTO fprt1_p2 SELECT i, i, to_char(i/50, 'FM0000') FROM generate_series(250, 499, 2) i;
10305CREATE FOREIGN TABLE ftprt1_p1 PARTITION OF fprt1 FOR VALUES FROM (0) TO (250)
10306	SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'fprt1_p1', use_remote_estimate 'true');
10307CREATE FOREIGN TABLE ftprt1_p2 PARTITION OF fprt1 FOR VALUES FROM (250) TO (500)
10308	SERVER loopback OPTIONS (TABLE_NAME 'fprt1_p2');
10309ANALYZE fprt1;
10310ANALYZE fprt1_p1;
10311ANALYZE fprt1_p2;
10312CREATE TABLE fprt2 (a int, b int, c varchar) PARTITION BY RANGE(b);
10313CREATE TABLE fprt2_p1 (LIKE fprt2);
10314CREATE TABLE fprt2_p2 (LIKE fprt2);
10315ALTER TABLE fprt2_p1 SET (autovacuum_enabled = 'false');
10316ALTER TABLE fprt2_p2 SET (autovacuum_enabled = 'false');
10317INSERT INTO fprt2_p1 SELECT i, i, to_char(i/50, 'FM0000') FROM generate_series(0, 249, 3) i;
10318INSERT INTO fprt2_p2 SELECT i, i, to_char(i/50, 'FM0000') FROM generate_series(250, 499, 3) i;
10319CREATE FOREIGN TABLE ftprt2_p1 (b int, c varchar, a int)
10320	SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'fprt2_p1', use_remote_estimate 'true');
10321ALTER TABLE fprt2 ATTACH PARTITION ftprt2_p1 FOR VALUES FROM (0) TO (250);
10322CREATE FOREIGN TABLE ftprt2_p2 PARTITION OF fprt2 FOR VALUES FROM (250) TO (500)
10323	SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'fprt2_p2', use_remote_estimate 'true');
10324ANALYZE fprt2;
10325ANALYZE fprt2_p1;
10326ANALYZE fprt2_p2;
10327-- inner join three tables
10328EXPLAIN (COSTS OFF)
10329SELECT t1.a,t2.b,t3.c FROM fprt1 t1 INNER JOIN fprt2 t2 ON (t1.a = t2.b) INNER JOIN fprt1 t3 ON (t2.b = t3.a) WHERE t1.a % 25 =0 ORDER BY 1,2,3;
10330                                             QUERY PLAN                                              
10331-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10332 Sort
10333   Sort Key: t1.a, t3.c
10334   ->  Append
10335         ->  Foreign Scan
10336               Relations: ((ftprt1_p1 t1_1) INNER JOIN (ftprt2_p1 t2_1)) INNER JOIN (ftprt1_p1 t3_1)
10337         ->  Foreign Scan
10338               Relations: ((ftprt1_p2 t1_2) INNER JOIN (ftprt2_p2 t2_2)) INNER JOIN (ftprt1_p2 t3_2)
10339(7 rows)
10340
10341SELECT t1.a,t2.b,t3.c FROM fprt1 t1 INNER JOIN fprt2 t2 ON (t1.a = t2.b) INNER JOIN fprt1 t3 ON (t2.b = t3.a) WHERE t1.a % 25 =0 ORDER BY 1,2,3;
10342  a  |  b  |  c   
10343-----+-----+------
10344   0 |   0 | 0000
10345 150 | 150 | 0003
10346 250 | 250 | 0005
10347 400 | 400 | 0008
10348(4 rows)
10349
10350-- left outer join + nullable clause
10351EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
10352SELECT t1.a,t2.b,t2.c FROM fprt1 t1 LEFT JOIN (SELECT * FROM fprt2 WHERE a < 10) t2 ON (t1.a = t2.b and t1.b = t2.a) WHERE t1.a < 10 ORDER BY 1,2,3;
10353                                                                                                                     QUERY PLAN                                                                                                                     
10354----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10355 Foreign Scan
10356   Output: t1.a, fprt2.b, fprt2.c
10357   Relations: (public.ftprt1_p1 t1) LEFT JOIN (public.ftprt2_p1 fprt2)
10358   Remote SQL: SELECT r5.a, r6.b, r6.c FROM (public.fprt1_p1 r5 LEFT JOIN public.fprt2_p1 r6 ON (((r5.a = r6.b)) AND ((r5.b = r6.a)) AND ((r6.a < 10)))) WHERE ((r5.a < 10)) ORDER BY r5.a ASC NULLS LAST, r6.b ASC NULLS LAST, r6.c ASC NULLS LAST
10359(4 rows)
10360
10361SELECT t1.a,t2.b,t2.c FROM fprt1 t1 LEFT JOIN (SELECT * FROM fprt2 WHERE a < 10) t2 ON (t1.a = t2.b and t1.b = t2.a) WHERE t1.a < 10 ORDER BY 1,2,3;
10362 a | b |  c   
10363---+---+------
10364 0 | 0 | 0000
10365 2 |   | 
10366 4 |   | 
10367 6 | 6 | 0000
10368 8 |   | 
10369(5 rows)
10370
10371-- with whole-row reference; partitionwise join does not apply
10372EXPLAIN (COSTS OFF)
10373SELECT t1.wr, t2.wr FROM (SELECT t1 wr, a FROM fprt1 t1 WHERE t1.a % 25 = 0) t1 FULL JOIN (SELECT t2 wr, b FROM fprt2 t2 WHERE t2.b % 25 = 0) t2 ON (t1.a = t2.b) ORDER BY 1,2;
10374                       QUERY PLAN                       
10375--------------------------------------------------------
10376 Sort
10377   Sort Key: ((t1.*)::fprt1), ((t2.*)::fprt2)
10378   ->  Hash Full Join
10379         Hash Cond: (t1.a = t2.b)
10380         ->  Append
10381               ->  Foreign Scan on ftprt1_p1 t1_1
10382               ->  Foreign Scan on ftprt1_p2 t1_2
10383         ->  Hash
10384               ->  Append
10385                     ->  Foreign Scan on ftprt2_p1 t2_1
10386                     ->  Foreign Scan on ftprt2_p2 t2_2
10387(11 rows)
10388
10389SELECT t1.wr, t2.wr FROM (SELECT t1 wr, a FROM fprt1 t1 WHERE t1.a % 25 = 0) t1 FULL JOIN (SELECT t2 wr, b FROM fprt2 t2 WHERE t2.b % 25 = 0) t2 ON (t1.a = t2.b) ORDER BY 1,2;
10390       wr       |       wr       
10391----------------+----------------
10392 (0,0,0000)     | (0,0,0000)
10393 (50,50,0001)   | 
10394 (100,100,0002) | 
10395 (150,150,0003) | (150,150,0003)
10396 (200,200,0004) | 
10397 (250,250,0005) | (250,250,0005)
10398 (300,300,0006) | 
10399 (350,350,0007) | 
10400 (400,400,0008) | (400,400,0008)
10401 (450,450,0009) | 
10402                | (75,75,0001)
10403                | (225,225,0004)
10404                | (325,325,0006)
10405                | (475,475,0009)
10406(14 rows)
10407
10408-- join with lateral reference
10409EXPLAIN (COSTS OFF)
10410SELECT t1.a,t1.b FROM fprt1 t1, LATERAL (SELECT t2.a, t2.b FROM fprt2 t2 WHERE t1.a = t2.b AND t1.b = t2.a) q WHERE t1.a%25 = 0 ORDER BY 1,2;
10411                              QUERY PLAN                               
10412-----------------------------------------------------------------------
10413 Sort
10414   Sort Key: t1.a, t1.b
10415   ->  Append
10416         ->  Foreign Scan
10417               Relations: (ftprt1_p1 t1_1) INNER JOIN (ftprt2_p1 t2_1)
10418         ->  Foreign Scan
10419               Relations: (ftprt1_p2 t1_2) INNER JOIN (ftprt2_p2 t2_2)
10420(7 rows)
10421
10422SELECT t1.a,t1.b FROM fprt1 t1, LATERAL (SELECT t2.a, t2.b FROM fprt2 t2 WHERE t1.a = t2.b AND t1.b = t2.a) q WHERE t1.a%25 = 0 ORDER BY 1,2;
10423  a  |  b  
10424-----+-----
10425   0 |   0
10426 150 | 150
10427 250 | 250
10428 400 | 400
10429(4 rows)
10430
10431-- with PHVs, partitionwise join selected but no join pushdown
10432EXPLAIN (COSTS OFF)
10433SELECT t1.a, t1.phv, t2.b, t2.phv FROM (SELECT 't1_phv' phv, * FROM fprt1 WHERE a % 25 = 0) t1 FULL JOIN (SELECT 't2_phv' phv, * FROM fprt2 WHERE b % 25 = 0) t2 ON (t1.a = t2.b) ORDER BY t1.a, t2.b;
10434                        QUERY PLAN                         
10435-----------------------------------------------------------
10436 Sort
10437   Sort Key: fprt1.a, fprt2.b
10438   ->  Append
10439         ->  Hash Full Join
10440               Hash Cond: (fprt1_1.a = fprt2_1.b)
10441               ->  Foreign Scan on ftprt1_p1 fprt1_1
10442               ->  Hash
10443                     ->  Foreign Scan on ftprt2_p1 fprt2_1
10444         ->  Hash Full Join
10445               Hash Cond: (fprt1_2.a = fprt2_2.b)
10446               ->  Foreign Scan on ftprt1_p2 fprt1_2
10447               ->  Hash
10448                     ->  Foreign Scan on ftprt2_p2 fprt2_2
10449(13 rows)
10450
10451SELECT t1.a, t1.phv, t2.b, t2.phv FROM (SELECT 't1_phv' phv, * FROM fprt1 WHERE a % 25 = 0) t1 FULL JOIN (SELECT 't2_phv' phv, * FROM fprt2 WHERE b % 25 = 0) t2 ON (t1.a = t2.b) ORDER BY t1.a, t2.b;
10452  a  |  phv   |  b  |  phv   
10453-----+--------+-----+--------
10454   0 | t1_phv |   0 | t2_phv
10455  50 | t1_phv |     | 
10456 100 | t1_phv |     | 
10457 150 | t1_phv | 150 | t2_phv
10458 200 | t1_phv |     | 
10459 250 | t1_phv | 250 | t2_phv
10460 300 | t1_phv |     | 
10461 350 | t1_phv |     | 
10462 400 | t1_phv | 400 | t2_phv
10463 450 | t1_phv |     | 
10464     |        |  75 | t2_phv
10465     |        | 225 | t2_phv
10466     |        | 325 | t2_phv
10467     |        | 475 | t2_phv
10468(14 rows)
10469
10470-- test FOR UPDATE; partitionwise join does not apply
10471EXPLAIN (COSTS OFF)
10472SELECT t1.a, t2.b FROM fprt1 t1 INNER JOIN fprt2 t2 ON (t1.a = t2.b) WHERE t1.a % 25 = 0 ORDER BY 1,2 FOR UPDATE OF t1;
10473                       QUERY PLAN                       
10474--------------------------------------------------------
10475 LockRows
10476   ->  Nested Loop
10477         Join Filter: (t1.a = t2.b)
10478         ->  Append
10479               ->  Foreign Scan on ftprt1_p1 t1_1
10480               ->  Foreign Scan on ftprt1_p2 t1_2
10481         ->  Materialize
10482               ->  Append
10483                     ->  Foreign Scan on ftprt2_p1 t2_1
10484                     ->  Foreign Scan on ftprt2_p2 t2_2
10485(10 rows)
10486
10487SELECT t1.a, t2.b FROM fprt1 t1 INNER JOIN fprt2 t2 ON (t1.a = t2.b) WHERE t1.a % 25 = 0 ORDER BY 1,2 FOR UPDATE OF t1;
10488  a  |  b  
10489-----+-----
10490   0 |   0
10491 150 | 150
10492 250 | 250
10493 400 | 400
10494(4 rows)
10495
10496RESET enable_partitionwise_join;
10497-- ===================================================================
10498-- test partitionwise aggregates
10499-- ===================================================================
10500CREATE TABLE pagg_tab (a int, b int, c text) PARTITION BY RANGE(a);
10501CREATE TABLE pagg_tab_p1 (LIKE pagg_tab);
10502CREATE TABLE pagg_tab_p2 (LIKE pagg_tab);
10503CREATE TABLE pagg_tab_p3 (LIKE pagg_tab);
10504INSERT INTO pagg_tab_p1 SELECT i % 30, i % 50, to_char(i/30, 'FM0000') FROM generate_series(1, 3000) i WHERE (i % 30) < 10;
10505INSERT INTO pagg_tab_p2 SELECT i % 30, i % 50, to_char(i/30, 'FM0000') FROM generate_series(1, 3000) i WHERE (i % 30) < 20 and (i % 30) >= 10;
10506INSERT INTO pagg_tab_p3 SELECT i % 30, i % 50, to_char(i/30, 'FM0000') FROM generate_series(1, 3000) i WHERE (i % 30) < 30 and (i % 30) >= 20;
10507-- Create foreign partitions
10508CREATE FOREIGN TABLE fpagg_tab_p1 PARTITION OF pagg_tab FOR VALUES FROM (0) TO (10) SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'pagg_tab_p1');
10509CREATE FOREIGN TABLE fpagg_tab_p2 PARTITION OF pagg_tab FOR VALUES FROM (10) TO (20) SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'pagg_tab_p2');
10510CREATE FOREIGN TABLE fpagg_tab_p3 PARTITION OF pagg_tab FOR VALUES FROM (20) TO (30) SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'pagg_tab_p3');
10511ANALYZE pagg_tab;
10512ANALYZE fpagg_tab_p1;
10513ANALYZE fpagg_tab_p2;
10514ANALYZE fpagg_tab_p3;
10515-- When GROUP BY clause matches with PARTITION KEY.
10516-- Plan with partitionwise aggregates is disabled
10517SET enable_partitionwise_aggregate TO false;
10518EXPLAIN (COSTS OFF)
10519SELECT a, sum(b), min(b), count(*) FROM pagg_tab GROUP BY a HAVING avg(b) < 22 ORDER BY 1;
10520                     QUERY PLAN                      
10521-----------------------------------------------------
10522 GroupAggregate
10523   Group Key: pagg_tab.a
10524   Filter: (avg(pagg_tab.b) < '22'::numeric)
10525   ->  Append
10526         ->  Foreign Scan on fpagg_tab_p1 pagg_tab_1
10527         ->  Foreign Scan on fpagg_tab_p2 pagg_tab_2
10528         ->  Foreign Scan on fpagg_tab_p3 pagg_tab_3
10529(7 rows)
10530
10531-- Plan with partitionwise aggregates is enabled
10532SET enable_partitionwise_aggregate TO true;
10533EXPLAIN (COSTS OFF)
10534SELECT a, sum(b), min(b), count(*) FROM pagg_tab GROUP BY a HAVING avg(b) < 22 ORDER BY 1;
10535                           QUERY PLAN                            
10536-----------------------------------------------------------------
10537 Sort
10538   Sort Key: pagg_tab.a
10539   ->  Append
10540         ->  Foreign Scan
10541               Relations: Aggregate on (fpagg_tab_p1 pagg_tab)
10542         ->  Foreign Scan
10543               Relations: Aggregate on (fpagg_tab_p2 pagg_tab_1)
10544         ->  Foreign Scan
10545               Relations: Aggregate on (fpagg_tab_p3 pagg_tab_2)
10546(9 rows)
10547
10548SELECT a, sum(b), min(b), count(*) FROM pagg_tab GROUP BY a HAVING avg(b) < 22 ORDER BY 1;
10549 a  | sum  | min | count 
10550----+------+-----+-------
10551  0 | 2000 |   0 |   100
10552  1 | 2100 |   1 |   100
10553 10 | 2000 |   0 |   100
10554 11 | 2100 |   1 |   100
10555 20 | 2000 |   0 |   100
10556 21 | 2100 |   1 |   100
10557(6 rows)
10558
10559-- Check with whole-row reference
10560-- Should have all the columns in the target list for the given relation
10561EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
10562SELECT a, count(t1) FROM pagg_tab t1 GROUP BY a HAVING avg(b) < 22 ORDER BY 1;
10563                                         QUERY PLAN                                         
10564--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10565 Merge Append
10566   Sort Key: t1.a
10567   ->  GroupAggregate
10568         Output: t1.a, count(((t1.*)::pagg_tab))
10569         Group Key: t1.a
10570         Filter: (avg(t1.b) < '22'::numeric)
10571         ->  Foreign Scan on public.fpagg_tab_p1 t1
10572               Output: t1.a, t1.*, t1.b
10573               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.pagg_tab_p1 ORDER BY a ASC NULLS LAST
10574   ->  GroupAggregate
10575         Output: t1_1.a, count(((t1_1.*)::pagg_tab))
10576         Group Key: t1_1.a
10577         Filter: (avg(t1_1.b) < '22'::numeric)
10578         ->  Foreign Scan on public.fpagg_tab_p2 t1_1
10579               Output: t1_1.a, t1_1.*, t1_1.b
10580               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.pagg_tab_p2 ORDER BY a ASC NULLS LAST
10581   ->  GroupAggregate
10582         Output: t1_2.a, count(((t1_2.*)::pagg_tab))
10583         Group Key: t1_2.a
10584         Filter: (avg(t1_2.b) < '22'::numeric)
10585         ->  Foreign Scan on public.fpagg_tab_p3 t1_2
10586               Output: t1_2.a, t1_2.*, t1_2.b
10587               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.pagg_tab_p3 ORDER BY a ASC NULLS LAST
10588(23 rows)
10589
10590SELECT a, count(t1) FROM pagg_tab t1 GROUP BY a HAVING avg(b) < 22 ORDER BY 1;
10591 a  | count 
10592----+-------
10593  0 |   100
10594  1 |   100
10595 10 |   100
10596 11 |   100
10597 20 |   100
10598 21 |   100
10599(6 rows)
10600
10601-- When GROUP BY clause does not match with PARTITION KEY.
10602EXPLAIN (COSTS OFF)
10603SELECT b, avg(a), max(a), count(*) FROM pagg_tab GROUP BY b HAVING sum(a) < 700 ORDER BY 1;
10604                        QUERY PLAN                         
10605-----------------------------------------------------------
10606 Finalize GroupAggregate
10607   Group Key: pagg_tab.b
10608   Filter: (sum(pagg_tab.a) < 700)
10609   ->  Merge Append
10610         Sort Key: pagg_tab.b
10611         ->  Partial GroupAggregate
10612               Group Key: pagg_tab.b
10613               ->  Foreign Scan on fpagg_tab_p1 pagg_tab
10614         ->  Partial GroupAggregate
10615               Group Key: pagg_tab_1.b
10616               ->  Foreign Scan on fpagg_tab_p2 pagg_tab_1
10617         ->  Partial GroupAggregate
10618               Group Key: pagg_tab_2.b
10619               ->  Foreign Scan on fpagg_tab_p3 pagg_tab_2
10620(14 rows)
10621
10622-- ===================================================================
10623-- access rights and superuser
10624-- ===================================================================
10625-- Non-superuser cannot create a FDW without a password in the connstr
10626CREATE ROLE regress_nosuper NOSUPERUSER;
10627GRANT USAGE ON FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER postgres_fdw TO regress_nosuper;
10628SET ROLE regress_nosuper;
10629SHOW is_superuser;
10630 is_superuser 
10631--------------
10632 off
10633(1 row)
10634
10635-- This will be OK, we can create the FDW
10636DO $d$
10637    BEGIN
10638        EXECUTE $$CREATE SERVER loopback_nopw FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER postgres_fdw
10639            OPTIONS (dbname '$$||current_database()||$$',
10640                     port '$$||current_setting('port')||$$'
10641            )$$;
10642    END;
10643$d$;
10644-- But creation of user mappings for non-superusers should fail
10645CREATE USER MAPPING FOR public SERVER loopback_nopw;
10646CREATE USER MAPPING FOR CURRENT_USER SERVER loopback_nopw;
10647CREATE FOREIGN TABLE pg_temp.ft1_nopw (
10648	c1 int NOT NULL,
10649	c2 int NOT NULL,
10650	c3 text,
10651	c4 timestamptz,
10652	c5 timestamp,
10653	c6 varchar(10),
10654	c7 char(10) default 'ft1',
10655	c8 user_enum
10656) SERVER loopback_nopw OPTIONS (schema_name 'public', table_name 'ft1');
10657SELECT 1 FROM ft1_nopw LIMIT 1;
10658ERROR:  password or GSSAPI delegated credentials required
10659DETAIL:  Non-superusers must delegate GSSAPI credentials, provide a password, or enable SCRAM pass-through in user mapping.
10660-- If we add a password to the connstr it'll fail, because we don't allow passwords
10661-- in connstrs only in user mappings.
10662ALTER SERVER loopback_nopw OPTIONS (ADD password 'dummypw');
10663ERROR:  invalid option "password"
10664HINT:  Perhaps you meant the option "passfile".
10665-- If we add a password for our user mapping instead, we should get a different
10666-- error because the password wasn't actually *used* when we run with trust auth.
10667--
10668-- This won't work with installcheck, but neither will most of the FDW checks.
10669ALTER USER MAPPING FOR CURRENT_USER SERVER loopback_nopw OPTIONS (ADD password 'dummypw');
10670SELECT 1 FROM ft1_nopw LIMIT 1;
10671ERROR:  password or GSSAPI delegated credentials required
10672DETAIL:  Non-superuser cannot connect if the server does not request a password or use GSSAPI with delegated credentials.
10673HINT:  Target server's authentication method must be changed or password_required=false set in the user mapping attributes.
10674-- Unpriv user cannot make the mapping passwordless
10675ALTER USER MAPPING FOR CURRENT_USER SERVER loopback_nopw OPTIONS (ADD password_required 'false');
10676ERROR:  password_required=false is superuser-only
10677HINT:  User mappings with the password_required option set to false may only be created or modified by the superuser.
10678SELECT 1 FROM ft1_nopw LIMIT 1;
10679ERROR:  password or GSSAPI delegated credentials required
10680DETAIL:  Non-superuser cannot connect if the server does not request a password or use GSSAPI with delegated credentials.
10681HINT:  Target server's authentication method must be changed or password_required=false set in the user mapping attributes.
10682RESET ROLE;
10683-- But the superuser can
10684ALTER USER MAPPING FOR regress_nosuper SERVER loopback_nopw OPTIONS (ADD password_required 'false');
10685SET ROLE regress_nosuper;
10686-- Should finally work now
10687SELECT 1 FROM ft1_nopw LIMIT 1;
10688 ?column? 
10689----------
10690        1
10691(1 row)
10692
10693-- unpriv user also cannot set sslcert / sslkey on the user mapping
10694-- first set password_required so we see the right error messages
10695ALTER USER MAPPING FOR CURRENT_USER SERVER loopback_nopw OPTIONS (SET password_required 'true');
10696ALTER USER MAPPING FOR CURRENT_USER SERVER loopback_nopw OPTIONS (ADD sslcert 'foo.crt');
10697ERROR:  sslcert and sslkey are superuser-only
10698HINT:  User mappings with the sslcert or sslkey options set may only be created or modified by the superuser.
10699ALTER USER MAPPING FOR CURRENT_USER SERVER loopback_nopw OPTIONS (ADD sslkey 'foo.key');
10700ERROR:  sslcert and sslkey are superuser-only
10701HINT:  User mappings with the sslcert or sslkey options set may only be created or modified by the superuser.
10702-- We're done with the role named after a specific user and need to check the
10703-- changes to the public mapping.
10704DROP USER MAPPING FOR CURRENT_USER SERVER loopback_nopw;
10705-- This will fail again as it'll resolve the user mapping for public, which
10706-- lacks password_required=false
10707SELECT 1 FROM ft1_nopw LIMIT 1;
10708ERROR:  password or GSSAPI delegated credentials required
10709DETAIL:  Non-superusers must delegate GSSAPI credentials, provide a password, or enable SCRAM pass-through in user mapping.
10710RESET ROLE;
10711-- The user mapping for public is passwordless and lacks the password_required=false
10712-- mapping option, but will work because the current user is a superuser.
10713SELECT 1 FROM ft1_nopw LIMIT 1;
10714 ?column? 
10715----------
10716        1
10717(1 row)
10718
10719-- cleanup
10720DROP USER MAPPING FOR public SERVER loopback_nopw;
10721DROP OWNED BY regress_nosuper;
10722DROP ROLE regress_nosuper;
10723-- Clean-up
10724RESET enable_partitionwise_aggregate;
10725-- Two-phase transactions are not supported.
10726BEGIN;
10727SELECT count(*) FROM ft1;
10728 count 
10729-------
10730   822
10731(1 row)
10732
10733-- error here
10734PREPARE TRANSACTION 'fdw_tpc';
10735ERROR:  cannot PREPARE a transaction that has operated on postgres_fdw foreign tables
10736ROLLBACK;
10737WARNING:  there is no transaction in progress
10738-- ===================================================================
10739-- reestablish new connection
10740-- ===================================================================
10741-- Change application_name of remote connection to special one
10742-- so that we can easily terminate the connection later.
10743ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (application_name 'fdw_retry_check');
10744-- Make sure we have a remote connection.
10745SELECT 1 FROM ft1 LIMIT 1;
10746 ?column? 
10747----------
10748        1
10749(1 row)
10750
10751-- Terminate the remote connection and wait for the termination to complete.
10752-- (If a cache flush happens, the remote connection might have already been
10753-- dropped; so code this step in a way that doesn't fail if no connection.)
10754DO $$ BEGIN
10755PERFORM pg_terminate_backend(pid, 180000) FROM pg_stat_activity
10756	WHERE application_name = 'fdw_retry_check';
10757END $$;
10758-- This query should detect the broken connection when starting new remote
10759-- transaction, reestablish new connection, and then succeed.
10760BEGIN;
10761SELECT 1 FROM ft1 LIMIT 1;
10762 ?column? 
10763----------
10764        1
10765(1 row)
10766
10767-- If we detect the broken connection when starting a new remote
10768-- subtransaction, we should fail instead of establishing a new connection.
10769-- Terminate the remote connection and wait for the termination to complete.
10770DO $$ BEGIN
10771PERFORM pg_terminate_backend(pid, 180000) FROM pg_stat_activity
10772	WHERE application_name = 'fdw_retry_check';
10773END $$;
10774SAVEPOINT s;
10775-- The text of the error might vary across platforms, so only show SQLSTATE.
10776\set VERBOSITY sqlstate
10777SELECT 1 FROM ft1 LIMIT 1;    -- should fail
10778ERROR:  08006
10779\set VERBOSITY default
10780COMMIT;
10781-- =============================================================================
10782-- test connection invalidation cases and postgres_fdw_get_connections function
10783-- =============================================================================
10784-- Let's ensure to close all the existing cached connections.
10785SELECT 1 FROM postgres_fdw_disconnect_all();
10786 ?column? 
10787----------
10788        1
10789(1 row)
10790
10791-- No cached connections, so no records should be output.
10792SELECT server_name FROM postgres_fdw_get_connections() ORDER BY 1;
10793 server_name 
10794-------------
10795(0 rows)
10796
10797-- This test case is for closing the connection in pgfdw_xact_callback
10798BEGIN;
10799-- Connection xact depth becomes 1 i.e. the connection is in midst of the xact.
10800SELECT 1 FROM ft1 LIMIT 1;
10801 ?column? 
10802----------
10803        1
10804(1 row)
10805
10806SELECT 1 FROM ft7 LIMIT 1;
10807 ?column? 
10808----------
10809        1
10810(1 row)
10811
10812-- List all the existing cached connections. loopback and loopback3 should be
10813-- output.
10814SELECT server_name FROM postgres_fdw_get_connections() ORDER BY 1;
10815 server_name 
10816-------------
10817 loopback
10818 loopback3
10819(2 rows)
10820
10821-- Connections are not closed at the end of the alter and drop statements.
10822-- That's because the connections are in midst of this xact,
10823-- they are just marked as invalid in pgfdw_inval_callback.
10824ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (ADD use_remote_estimate 'off');
10825DROP SERVER loopback3 CASCADE;
10826NOTICE:  drop cascades to 2 other objects
10827DETAIL:  drop cascades to user mapping for public on server loopback3
10828drop cascades to foreign table ft7
10829-- List all the existing cached connections. loopback and loopback3
10830-- should be output as invalid connections. Also the server name and user name
10831-- for loopback3 should be NULL because both server and user mapping were
10832-- dropped. In this test, the PIDs of remote backends can be gathered from
10833-- pg_stat_activity, and remote_backend_pid should match one of those PIDs.
10834SELECT server_name, user_name = CURRENT_USER as "user_name = CURRENT_USER",
10835  valid, used_in_xact, closed,
10836  remote_backend_pid = ANY(SELECT pid FROM pg_stat_activity
10837  WHERE backend_type = 'client backend' AND pid <> pg_backend_pid())
10838  as remote_backend_pid
10839  FROM postgres_fdw_get_connections() ORDER BY 1;
10840 server_name | user_name = CURRENT_USER | valid | used_in_xact | closed | remote_backend_pid 
10841-------------+--------------------------+-------+--------------+--------+--------------------
10842 loopback    | t                        | f     | t            |        | t
10843             |                          | f     | t            |        | t
10844(2 rows)
10845
10846-- The invalid connections get closed in pgfdw_xact_callback during commit.
10847COMMIT;
10848-- All cached connections were closed while committing above xact, so no
10849-- records should be output.
10850SELECT server_name FROM postgres_fdw_get_connections() ORDER BY 1;
10851 server_name 
10852-------------
10853(0 rows)
10854
10855-- =======================================================================
10856-- test postgres_fdw_disconnect and postgres_fdw_disconnect_all functions
10857-- =======================================================================
10858BEGIN;
10859-- Ensure to cache loopback connection.
10860SELECT 1 FROM ft1 LIMIT 1;
10861 ?column? 
10862----------
10863        1
10864(1 row)
10865
10866-- Ensure to cache loopback2 connection.
10867SELECT 1 FROM ft6 LIMIT 1;
10868 ?column? 
10869----------
10870        1
10871(1 row)
10872
10873-- List all the existing cached connections. loopback and loopback2 should be
10874-- output.
10875SELECT server_name FROM postgres_fdw_get_connections() ORDER BY 1;
10876 server_name 
10877-------------
10878 loopback
10879 loopback2
10880(2 rows)
10881
10882-- Issue a warning and return false as loopback connection is still in use and
10883-- can not be closed.
10884SELECT postgres_fdw_disconnect('loopback');
10885WARNING:  cannot close connection for server "loopback" because it is still in use
10886 postgres_fdw_disconnect 
10887-------------------------
10888 f
10889(1 row)
10890
10891-- List all the existing cached connections. loopback and loopback2 should be
10892-- output.
10893SELECT server_name FROM postgres_fdw_get_connections() ORDER BY 1;
10894 server_name 
10895-------------
10896 loopback
10897 loopback2
10898(2 rows)
10899
10900-- Return false as connections are still in use, warnings are issued.
10901-- But disable warnings temporarily because the order of them is not stable.
10902SET client_min_messages = 'ERROR';
10903SELECT postgres_fdw_disconnect_all();
10904 postgres_fdw_disconnect_all 
10905-----------------------------
10906 f
10907(1 row)
10908
10909RESET client_min_messages;
10910COMMIT;
10911-- Ensure that loopback2 connection is closed.
10912SELECT 1 FROM postgres_fdw_disconnect('loopback2');
10913 ?column? 
10914----------
10915        1
10916(1 row)
10917
10918SELECT server_name FROM postgres_fdw_get_connections() WHERE server_name = 'loopback2';
10919 server_name 
10920-------------
10921(0 rows)
10922
10923-- Return false as loopback2 connection is closed already.
10924SELECT postgres_fdw_disconnect('loopback2');
10925 postgres_fdw_disconnect 
10926-------------------------
10927 f
10928(1 row)
10929
10930-- Return an error as there is no foreign server with given name.
10931SELECT postgres_fdw_disconnect('unknownserver');
10932ERROR:  server "unknownserver" does not exist
10933-- Let's ensure to close all the existing cached connections.
10934SELECT 1 FROM postgres_fdw_disconnect_all();
10935 ?column? 
10936----------
10937        1
10938(1 row)
10939
10940-- No cached connections, so no records should be output.
10941SELECT server_name FROM postgres_fdw_get_connections() ORDER BY 1;
10942 server_name 
10943-------------
10944(0 rows)
10945
10946-- =============================================================================
10947-- test case for having multiple cached connections for a foreign server
10948-- =============================================================================
10949CREATE ROLE regress_multi_conn_user1 SUPERUSER;
10950CREATE ROLE regress_multi_conn_user2 SUPERUSER;
10951CREATE USER MAPPING FOR regress_multi_conn_user1 SERVER loopback;
10952CREATE USER MAPPING FOR regress_multi_conn_user2 SERVER loopback;
10953BEGIN;
10954-- Will cache loopback connection with user mapping for regress_multi_conn_user1
10955SET ROLE regress_multi_conn_user1;
10956SELECT 1 FROM ft1 LIMIT 1;
10957 ?column? 
10958----------
10959        1
10960(1 row)
10961
10962RESET ROLE;
10963-- Will cache loopback connection with user mapping for regress_multi_conn_user2
10964SET ROLE regress_multi_conn_user2;
10965SELECT 1 FROM ft1 LIMIT 1;
10966 ?column? 
10967----------
10968        1
10969(1 row)
10970
10971RESET ROLE;
10972-- Should output two connections for loopback server
10973SELECT server_name FROM postgres_fdw_get_connections() ORDER BY 1;
10974 server_name 
10975-------------
10976 loopback
10977 loopback
10978(2 rows)
10979
10980COMMIT;
10981-- Let's ensure to close all the existing cached connections.
10982SELECT 1 FROM postgres_fdw_disconnect_all();
10983 ?column? 
10984----------
10985        1
10986(1 row)
10987
10988-- No cached connections, so no records should be output.
10989SELECT server_name FROM postgres_fdw_get_connections() ORDER BY 1;
10990 server_name 
10991-------------
10992(0 rows)
10993
10994-- Clean up
10995DROP USER MAPPING FOR regress_multi_conn_user1 SERVER loopback;
10996DROP USER MAPPING FOR regress_multi_conn_user2 SERVER loopback;
10997DROP ROLE regress_multi_conn_user1;
10998DROP ROLE regress_multi_conn_user2;
10999-- ===================================================================
11000-- Test foreign server level option keep_connections
11001-- ===================================================================
11002-- By default, the connections associated with foreign server are cached i.e.
11003-- keep_connections option is on. Set it to off.
11004ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (keep_connections 'off');
11005-- connection to loopback server is closed at the end of xact
11006-- as keep_connections was set to off.
11007SELECT 1 FROM ft1 LIMIT 1;
11008 ?column? 
11009----------
11010        1
11011(1 row)
11012
11013-- No cached connections, so no records should be output.
11014SELECT server_name FROM postgres_fdw_get_connections() ORDER BY 1;
11015 server_name 
11016-------------
11017(0 rows)
11018
11019ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (SET keep_connections 'on');
11020-- ===================================================================
11021-- batch insert
11022-- ===================================================================
11023BEGIN;
11024CREATE SERVER batch10 FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER postgres_fdw OPTIONS( batch_size '10' );
11025SELECT count(*)
11026FROM pg_foreign_server
11027WHERE srvname = 'batch10'
11028AND srvoptions @> array['batch_size=10'];
11029 count 
11030-------
11031     1
11032(1 row)
11033
11034ALTER SERVER batch10 OPTIONS( SET batch_size '20' );
11035SELECT count(*)
11036FROM pg_foreign_server
11037WHERE srvname = 'batch10'
11038AND srvoptions @> array['batch_size=10'];
11039 count 
11040-------
11041     0
11042(1 row)
11043
11044SELECT count(*)
11045FROM pg_foreign_server
11046WHERE srvname = 'batch10'
11047AND srvoptions @> array['batch_size=20'];
11048 count 
11049-------
11050     1
11051(1 row)
11052
11053CREATE FOREIGN TABLE table30 ( x int ) SERVER batch10 OPTIONS ( batch_size '30' );
11054SELECT COUNT(*)
11055FROM pg_foreign_table
11056WHERE ftrelid = 'table30'::regclass
11057AND ftoptions @> array['batch_size=30'];
11058 count 
11059-------
11060     1
11061(1 row)
11062
11063ALTER FOREIGN TABLE table30 OPTIONS ( SET batch_size '40');
11064SELECT COUNT(*)
11065FROM pg_foreign_table
11066WHERE ftrelid = 'table30'::regclass
11067AND ftoptions @> array['batch_size=30'];
11068 count 
11069-------
11070     0
11071(1 row)
11072
11073SELECT COUNT(*)
11074FROM pg_foreign_table
11075WHERE ftrelid = 'table30'::regclass
11076AND ftoptions @> array['batch_size=40'];
11077 count 
11078-------
11079     1
11080(1 row)
11081
11082ROLLBACK;
11083CREATE TABLE batch_table ( x int );
11084CREATE FOREIGN TABLE ftable ( x int ) SERVER loopback OPTIONS ( table_name 'batch_table', batch_size '10' );
11085EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) INSERT INTO ftable SELECT * FROM generate_series(1, 10) i;
11086                         QUERY PLAN                          
11087-------------------------------------------------------------
11088 Insert on public.ftable
11089   Remote SQL: INSERT INTO public.batch_table(x) VALUES ($1)
11090   Batch Size: 10
11091   ->  Function Scan on pg_catalog.generate_series i
11092         Output: i.i
11093         Function Call: generate_series(1, 10)
11094(6 rows)
11095
11096INSERT INTO ftable SELECT * FROM generate_series(1, 10) i;
11097INSERT INTO ftable SELECT * FROM generate_series(11, 31) i;
11098INSERT INTO ftable VALUES (32);
11099INSERT INTO ftable VALUES (33), (34);
11100SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ftable;
11101 count 
11102-------
11103    34
11104(1 row)
11105
11106TRUNCATE batch_table;
11107DROP FOREIGN TABLE ftable;
11108-- Disable batch insert
11109CREATE FOREIGN TABLE ftable ( x int ) SERVER loopback OPTIONS ( table_name 'batch_table', batch_size '1' );
11110EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) INSERT INTO ftable VALUES (1), (2);
11111                         QUERY PLAN                          
11112-------------------------------------------------------------
11113 Insert on public.ftable
11114   Remote SQL: INSERT INTO public.batch_table(x) VALUES ($1)
11115   Batch Size: 1
11116   ->  Values Scan on "*VALUES*"
11117         Output: "*VALUES*".column1
11118(5 rows)
11119
11120INSERT INTO ftable VALUES (1), (2);
11121SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ftable;
11122 count 
11123-------
11124     2
11125(1 row)
11126
11127-- Disable batch inserting into foreign tables with BEFORE ROW INSERT triggers
11128-- even if the batch_size option is enabled.
11129ALTER FOREIGN TABLE ftable OPTIONS ( SET batch_size '10' );
11130CREATE TRIGGER trig_row_before BEFORE INSERT ON ftable
11131FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_data(23,'skidoo');
11132EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF) INSERT INTO ftable VALUES (3), (4);
11133                         QUERY PLAN                          
11134-------------------------------------------------------------
11135 Insert on public.ftable
11136   Remote SQL: INSERT INTO public.batch_table(x) VALUES ($1)
11137   Batch Size: 1
11138   ->  Values Scan on "*VALUES*"
11139         Output: "*VALUES*".column1
11140(5 rows)
11141
11142INSERT INTO ftable VALUES (3), (4);
11143NOTICE:  trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW INSERT ON ftable
11144NOTICE:  NEW: (3)
11145NOTICE:  trig_row_before(23, skidoo) BEFORE ROW INSERT ON ftable
11146NOTICE:  NEW: (4)
11147SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ftable;
11148 count 
11149-------
11150     4
11151(1 row)
11152
11153-- Clean up
11154DROP TRIGGER trig_row_before ON ftable;
11155DROP FOREIGN TABLE ftable;
11156DROP TABLE batch_table;
11157-- Use partitioning
11158CREATE TABLE batch_table ( x int ) PARTITION BY HASH (x);
11159CREATE TABLE batch_table_p0 (LIKE batch_table);
11160CREATE FOREIGN TABLE batch_table_p0f
11161	PARTITION OF batch_table
11162	FOR VALUES WITH (MODULUS 3, REMAINDER 0)
11163	SERVER loopback
11164	OPTIONS (table_name 'batch_table_p0', batch_size '10');
11165CREATE TABLE batch_table_p1 (LIKE batch_table);
11166CREATE FOREIGN TABLE batch_table_p1f
11167	PARTITION OF batch_table
11168	FOR VALUES WITH (MODULUS 3, REMAINDER 1)
11169	SERVER loopback
11170	OPTIONS (table_name 'batch_table_p1', batch_size '1');
11171CREATE TABLE batch_table_p2
11172	PARTITION OF batch_table
11173	FOR VALUES WITH (MODULUS 3, REMAINDER 2);
11174INSERT INTO batch_table SELECT * FROM generate_series(1, 66) i;
11175SELECT COUNT(*) FROM batch_table;
11176 count 
11177-------
11178    66
11179(1 row)
11180
11181-- Clean up
11182DROP TABLE batch_table;
11183DROP TABLE batch_table_p0;
11184DROP TABLE batch_table_p1;
11185-- Check that batched mode also works for some inserts made during
11186-- cross-partition updates
11187CREATE TABLE batch_cp_upd_test (a int) PARTITION BY LIST (a);
11188CREATE TABLE batch_cp_upd_test1 (LIKE batch_cp_upd_test);
11189CREATE FOREIGN TABLE batch_cp_upd_test1_f
11190	PARTITION OF batch_cp_upd_test
11191	FOR VALUES IN (1)
11192	SERVER loopback
11193	OPTIONS (table_name 'batch_cp_upd_test1', batch_size '10');
11194CREATE TABLE batch_cp_upd_test2 PARTITION OF batch_cp_upd_test
11195	FOR VALUES IN (2);
11196CREATE TABLE batch_cp_upd_test3 (LIKE batch_cp_upd_test);
11197CREATE FOREIGN TABLE batch_cp_upd_test3_f
11198	PARTITION OF batch_cp_upd_test
11199	FOR VALUES IN (3)
11200	SERVER loopback
11201	OPTIONS (table_name 'batch_cp_upd_test3', batch_size '1');
11202-- Create statement triggers on remote tables that "log" any INSERTs
11203-- performed on them.
11204CREATE TABLE cmdlog (cmd text);
11205CREATE FUNCTION log_stmt() RETURNS TRIGGER LANGUAGE plpgsql AS $$
11206	BEGIN INSERT INTO public.cmdlog VALUES (TG_OP || ' on ' || TG_RELNAME); RETURN NULL; END;
11207$$;
11208CREATE TRIGGER stmt_trig AFTER INSERT ON batch_cp_upd_test1
11209	FOR EACH STATEMENT EXECUTE FUNCTION log_stmt();
11210CREATE TRIGGER stmt_trig AFTER INSERT ON batch_cp_upd_test3
11211	FOR EACH STATEMENT EXECUTE FUNCTION log_stmt();
11212-- This update moves rows from the local partition 'batch_cp_upd_test2' to the
11213-- foreign partition 'batch_cp_upd_test1', one that has insert batching
11214-- enabled, so a single INSERT for both rows.
11215INSERT INTO batch_cp_upd_test VALUES (2), (2);
11216UPDATE batch_cp_upd_test t SET a = 1 FROM (VALUES (1), (2)) s(a) WHERE t.a = s.a AND s.a = 2;
11217-- This one moves rows from the local partition 'batch_cp_upd_test2' to the
11218-- foreign partition 'batch_cp_upd_test2', one that has insert batching
11219-- disabled, so separate INSERTs for the two rows.
11220INSERT INTO batch_cp_upd_test VALUES (2), (2);
11221UPDATE batch_cp_upd_test t SET a = 3 FROM (VALUES (1), (2)) s(a) WHERE t.a = s.a AND s.a = 2;
11222SELECT tableoid::regclass, * FROM batch_cp_upd_test ORDER BY 1;
11223       tableoid       | a 
11224----------------------+---
11225 batch_cp_upd_test1_f | 1
11226 batch_cp_upd_test1_f | 1
11227 batch_cp_upd_test3_f | 3
11228 batch_cp_upd_test3_f | 3
11229(4 rows)
11230
11231-- Should see 1 INSERT on batch_cp_upd_test1 and 2 on batch_cp_upd_test3 as
11232-- described above.
11233SELECT * FROM cmdlog ORDER BY 1;
11234             cmd              
11235------------------------------
11236 INSERT on batch_cp_upd_test1
11237 INSERT on batch_cp_upd_test3
11238 INSERT on batch_cp_upd_test3
11239(3 rows)
11240
11241-- Clean up
11242DROP TABLE batch_cp_upd_test;
11243DROP TABLE batch_cp_upd_test1;
11244DROP TABLE batch_cp_upd_test3;
11245DROP TABLE cmdlog;
11246DROP FUNCTION log_stmt();
11247-- Use partitioning
11248ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (ADD batch_size '10');
11249CREATE TABLE batch_table ( x int, field1 text, field2 text) PARTITION BY HASH (x);
11250CREATE TABLE batch_table_p0 (LIKE batch_table);
11251ALTER TABLE batch_table_p0 ADD CONSTRAINT p0_pkey PRIMARY KEY (x);
11252CREATE FOREIGN TABLE batch_table_p0f
11253	PARTITION OF batch_table
11254	FOR VALUES WITH (MODULUS 2, REMAINDER 0)
11255	SERVER loopback
11256	OPTIONS (table_name 'batch_table_p0');
11257CREATE TABLE batch_table_p1 (LIKE batch_table);
11258ALTER TABLE batch_table_p1 ADD CONSTRAINT p1_pkey PRIMARY KEY (x);
11259CREATE FOREIGN TABLE batch_table_p1f
11260	PARTITION OF batch_table
11261	FOR VALUES WITH (MODULUS 2, REMAINDER 1)
11262	SERVER loopback
11263	OPTIONS (table_name 'batch_table_p1');
11264INSERT INTO batch_table SELECT i, 'test'||i, 'test'|| i FROM generate_series(1, 50) i;
11265SELECT COUNT(*) FROM batch_table;
11266 count 
11267-------
11268    50
11269(1 row)
11270
11271SELECT * FROM batch_table ORDER BY x;
11272 x  | field1 | field2 
11273----+--------+--------
11274  1 | test1  | test1
11275  2 | test2  | test2
11276  3 | test3  | test3
11277  4 | test4  | test4
11278  5 | test5  | test5
11279  6 | test6  | test6
11280  7 | test7  | test7
11281  8 | test8  | test8
11282  9 | test9  | test9
11283 10 | test10 | test10
11284 11 | test11 | test11
11285 12 | test12 | test12
11286 13 | test13 | test13
11287 14 | test14 | test14
11288 15 | test15 | test15
11289 16 | test16 | test16
11290 17 | test17 | test17
11291 18 | test18 | test18
11292 19 | test19 | test19
11293 20 | test20 | test20
11294 21 | test21 | test21
11295 22 | test22 | test22
11296 23 | test23 | test23
11297 24 | test24 | test24
11298 25 | test25 | test25
11299 26 | test26 | test26
11300 27 | test27 | test27
11301 28 | test28 | test28
11302 29 | test29 | test29
11303 30 | test30 | test30
11304 31 | test31 | test31
11305 32 | test32 | test32
11306 33 | test33 | test33
11307 34 | test34 | test34
11308 35 | test35 | test35
11309 36 | test36 | test36
11310 37 | test37 | test37
11311 38 | test38 | test38
11312 39 | test39 | test39
11313 40 | test40 | test40
11314 41 | test41 | test41
11315 42 | test42 | test42
11316 43 | test43 | test43
11317 44 | test44 | test44
11318 45 | test45 | test45
11319 46 | test46 | test46
11320 47 | test47 | test47
11321 48 | test48 | test48
11322 49 | test49 | test49
11323 50 | test50 | test50
11324(50 rows)
11325
11326-- Clean up
11327DROP TABLE batch_table;
11328DROP TABLE batch_table_p0;
11329DROP TABLE batch_table_p1;
11330ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (DROP batch_size);
11331-- Test that pending inserts are handled properly when needed
11332CREATE TABLE batch_table (a text, b int);
11333CREATE FOREIGN TABLE ftable (a text, b int)
11334	SERVER loopback
11335	OPTIONS (table_name 'batch_table', batch_size '2');
11336CREATE TABLE ltable (a text, b int);
11337CREATE FUNCTION ftable_rowcount_trigf() RETURNS trigger LANGUAGE plpgsql AS
11338$$
11339begin
11340	raise notice '%: there are % rows in ftable',
11341		TG_NAME, (SELECT count(*) FROM ftable);
11342	if TG_OP = 'DELETE' then
11343		return OLD;
11344	else
11345		return NEW;
11346	end if;
11347end;
11348$$;
11349CREATE TRIGGER ftable_rowcount_trigger
11350BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE OR DELETE ON ltable
11351FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE ftable_rowcount_trigf();
11352WITH t AS (
11353	INSERT INTO ltable VALUES ('AAA', 42), ('BBB', 42) RETURNING *
11354)
11355INSERT INTO ftable SELECT * FROM t;
11356NOTICE:  ftable_rowcount_trigger: there are 0 rows in ftable
11357NOTICE:  ftable_rowcount_trigger: there are 1 rows in ftable
11358SELECT * FROM ltable;
11359  a  | b  
11360-----+----
11361 AAA | 42
11362 BBB | 42
11363(2 rows)
11364
11365SELECT * FROM ftable;
11366  a  | b  
11367-----+----
11368 AAA | 42
11369 BBB | 42
11370(2 rows)
11371
11372DELETE FROM ftable;
11373WITH t AS (
11374	UPDATE ltable SET b = b + 100 RETURNING *
11375)
11376INSERT INTO ftable SELECT * FROM t;
11377NOTICE:  ftable_rowcount_trigger: there are 0 rows in ftable
11378NOTICE:  ftable_rowcount_trigger: there are 1 rows in ftable
11379SELECT * FROM ltable;
11380  a  |  b  
11381-----+-----
11382 AAA | 142
11383 BBB | 142
11384(2 rows)
11385
11386SELECT * FROM ftable;
11387  a  |  b  
11388-----+-----
11389 AAA | 142
11390 BBB | 142
11391(2 rows)
11392
11393DELETE FROM ftable;
11394WITH t AS (
11395	DELETE FROM ltable RETURNING *
11396)
11397INSERT INTO ftable SELECT * FROM t;
11398NOTICE:  ftable_rowcount_trigger: there are 0 rows in ftable
11399NOTICE:  ftable_rowcount_trigger: there are 1 rows in ftable
11400SELECT * FROM ltable;
11401 a | b 
11402---+---
11403(0 rows)
11404
11405SELECT * FROM ftable;
11406  a  |  b  
11407-----+-----
11408 AAA | 142
11409 BBB | 142
11410(2 rows)
11411
11412DELETE FROM ftable;
11413-- Clean up
11414DROP FOREIGN TABLE ftable;
11415DROP TABLE batch_table;
11416DROP TRIGGER ftable_rowcount_trigger ON ltable;
11417DROP TABLE ltable;
11418CREATE TABLE parent (a text, b int) PARTITION BY LIST (a);
11419CREATE TABLE batch_table (a text, b int);
11420CREATE FOREIGN TABLE ftable
11421	PARTITION OF parent
11422	FOR VALUES IN ('AAA')
11423	SERVER loopback
11424	OPTIONS (table_name 'batch_table', batch_size '2');
11425CREATE TABLE ltable
11426	PARTITION OF parent
11427	FOR VALUES IN ('BBB');
11428CREATE TRIGGER ftable_rowcount_trigger
11429BEFORE INSERT ON ltable
11430FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE ftable_rowcount_trigf();
11431INSERT INTO parent VALUES ('AAA', 42), ('BBB', 42), ('AAA', 42), ('BBB', 42);
11432NOTICE:  ftable_rowcount_trigger: there are 1 rows in ftable
11433NOTICE:  ftable_rowcount_trigger: there are 2 rows in ftable
11434SELECT tableoid::regclass, * FROM parent;
11435 tableoid |  a  | b  
11436----------+-----+----
11437 ftable   | AAA | 42
11438 ftable   | AAA | 42
11439 ltable   | BBB | 42
11440 ltable   | BBB | 42
11441(4 rows)
11442
11443-- Clean up
11444DROP FOREIGN TABLE ftable;
11445DROP TABLE batch_table;
11446DROP TRIGGER ftable_rowcount_trigger ON ltable;
11447DROP TABLE ltable;
11448DROP TABLE parent;
11449DROP FUNCTION ftable_rowcount_trigf;
11450-- ===================================================================
11451-- test asynchronous execution
11452-- ===================================================================
11453ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (DROP extensions);
11454ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (ADD async_capable 'true');
11455ALTER SERVER loopback2 OPTIONS (ADD async_capable 'true');
11456CREATE TABLE async_pt (a int, b int, c text) PARTITION BY RANGE (a);
11457CREATE TABLE base_tbl1 (a int, b int, c text);
11458CREATE TABLE base_tbl2 (a int, b int, c text);
11459CREATE FOREIGN TABLE async_p1 PARTITION OF async_pt FOR VALUES FROM (1000) TO (2000)
11460  SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'base_tbl1');
11461CREATE FOREIGN TABLE async_p2 PARTITION OF async_pt FOR VALUES FROM (2000) TO (3000)
11462  SERVER loopback2 OPTIONS (table_name 'base_tbl2');
11463INSERT INTO async_p1 SELECT 1000 + i, i, to_char(i, 'FM0000') FROM generate_series(0, 999, 5) i;
11464INSERT INTO async_p2 SELECT 2000 + i, i, to_char(i, 'FM0000') FROM generate_series(0, 999, 5) i;
11465ANALYZE async_pt;
11466-- simple queries
11467CREATE TABLE result_tbl (a int, b int, c text);
11468EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
11469INSERT INTO result_tbl SELECT * FROM async_pt WHERE b % 100 = 0;
11470                                       QUERY PLAN                                       
11471----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11472 Insert on public.result_tbl
11473   ->  Append
11474         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p1 async_pt_1
11475               Output: async_pt_1.a, async_pt_1.b, async_pt_1.c
11476               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1 WHERE (((b % 100) = 0))
11477         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p2 async_pt_2
11478               Output: async_pt_2.a, async_pt_2.b, async_pt_2.c
11479               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl2 WHERE (((b % 100) = 0))
11480(8 rows)
11481
11482INSERT INTO result_tbl SELECT * FROM async_pt WHERE b % 100 = 0;
11483SELECT * FROM result_tbl ORDER BY a;
11484  a   |  b  |  c   
11485------+-----+------
11486 1000 |   0 | 0000
11487 1100 | 100 | 0100
11488 1200 | 200 | 0200
11489 1300 | 300 | 0300
11490 1400 | 400 | 0400
11491 1500 | 500 | 0500
11492 1600 | 600 | 0600
11493 1700 | 700 | 0700
11494 1800 | 800 | 0800
11495 1900 | 900 | 0900
11496 2000 |   0 | 0000
11497 2100 | 100 | 0100
11498 2200 | 200 | 0200
11499 2300 | 300 | 0300
11500 2400 | 400 | 0400
11501 2500 | 500 | 0500
11502 2600 | 600 | 0600
11503 2700 | 700 | 0700
11504 2800 | 800 | 0800
11505 2900 | 900 | 0900
11506(20 rows)
11507
11508DELETE FROM result_tbl;
11509EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
11510INSERT INTO result_tbl SELECT * FROM async_pt WHERE b === 505;
11511                           QUERY PLAN                           
11512----------------------------------------------------------------
11513 Insert on public.result_tbl
11514   ->  Append
11515         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p1 async_pt_1
11516               Output: async_pt_1.a, async_pt_1.b, async_pt_1.c
11517               Filter: (async_pt_1.b === 505)
11518               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1
11519         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p2 async_pt_2
11520               Output: async_pt_2.a, async_pt_2.b, async_pt_2.c
11521               Filter: (async_pt_2.b === 505)
11522               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl2
11523(10 rows)
11524
11525INSERT INTO result_tbl SELECT * FROM async_pt WHERE b === 505;
11526SELECT * FROM result_tbl ORDER BY a;
11527  a   |  b  |  c   
11528------+-----+------
11529 1505 | 505 | 0505
11530 2505 | 505 | 0505
11531(2 rows)
11532
11533DELETE FROM result_tbl;
11534EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
11535INSERT INTO result_tbl SELECT a, b, 'AAA' || c FROM async_pt WHERE b === 505;
11536                                   QUERY PLAN                                    
11537---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11538 Insert on public.result_tbl
11539   ->  Append
11540         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p1 async_pt_1
11541               Output: async_pt_1.a, async_pt_1.b, ('AAA'::text || async_pt_1.c)
11542               Filter: (async_pt_1.b === 505)
11543               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1
11544         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p2 async_pt_2
11545               Output: async_pt_2.a, async_pt_2.b, ('AAA'::text || async_pt_2.c)
11546               Filter: (async_pt_2.b === 505)
11547               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl2
11548(10 rows)
11549
11550INSERT INTO result_tbl SELECT a, b, 'AAA' || c FROM async_pt WHERE b === 505;
11551SELECT * FROM result_tbl ORDER BY a;
11552  a   |  b  |    c    
11553------+-----+---------
11554 1505 | 505 | AAA0505
11555 2505 | 505 | AAA0505
11556(2 rows)
11557
11558DELETE FROM result_tbl;
11559-- Test error handling, if accessing one of the foreign partitions errors out
11560CREATE FOREIGN TABLE async_p_broken PARTITION OF async_pt FOR VALUES FROM (10000) TO (10001)
11561  SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'non_existent_table');
11562SELECT * FROM async_pt;
11563ERROR:  relation "public.non_existent_table" does not exist
11564CONTEXT:  remote SQL command: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.non_existent_table
11565DROP FOREIGN TABLE async_p_broken;
11566-- Check case where multiple partitions use the same connection
11567CREATE TABLE base_tbl3 (a int, b int, c text);
11568CREATE FOREIGN TABLE async_p3 PARTITION OF async_pt FOR VALUES FROM (3000) TO (4000)
11569  SERVER loopback2 OPTIONS (table_name 'base_tbl3');
11570INSERT INTO async_p3 SELECT 3000 + i, i, to_char(i, 'FM0000') FROM generate_series(0, 999, 5) i;
11571ANALYZE async_pt;
11572EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
11573INSERT INTO result_tbl SELECT * FROM async_pt WHERE b === 505;
11574                           QUERY PLAN                           
11575----------------------------------------------------------------
11576 Insert on public.result_tbl
11577   ->  Append
11578         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p1 async_pt_1
11579               Output: async_pt_1.a, async_pt_1.b, async_pt_1.c
11580               Filter: (async_pt_1.b === 505)
11581               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1
11582         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p2 async_pt_2
11583               Output: async_pt_2.a, async_pt_2.b, async_pt_2.c
11584               Filter: (async_pt_2.b === 505)
11585               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl2
11586         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p3 async_pt_3
11587               Output: async_pt_3.a, async_pt_3.b, async_pt_3.c
11588               Filter: (async_pt_3.b === 505)
11589               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl3
11590(14 rows)
11591
11592INSERT INTO result_tbl SELECT * FROM async_pt WHERE b === 505;
11593SELECT * FROM result_tbl ORDER BY a;
11594  a   |  b  |  c   
11595------+-----+------
11596 1505 | 505 | 0505
11597 2505 | 505 | 0505
11598 3505 | 505 | 0505
11599(3 rows)
11600
11601DELETE FROM result_tbl;
11602DROP FOREIGN TABLE async_p3;
11603DROP TABLE base_tbl3;
11604-- Check case where the partitioned table has local/remote partitions
11605CREATE TABLE async_p3 PARTITION OF async_pt FOR VALUES FROM (3000) TO (4000);
11606INSERT INTO async_p3 SELECT 3000 + i, i, to_char(i, 'FM0000') FROM generate_series(0, 999, 5) i;
11607ANALYZE async_pt;
11608EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
11609INSERT INTO result_tbl SELECT * FROM async_pt WHERE b === 505;
11610                           QUERY PLAN                           
11611----------------------------------------------------------------
11612 Insert on public.result_tbl
11613   ->  Append
11614         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p1 async_pt_1
11615               Output: async_pt_1.a, async_pt_1.b, async_pt_1.c
11616               Filter: (async_pt_1.b === 505)
11617               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1
11618         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p2 async_pt_2
11619               Output: async_pt_2.a, async_pt_2.b, async_pt_2.c
11620               Filter: (async_pt_2.b === 505)
11621               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl2
11622         ->  Seq Scan on public.async_p3 async_pt_3
11623               Output: async_pt_3.a, async_pt_3.b, async_pt_3.c
11624               Filter: (async_pt_3.b === 505)
11625(13 rows)
11626
11627INSERT INTO result_tbl SELECT * FROM async_pt WHERE b === 505;
11628SELECT * FROM result_tbl ORDER BY a;
11629  a   |  b  |  c   
11630------+-----+------
11631 1505 | 505 | 0505
11632 2505 | 505 | 0505
11633 3505 | 505 | 0505
11634(3 rows)
11635
11636DELETE FROM result_tbl;
11637-- partitionwise joins
11638SET enable_partitionwise_join TO true;
11639CREATE TABLE join_tbl (a1 int, b1 int, c1 text, a2 int, b2 int, c2 text);
11640EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
11641INSERT INTO join_tbl SELECT * FROM async_pt t1, async_pt t2 WHERE t1.a = t2.a AND t1.b = t2.b AND t1.b % 100 = 0;
11642                                                                                           QUERY PLAN                                                                                            
11643-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11644 Insert on public.join_tbl
11645   ->  Append
11646         ->  Async Foreign Scan
11647               Output: t1_1.a, t1_1.b, t1_1.c, t2_1.a, t2_1.b, t2_1.c
11648               Relations: (public.async_p1 t1_1) INNER JOIN (public.async_p1 t2_1)
11649               Remote SQL: SELECT r5.a, r5.b, r5.c, r8.a, r8.b, r8.c FROM (public.base_tbl1 r5 INNER JOIN public.base_tbl1 r8 ON (((r5.a = r8.a)) AND ((r5.b = r8.b)) AND (((r5.b % 100) = 0))))
11650         ->  Async Foreign Scan
11651               Output: t1_2.a, t1_2.b, t1_2.c, t2_2.a, t2_2.b, t2_2.c
11652               Relations: (public.async_p2 t1_2) INNER JOIN (public.async_p2 t2_2)
11653               Remote SQL: SELECT r6.a, r6.b, r6.c, r9.a, r9.b, r9.c FROM (public.base_tbl2 r6 INNER JOIN public.base_tbl2 r9 ON (((r6.a = r9.a)) AND ((r6.b = r9.b)) AND (((r6.b % 100) = 0))))
11654         ->  Hash Join
11655               Output: t1_3.a, t1_3.b, t1_3.c, t2_3.a, t2_3.b, t2_3.c
11656               Hash Cond: ((t2_3.a = t1_3.a) AND (t2_3.b = t1_3.b))
11657               ->  Seq Scan on public.async_p3 t2_3
11658                     Output: t2_3.a, t2_3.b, t2_3.c
11659               ->  Hash
11660                     Output: t1_3.a, t1_3.b, t1_3.c
11661                     ->  Seq Scan on public.async_p3 t1_3
11662                           Output: t1_3.a, t1_3.b, t1_3.c
11663                           Filter: ((t1_3.b % 100) = 0)
11664(20 rows)
11665
11666INSERT INTO join_tbl SELECT * FROM async_pt t1, async_pt t2 WHERE t1.a = t2.a AND t1.b = t2.b AND t1.b % 100 = 0;
11667SELECT * FROM join_tbl ORDER BY a1;
11668  a1  | b1  |  c1  |  a2  | b2  |  c2  
11669------+-----+------+------+-----+------
11670 1000 |   0 | 0000 | 1000 |   0 | 0000
11671 1100 | 100 | 0100 | 1100 | 100 | 0100
11672 1200 | 200 | 0200 | 1200 | 200 | 0200
11673 1300 | 300 | 0300 | 1300 | 300 | 0300
11674 1400 | 400 | 0400 | 1400 | 400 | 0400
11675 1500 | 500 | 0500 | 1500 | 500 | 0500
11676 1600 | 600 | 0600 | 1600 | 600 | 0600
11677 1700 | 700 | 0700 | 1700 | 700 | 0700
11678 1800 | 800 | 0800 | 1800 | 800 | 0800
11679 1900 | 900 | 0900 | 1900 | 900 | 0900
11680 2000 |   0 | 0000 | 2000 |   0 | 0000
11681 2100 | 100 | 0100 | 2100 | 100 | 0100
11682 2200 | 200 | 0200 | 2200 | 200 | 0200
11683 2300 | 300 | 0300 | 2300 | 300 | 0300
11684 2400 | 400 | 0400 | 2400 | 400 | 0400
11685 2500 | 500 | 0500 | 2500 | 500 | 0500
11686 2600 | 600 | 0600 | 2600 | 600 | 0600
11687 2700 | 700 | 0700 | 2700 | 700 | 0700
11688 2800 | 800 | 0800 | 2800 | 800 | 0800
11689 2900 | 900 | 0900 | 2900 | 900 | 0900
11690 3000 |   0 | 0000 | 3000 |   0 | 0000
11691 3100 | 100 | 0100 | 3100 | 100 | 0100
11692 3200 | 200 | 0200 | 3200 | 200 | 0200
11693 3300 | 300 | 0300 | 3300 | 300 | 0300
11694 3400 | 400 | 0400 | 3400 | 400 | 0400
11695 3500 | 500 | 0500 | 3500 | 500 | 0500
11696 3600 | 600 | 0600 | 3600 | 600 | 0600
11697 3700 | 700 | 0700 | 3700 | 700 | 0700
11698 3800 | 800 | 0800 | 3800 | 800 | 0800
11699 3900 | 900 | 0900 | 3900 | 900 | 0900
11700(30 rows)
11701
11702DELETE FROM join_tbl;
11703EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
11704INSERT INTO join_tbl SELECT t1.a, t1.b, 'AAA' || t1.c, t2.a, t2.b, 'AAA' || t2.c FROM async_pt t1, async_pt t2 WHERE t1.a = t2.a AND t1.b = t2.b AND t1.b % 100 = 0;
11705                                                                                           QUERY PLAN                                                                                            
11706-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11707 Insert on public.join_tbl
11708   ->  Append
11709         ->  Async Foreign Scan
11710               Output: t1_1.a, t1_1.b, ('AAA'::text || t1_1.c), t2_1.a, t2_1.b, ('AAA'::text || t2_1.c)
11711               Relations: (public.async_p1 t1_1) INNER JOIN (public.async_p1 t2_1)
11712               Remote SQL: SELECT r5.a, r5.b, r5.c, r8.a, r8.b, r8.c FROM (public.base_tbl1 r5 INNER JOIN public.base_tbl1 r8 ON (((r5.a = r8.a)) AND ((r5.b = r8.b)) AND (((r5.b % 100) = 0))))
11713         ->  Async Foreign Scan
11714               Output: t1_2.a, t1_2.b, ('AAA'::text || t1_2.c), t2_2.a, t2_2.b, ('AAA'::text || t2_2.c)
11715               Relations: (public.async_p2 t1_2) INNER JOIN (public.async_p2 t2_2)
11716               Remote SQL: SELECT r6.a, r6.b, r6.c, r9.a, r9.b, r9.c FROM (public.base_tbl2 r6 INNER JOIN public.base_tbl2 r9 ON (((r6.a = r9.a)) AND ((r6.b = r9.b)) AND (((r6.b % 100) = 0))))
11717         ->  Hash Join
11718               Output: t1_3.a, t1_3.b, ('AAA'::text || t1_3.c), t2_3.a, t2_3.b, ('AAA'::text || t2_3.c)
11719               Hash Cond: ((t2_3.a = t1_3.a) AND (t2_3.b = t1_3.b))
11720               ->  Seq Scan on public.async_p3 t2_3
11721                     Output: t2_3.a, t2_3.b, t2_3.c
11722               ->  Hash
11723                     Output: t1_3.a, t1_3.b, t1_3.c
11724                     ->  Seq Scan on public.async_p3 t1_3
11725                           Output: t1_3.a, t1_3.b, t1_3.c
11726                           Filter: ((t1_3.b % 100) = 0)
11727(20 rows)
11728
11729INSERT INTO join_tbl SELECT t1.a, t1.b, 'AAA' || t1.c, t2.a, t2.b, 'AAA' || t2.c FROM async_pt t1, async_pt t2 WHERE t1.a = t2.a AND t1.b = t2.b AND t1.b % 100 = 0;

11732------+-----+---------+------+-----+---------

11746 2300 | 300 | AAA0300 | 2300 | 300 | AAA0300
11747 2400 | 400 | AAA0400 | 2400 | 400 | AAA0400

11755 3200 | 200 | AAA0200 | 3200 | 200 | AAA0200
11756 3300 | 300 | AAA0300 | 3300 | 300 | AAA0300

11764
11765DELETE FROM join_tbl;
11766RESET enable_partitionwise_join;
11767-- Test rescan of an async Append node with do_exec_prune=false
11768SET enable_hashjoin TO false;
11769EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
11770INSERT INTO join_tbl SELECT * FROM async_p1 t1, async_pt t2 WHERE t1.a = t2.a AND t1.b = t2.b AND t1.b % 100 = 0;
11771                                       QUERY PLAN                                       
11772----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11773 Insert on public.join_tbl
11774   ->  Nested Loop
11775         Output: t1.a, t1.b, t1.c, t2.a, t2.b, t2.c
11776         Join Filter: ((t1.a = t2.a) AND (t1.b = t2.b))
11777         ->  Foreign Scan on public.async_p1 t1
11778               Output: t1.a, t1.b, t1.c
11779               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1 WHERE (((b % 100) = 0))
11780         ->  Append
11781               ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p1 t2_1
11782                     Output: t2_1.a, t2_1.b, t2_1.c
11783                     Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1
11784               ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p2 t2_2
11785                     Output: t2_2.a, t2_2.b, t2_2.c
11786                     Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl2
11787               ->  Seq Scan on public.async_p3 t2_3
11788                     Output: t2_3.a, t2_3.b, t2_3.c
11789(16 rows)
11790
11791INSERT INTO join_tbl SELECT * FROM async_p1 t1, async_pt t2 WHERE t1.a = t2.a AND t1.b = t2.b AND t1.b % 100 = 0;
11792SELECT * FROM join_tbl ORDER BY a1;
11793  a1  | b1  |  c1  |  a2  | b2  |  c2  
11794------+-----+------+------+-----+------
11795 1000 |   0 | 0000 | 1000 |   0 | 0000
11796 1100 | 100 | 0100 | 1100 | 100 | 0100
11797 1200 | 200 | 0200 | 1200 | 200 | 0200
11798 1300 | 300 | 0300 | 1300 | 300 | 0300
11799 1400 | 400 | 0400 | 1400 | 400 | 0400
11800 1500 | 500 | 0500 | 1500 | 500 | 0500
11801 1600 | 600 | 0600 | 1600 | 600 | 0600
11802 1700 | 700 | 0700 | 1700 | 700 | 0700
11803 1800 | 800 | 0800 | 1800 | 800 | 0800
11804 1900 | 900 | 0900 | 1900 | 900 | 0900
11805(10 rows)
11806
11807DELETE FROM join_tbl;
11808RESET enable_hashjoin;
11809-- Test interaction of async execution with plan-time partition pruning
11810EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
11811SELECT * FROM async_pt WHERE a < 3000;
11812                                 QUERY PLAN                                  
11813-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
11814 Append
11815   ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p1 async_pt_1
11816         Output: async_pt_1.a, async_pt_1.b, async_pt_1.c
11817         Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1 WHERE ((a < 3000))
11818   ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p2 async_pt_2
11819         Output: async_pt_2.a, async_pt_2.b, async_pt_2.c
11820         Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl2 WHERE ((a < 3000))
11821(7 rows)
11822
11823EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
11824SELECT * FROM async_pt WHERE a < 2000;
11825                              QUERY PLAN                               
11826-----------------------------------------------------------------------
11827 Foreign Scan on public.async_p1 async_pt
11828   Output: async_pt.a, async_pt.b, async_pt.c
11829   Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1 WHERE ((a < 2000))
11830(3 rows)
11831
11832-- Test interaction of async execution with run-time partition pruning
11833SET plan_cache_mode TO force_generic_plan;
11834PREPARE async_pt_query (int, int) AS
11835  INSERT INTO result_tbl SELECT * FROM async_pt WHERE a < $1 AND b === $2;
11836EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
11837EXECUTE async_pt_query (3000, 505);
11838                                        QUERY PLAN                                        
11839------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11840 Insert on public.result_tbl
11841   ->  Append
11842         Subplans Removed: 1
11843         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p1 async_pt_1
11844               Output: async_pt_1.a, async_pt_1.b, async_pt_1.c
11845               Filter: (async_pt_1.b === $2)
11846               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1 WHERE ((a < $1::integer))
11847         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p2 async_pt_2
11848               Output: async_pt_2.a, async_pt_2.b, async_pt_2.c
11849               Filter: (async_pt_2.b === $2)
11850               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl2 WHERE ((a < $1::integer))
11851(11 rows)
11852
11853EXECUTE async_pt_query (3000, 505);
11854SELECT * FROM result_tbl ORDER BY a;
11855  a   |  b  |  c   
11856------+-----+------
11857 1505 | 505 | 0505
11858 2505 | 505 | 0505
11859(2 rows)
11860
11861DELETE FROM result_tbl;
11862EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
11863EXECUTE async_pt_query (2000, 505);
11864                                        QUERY PLAN                                        
11865------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11866 Insert on public.result_tbl
11867   ->  Append
11868         Subplans Removed: 2
11869         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p1 async_pt_1
11870               Output: async_pt_1.a, async_pt_1.b, async_pt_1.c
11871               Filter: (async_pt_1.b === $2)
11872               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1 WHERE ((a < $1::integer))
11873(7 rows)
11874
11875EXECUTE async_pt_query (2000, 505);
11876SELECT * FROM result_tbl ORDER BY a;
11877  a   |  b  |  c   
11878------+-----+------
11879 1505 | 505 | 0505
11880(1 row)
11881
11882DELETE FROM result_tbl;
11883RESET plan_cache_mode;
11884CREATE TABLE local_tbl(a int, b int, c text);
11885INSERT INTO local_tbl VALUES (1505, 505, 'foo'), (2505, 505, 'bar');
11886ANALYZE local_tbl;
11887CREATE INDEX base_tbl1_idx ON base_tbl1 (a);
11888CREATE INDEX base_tbl2_idx ON base_tbl2 (a);
11889CREATE INDEX async_p3_idx ON async_p3 (a);
11890ANALYZE base_tbl1;
11891ANALYZE base_tbl2;
11892ANALYZE async_p3;
11893ALTER FOREIGN TABLE async_p1 OPTIONS (use_remote_estimate 'true');
11894ALTER FOREIGN TABLE async_p2 OPTIONS (use_remote_estimate 'true');
11895EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
11896SELECT * FROM local_tbl, async_pt WHERE local_tbl.a = async_pt.a AND local_tbl.c = 'bar';
11897                                        QUERY PLAN                                        
11898------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11899 Nested Loop
11900   Output: local_tbl.a, local_tbl.b, local_tbl.c, async_pt.a, async_pt.b, async_pt.c
11901   ->  Seq Scan on public.local_tbl
11902         Output: local_tbl.a, local_tbl.b, local_tbl.c
11903         Filter: (local_tbl.c = 'bar'::text)
11904   ->  Append
11905         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p1 async_pt_1
11906               Output: async_pt_1.a, async_pt_1.b, async_pt_1.c
11907               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1 WHERE ((a = $1::integer))
11908         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p2 async_pt_2
11909               Output: async_pt_2.a, async_pt_2.b, async_pt_2.c
11910               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl2 WHERE ((a = $1::integer))
11911         ->  Seq Scan on public.async_p3 async_pt_3
11912               Output: async_pt_3.a, async_pt_3.b, async_pt_3.c
11913               Filter: (async_pt_3.a = local_tbl.a)
11914(15 rows)
11915
11916EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, COSTS OFF, SUMMARY OFF, TIMING OFF, BUFFERS OFF)
11917SELECT * FROM local_tbl, async_pt WHERE local_tbl.a = async_pt.a AND local_tbl.c = 'bar';
11918                                    QUERY PLAN                                    
11919----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11920 Nested Loop (actual rows=1.00 loops=1)
11921   ->  Seq Scan on local_tbl (actual rows=1.00 loops=1)
11922         Filter: (c = 'bar'::text)
11923         Rows Removed by Filter: 1
11924   ->  Append (actual rows=1.00 loops=1)
11925         ->  Async Foreign Scan on async_p1 async_pt_1 (never executed)
11926         ->  Async Foreign Scan on async_p2 async_pt_2 (actual rows=1.00 loops=1)
11927         ->  Seq Scan on async_p3 async_pt_3 (never executed)
11928               Filter: (a = local_tbl.a)
11929(9 rows)
11930
11931SELECT * FROM local_tbl, async_pt WHERE local_tbl.a = async_pt.a AND local_tbl.c = 'bar';
11932  a   |  b  |  c  |  a   |  b  |  c   
11933------+-----+-----+------+-----+------
11934 2505 | 505 | bar | 2505 | 505 | 0505
11935(1 row)
11936
11937ALTER FOREIGN TABLE async_p1 OPTIONS (DROP use_remote_estimate);
11938ALTER FOREIGN TABLE async_p2 OPTIONS (DROP use_remote_estimate);
11939DROP TABLE local_tbl;
11940DROP INDEX base_tbl1_idx;
11941DROP INDEX base_tbl2_idx;
11942DROP INDEX async_p3_idx;
11943-- UNION queries
11944SET enable_sort TO off;
11945SET enable_incremental_sort TO off;
11946-- Adjust fdw_startup_cost so that we get an unordered path in the Append.
11947ALTER SERVER loopback2 OPTIONS (ADD fdw_startup_cost '0.00');
11948EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
11949INSERT INTO result_tbl
11950(SELECT a, b, 'AAA' || c FROM async_p1 ORDER BY a LIMIT 10)
11951UNION
11952(SELECT a, b, 'AAA' || c FROM async_p2 WHERE b < 10);
11953                                                   QUERY PLAN                                                    
11954-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11955 Insert on public.result_tbl
11956   ->  HashAggregate
11957         Output: async_p1.a, async_p1.b, (('AAA'::text || async_p1.c))
11958         Group Key: async_p1.a, async_p1.b, (('AAA'::text || async_p1.c))
11959         ->  Append
11960               ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p1
11961                     Output: async_p1.a, async_p1.b, ('AAA'::text || async_p1.c)
11962                     Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1 ORDER BY a ASC NULLS LAST LIMIT 10::bigint
11963               ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p2
11964                     Output: async_p2.a, async_p2.b, ('AAA'::text || async_p2.c)
11965                     Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl2 WHERE ((b < 10))
11966(11 rows)
11967
11968INSERT INTO result_tbl
11969(SELECT a, b, 'AAA' || c FROM async_p1 ORDER BY a LIMIT 10)
11970UNION
11971(SELECT a, b, 'AAA' || c FROM async_p2 WHERE b < 10);
11972SELECT * FROM result_tbl ORDER BY a;
11973  a   | b  |    c    
11974------+----+---------
11975 1000 |  0 | AAA0000
11976 1005 |  5 | AAA0005
11977 1010 | 10 | AAA0010
11978 1015 | 15 | AAA0015
11979 1020 | 20 | AAA0020
11980 1025 | 25 | AAA0025
11981 1030 | 30 | AAA0030
11982 1035 | 35 | AAA0035
11983 1040 | 40 | AAA0040
11984 1045 | 45 | AAA0045
11985 2000 |  0 | AAA0000
11986 2005 |  5 | AAA0005
11987(12 rows)
11988
11989DELETE FROM result_tbl;
11990EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
11991INSERT INTO result_tbl
11992(SELECT a, b, 'AAA' || c FROM async_p1 ORDER BY a LIMIT 10)
11993UNION ALL
11994(SELECT a, b, 'AAA' || c FROM async_p2 WHERE b < 10);
11995                                                QUERY PLAN                                                 
11996-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11997 Insert on public.result_tbl
11998   ->  Append
11999         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p1
12000               Output: async_p1.a, async_p1.b, ('AAA'::text || async_p1.c)
12001               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1 ORDER BY a ASC NULLS LAST LIMIT 10::bigint
12002         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p2
12003               Output: async_p2.a, async_p2.b, ('AAA'::text || async_p2.c)
12004               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl2 WHERE ((b < 10))
12005(8 rows)
12006

12009UNION ALL
