12028DELETE FROM result_tbl;
12029RESET enable_incremental_sort;
12030RESET enable_sort;
12031ALTER SERVER loopback2 OPTIONS (DROP fdw_startup_cost);
12032-- Disable async execution if we use gating Result nodes for pseudoconstant
12033-- quals
12034EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
12035SELECT * FROM async_pt WHERE CURRENT_USER = SESSION_USER;
12036                           QUERY PLAN                           
12037----------------------------------------------------------------
12038 Append
12039   ->  Result
12040         Output: async_pt_1.a, async_pt_1.b, async_pt_1.c
12041         One-Time Filter: (CURRENT_USER = SESSION_USER)
12042         ->  Foreign Scan on public.async_p1 async_pt_1
12043               Output: async_pt_1.a, async_pt_1.b, async_pt_1.c
12044               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1
12045   ->  Result
12046         Output: async_pt_2.a, async_pt_2.b, async_pt_2.c
12047         One-Time Filter: (CURRENT_USER = SESSION_USER)
12048         ->  Foreign Scan on public.async_p2 async_pt_2
12049               Output: async_pt_2.a, async_pt_2.b, async_pt_2.c
12050               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl2
12051   ->  Result
12052         Output: async_pt_3.a, async_pt_3.b, async_pt_3.c
12053         One-Time Filter: (CURRENT_USER = SESSION_USER)
12054         ->  Seq Scan on public.async_p3 async_pt_3
12055               Output: async_pt_3.a, async_pt_3.b, async_pt_3.c
12056(18 rows)
12057
12058EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
12059(SELECT * FROM async_p1 WHERE CURRENT_USER = SESSION_USER)
12060UNION ALL
12061(SELECT * FROM async_p2 WHERE CURRENT_USER = SESSION_USER);
12062                           QUERY PLAN                           
12063----------------------------------------------------------------
12064 Append
12065   ->  Result
12066         Output: async_p1.a, async_p1.b, async_p1.c
12067         One-Time Filter: (CURRENT_USER = SESSION_USER)
12068         ->  Foreign Scan on public.async_p1
12069               Output: async_p1.a, async_p1.b, async_p1.c
12070               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1
12071   ->  Result
12072         Output: async_p2.a, async_p2.b, async_p2.c
12073         One-Time Filter: (CURRENT_USER = SESSION_USER)
12074         ->  Foreign Scan on public.async_p2
12075               Output: async_p2.a, async_p2.b, async_p2.c
12076               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl2
12077(13 rows)
12078
12079EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
12080SELECT * FROM ((SELECT * FROM async_p1 WHERE b < 10) UNION ALL (SELECT * FROM async_p2 WHERE b < 10)) s WHERE CURRENT_USER = SESSION_USER;
12081                                   QUERY PLAN                                    
12082---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12083 Append
12084   ->  Result
12085         Output: async_p1.a, async_p1.b, async_p1.c
12086         One-Time Filter: (CURRENT_USER = SESSION_USER)
12087         ->  Foreign Scan on public.async_p1
12088               Output: async_p1.a, async_p1.b, async_p1.c
12089               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1 WHERE ((b < 10))
12090   ->  Result
12091         Output: async_p2.a, async_p2.b, async_p2.c
12092         One-Time Filter: (CURRENT_USER = SESSION_USER)
12093         ->  Foreign Scan on public.async_p2
12094               Output: async_p2.a, async_p2.b, async_p2.c
12095               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl2 WHERE ((b < 10))
12096(13 rows)
12097
12098-- Test that pending requests are processed properly
12099SET enable_mergejoin TO false;
12100SET enable_hashjoin TO false;
12101EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
12102SELECT * FROM async_pt t1, async_p2 t2 WHERE t1.a = t2.a AND t1.b === 505;
12103                           QUERY PLAN                           
12104----------------------------------------------------------------
12105 Nested Loop
12106   Output: t1.a, t1.b, t1.c, t2.a, t2.b, t2.c
12107   Join Filter: (t1.a = t2.a)
12108   ->  Append
12109         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p1 t1_1
12110               Output: t1_1.a, t1_1.b, t1_1.c
12111               Filter: (t1_1.b === 505)
12112               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1
12113         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p2 t1_2
12114               Output: t1_2.a, t1_2.b, t1_2.c
12115               Filter: (t1_2.b === 505)
12116               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl2
12117         ->  Seq Scan on public.async_p3 t1_3
12118               Output: t1_3.a, t1_3.b, t1_3.c
12119               Filter: (t1_3.b === 505)
12120   ->  Materialize
12121         Output: t2.a, t2.b, t2.c
12122         ->  Foreign Scan on public.async_p2 t2
12123               Output: t2.a, t2.b, t2.c
12124               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl2
12125(20 rows)
12126
12127SELECT * FROM async_pt t1, async_p2 t2 WHERE t1.a = t2.a AND t1.b === 505;
12128  a   |  b  |  c   |  a   |  b  |  c   
12129------+-----+------+------+-----+------
12130 2505 | 505 | 0505 | 2505 | 505 | 0505
12131(1 row)
12132
12133CREATE TABLE local_tbl (a int, b int, c text);
12134INSERT INTO local_tbl VALUES (1505, 505, 'foo');
12135ANALYZE local_tbl;
12136EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
12137SELECT * FROM local_tbl t1 LEFT JOIN (SELECT *, (SELECT count(*) FROM async_pt WHERE a < 3000) FROM async_pt WHERE a < 3000) t2 ON t1.a = t2.a;
12138                                       QUERY PLAN                                       
12139----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12140 Nested Loop Left Join
12141   Output: t1.a, t1.b, t1.c, async_pt.a, async_pt.b, async_pt.c, ((InitPlan 1).col1)
12142   Join Filter: (t1.a = async_pt.a)
12143   InitPlan 1
12144     ->  Aggregate
12145           Output: count(*)
12146           ->  Append
12147                 ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p1 async_pt_4
12148                       Remote SQL: SELECT NULL FROM public.base_tbl1 WHERE ((a < 3000))
12149                 ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p2 async_pt_5
12150                       Remote SQL: SELECT NULL FROM public.base_tbl2 WHERE ((a < 3000))
12151   ->  Seq Scan on public.local_tbl t1
12152         Output: t1.a, t1.b, t1.c
12153   ->  Append
12154         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p1 async_pt_1
12155               Output: async_pt_1.a, async_pt_1.b, async_pt_1.c, (InitPlan 1).col1
12156               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1 WHERE ((a < 3000))
12157         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p2 async_pt_2
12158               Output: async_pt_2.a, async_pt_2.b, async_pt_2.c, (InitPlan 1).col1
12159               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl2 WHERE ((a < 3000))
12160(20 rows)
12161
12162EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, COSTS OFF, SUMMARY OFF, TIMING OFF, BUFFERS OFF)
12163SELECT * FROM local_tbl t1 LEFT JOIN (SELECT *, (SELECT count(*) FROM async_pt WHERE a < 3000) FROM async_pt WHERE a < 3000) t2 ON t1.a = t2.a;
12164                                         QUERY PLAN                                         
12165--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12166 Nested Loop Left Join (actual rows=1.00 loops=1)
12167   Join Filter: (t1.a = async_pt.a)
12168   Rows Removed by Join Filter: 399
12169   InitPlan 1
12170     ->  Aggregate (actual rows=1.00 loops=1)
12171           ->  Append (actual rows=400.00 loops=1)
12172                 ->  Async Foreign Scan on async_p1 async_pt_4 (actual rows=200.00 loops=1)
12173                 ->  Async Foreign Scan on async_p2 async_pt_5 (actual rows=200.00 loops=1)
12174   ->  Seq Scan on local_tbl t1 (actual rows=1.00 loops=1)
12175   ->  Append (actual rows=400.00 loops=1)
12176         ->  Async Foreign Scan on async_p1 async_pt_1 (actual rows=200.00 loops=1)
12177         ->  Async Foreign Scan on async_p2 async_pt_2 (actual rows=200.00 loops=1)
12178(12 rows)
12179
12180SELECT * FROM local_tbl t1 LEFT JOIN (SELECT *, (SELECT count(*) FROM async_pt WHERE a < 3000) FROM async_pt WHERE a < 3000) t2 ON t1.a = t2.a;

12182------+-----+-----+------+-----+------+-------
12183 1505 | 505 | foo | 1505 | 505 | 0505 |   400
12184(1 row)

12227-- Check with foreign modify
12228CREATE TABLE base_tbl3 (a int, b int, c text);
12229CREATE FOREIGN TABLE remote_tbl (a int, b int, c text)
12230  SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'base_tbl3');
12231INSERT INTO remote_tbl VALUES (2505, 505, 'bar');
12232CREATE TABLE base_tbl4 (a int, b int, c text);
12233CREATE FOREIGN TABLE insert_tbl (a int, b int, c text)
12234  SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'base_tbl4');
12235EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
12236INSERT INTO insert_tbl (SELECT * FROM local_tbl UNION ALL SELECT * FROM remote_tbl);
12237                               QUERY PLAN                                
12238-------------------------------------------------------------------------
12239 Insert on public.insert_tbl
12240   Remote SQL: INSERT INTO public.base_tbl4(a, b, c) VALUES ($1, $2, $3)
12241   Batch Size: 1
12242   ->  Append
12243         ->  Seq Scan on public.local_tbl
12244               Output: local_tbl.a, local_tbl.b, local_tbl.c
12245         ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.remote_tbl
12246               Output: remote_tbl.a, remote_tbl.b, remote_tbl.c
12247               Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl3
12248(9 rows)
12249
12250INSERT INTO insert_tbl (SELECT * FROM local_tbl UNION ALL SELECT * FROM remote_tbl);
12251SELECT * FROM insert_tbl ORDER BY a;

12253------+-----+-----
12254 1505 | 505 | foo
12255 2505 | 505 | bar
12256(2 rows)
12257
12258-- Check with direct modify
12259EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
12260WITH t AS (UPDATE remote_tbl SET c = c || c RETURNING *)
12261INSERT INTO join_tbl SELECT * FROM async_pt LEFT JOIN t ON (async_pt.a = t.a AND async_pt.b = t.b) WHERE async_pt.b === 505;
12262                                       QUERY PLAN                                       
12263----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12264 Insert on public.join_tbl
12265   CTE t
12266     ->  Update on public.remote_tbl
12267           Output: remote_tbl.a, remote_tbl.b, remote_tbl.c
12268           ->  Foreign Update on public.remote_tbl
12269                 Remote SQL: UPDATE public.base_tbl3 SET c = (c || c) RETURNING a, b, c
12270   ->  Nested Loop Left Join
12271         Output: async_pt.a, async_pt.b, async_pt.c, t.a, t.b, t.c
12272         Join Filter: ((async_pt.a = t.a) AND (async_pt.b = t.b))
12273         ->  Append
12274               ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p1 async_pt_1
12275                     Output: async_pt_1.a, async_pt_1.b, async_pt_1.c
12276                     Filter: (async_pt_1.b === 505)
12277                     Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl1
12278               ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.async_p2 async_pt_2
12279                     Output: async_pt_2.a, async_pt_2.b, async_pt_2.c
12280                     Filter: (async_pt_2.b === 505)
12281                     Remote SQL: SELECT a, b, c FROM public.base_tbl2
12282               ->  Seq Scan on public.async_p3 async_pt_3
12283                     Output: async_pt_3.a, async_pt_3.b, async_pt_3.c
12284                     Filter: (async_pt_3.b === 505)
12285         ->  CTE Scan on t
12286               Output: t.a, t.b, t.c
12287(23 rows)
12288
12289WITH t AS (UPDATE remote_tbl SET c = c || c RETURNING *)
12290INSERT INTO join_tbl SELECT * FROM async_pt LEFT JOIN t ON (async_pt.a = t.a AND async_pt.b = t.b) WHERE async_pt.b === 505;
12291SELECT * FROM join_tbl ORDER BY a1;

12293------+-----+------+------+-----+--------
12294 1505 | 505 | 0505 |      |     | 
12295 2505 | 505 | 0505 | 2505 | 505 | barbar
12296 3505 | 505 | 0505 |      |     | 
12297(3 rows)

12299DELETE FROM join_tbl;
12300DROP TABLE local_tbl;
12301DROP FOREIGN TABLE remote_tbl;
12302DROP FOREIGN TABLE insert_tbl;
12303DROP TABLE base_tbl3;
12304DROP TABLE base_tbl4;
12305RESET enable_mergejoin;
12306RESET enable_hashjoin;
12307-- Test that UPDATE/DELETE with inherited target works with async_capable enabled
12308EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
12309UPDATE async_pt SET c = c || c WHERE b = 0 RETURNING *;
12310                                                QUERY PLAN                                                
12311----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12312 Update on public.async_pt
12313   Output: async_pt_1.a, async_pt_1.b, async_pt_1.c
12314   Foreign Update on public.async_p1 async_pt_1
12315   Foreign Update on public.async_p2 async_pt_2
12316   Update on public.async_p3 async_pt_3
12317   ->  Append
12318         ->  Foreign Update on public.async_p1 async_pt_1
12319               Remote SQL: UPDATE public.base_tbl1 SET c = (c || c) WHERE ((b = 0)) RETURNING a, b, c
12320         ->  Foreign Update on public.async_p2 async_pt_2
12321               Remote SQL: UPDATE public.base_tbl2 SET c = (c || c) WHERE ((b = 0)) RETURNING a, b, c
12322         ->  Seq Scan on public.async_p3 async_pt_3
12323               Output: (async_pt_3.c || async_pt_3.c), async_pt_3.tableoid, async_pt_3.ctid, NULL::record
12324               Filter: (async_pt_3.b = 0)
12325(13 rows)
12326
12327UPDATE async_pt SET c = c || c WHERE b = 0 RETURNING *;
12328  a   | b |    c     
12329------+---+----------
12330 1000 | 0 | 00000000
12331 2000 | 0 | 00000000
12332 3000 | 0 | 00000000
12333(3 rows)
12334
12335EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
12336DELETE FROM async_pt WHERE b = 0 RETURNING *;
12337                                        QUERY PLAN                                        
12338------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12339 Delete on public.async_pt
12340   Output: async_pt_1.a, async_pt_1.b, async_pt_1.c
12341   Foreign Delete on public.async_p1 async_pt_1
12342   Foreign Delete on public.async_p2 async_pt_2
12343   Delete on public.async_p3 async_pt_3
12344   ->  Append
12345         ->  Foreign Delete on public.async_p1 async_pt_1
12346               Remote SQL: DELETE FROM public.base_tbl1 WHERE ((b = 0)) RETURNING a, b, c
12347         ->  Foreign Delete on public.async_p2 async_pt_2
12348               Remote SQL: DELETE FROM public.base_tbl2 WHERE ((b = 0)) RETURNING a, b, c
12349         ->  Seq Scan on public.async_p3 async_pt_3
12350               Output: async_pt_3.tableoid, async_pt_3.ctid
12351               Filter: (async_pt_3.b = 0)
12352(13 rows)
12353
12354DELETE FROM async_pt WHERE b = 0 RETURNING *;
12355  a   | b |    c     
12356------+---+----------
12357 1000 | 0 | 00000000
12358 2000 | 0 | 00000000
12359 3000 | 0 | 00000000
12360(3 rows)
12361
12362-- Check EXPLAIN ANALYZE for a query that scans empty partitions asynchronously
12363DELETE FROM async_p1;
12364DELETE FROM async_p2;
12365DELETE FROM async_p3;
12366EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, COSTS OFF, SUMMARY OFF, TIMING OFF, BUFFERS OFF)
12367SELECT * FROM async_pt;
12368                                 QUERY PLAN                                 
12369----------------------------------------------------------------------------
12370 Append (actual rows=0.00 loops=1)
12371   ->  Async Foreign Scan on async_p1 async_pt_1 (actual rows=0.00 loops=1)
12372   ->  Async Foreign Scan on async_p2 async_pt_2 (actual rows=0.00 loops=1)
12373   ->  Seq Scan on async_p3 async_pt_3 (actual rows=0.00 loops=1)
12374(4 rows)
12375
12376-- Clean up
12377DROP TABLE async_pt;
12378DROP TABLE base_tbl1;
12379DROP TABLE base_tbl2;
12380DROP TABLE result_tbl;
12381DROP TABLE join_tbl;
12382-- Test that an asynchronous fetch is processed before restarting the scan in
12383-- ReScanForeignScan
12384CREATE TABLE base_tbl (a int, b int);
12385INSERT INTO base_tbl VALUES (1, 11), (2, 22), (3, 33);
12386CREATE FOREIGN TABLE foreign_tbl (b int)
12387  SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'base_tbl');
12388CREATE FOREIGN TABLE foreign_tbl2 () INHERITS (foreign_tbl)
12389  SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'base_tbl');
12390EXPLAIN (VERBOSE, COSTS OFF)
12391SELECT a FROM base_tbl WHERE (a, random() > 0) IN (SELECT a, random() > 0 FROM foreign_tbl);
12392                                                  QUERY PLAN                                                   
12393---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12394 Seq Scan on public.base_tbl
12395   Output: base_tbl.a
12396   Filter: (ANY ((base_tbl.a = (SubPlan 1).col1) AND ((random() > '0'::double precision) = (SubPlan 1).col2)))
12397   SubPlan 1
12398     ->  Result
12399           Output: base_tbl.a, (random() > '0'::double precision)
12400           ->  Append
12401                 ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.foreign_tbl foreign_tbl_1
12402                       Remote SQL: SELECT NULL FROM public.base_tbl
12403                 ->  Async Foreign Scan on public.foreign_tbl2 foreign_tbl_2
12404                       Remote SQL: SELECT NULL FROM public.base_tbl
12405(11 rows)
12406
12407SELECT a FROM base_tbl WHERE (a, random() > 0) IN (SELECT a, random() > 0 FROM foreign_tbl);
12408 a 
12409---
12410 1
12411 2
12412 3
12413(3 rows)
12414
12415-- Clean up
12416DROP FOREIGN TABLE foreign_tbl CASCADE;
12417NOTICE:  drop cascades to foreign table foreign_tbl2
12418DROP TABLE base_tbl;
12419ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (DROP async_capable);
12420ALTER SERVER loopback2 OPTIONS (DROP async_capable);
12421-- ===================================================================
12422-- test invalid server, foreign table and foreign data wrapper options
12423-- ===================================================================
12424-- Invalid fdw_startup_cost option
12425CREATE SERVER inv_scst FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER postgres_fdw
12426	OPTIONS(fdw_startup_cost '100$%$#$#');
12427ERROR:  invalid value for floating point option "fdw_startup_cost": 100$%$#$#
12428-- Invalid fdw_tuple_cost option
12429CREATE SERVER inv_scst FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER postgres_fdw
12430	OPTIONS(fdw_tuple_cost '100$%$#$#');
12431ERROR:  invalid value for floating point option "fdw_tuple_cost": 100$%$#$#
12432-- Invalid fetch_size option
12433CREATE FOREIGN TABLE inv_fsz (c1 int )
12434	SERVER loopback OPTIONS (fetch_size '100$%$#$#');
12435ERROR:  invalid value for integer option "fetch_size": 100$%$#$#
12436-- Invalid batch_size option
12437CREATE FOREIGN TABLE inv_bsz (c1 int )
12438	SERVER loopback OPTIONS (batch_size '100$%$#$#');
12439ERROR:  invalid value for integer option "batch_size": 100$%$#$#
12440-- No option is allowed to be specified at foreign data wrapper level
12441ALTER FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER postgres_fdw OPTIONS (nonexistent 'fdw');
12442ERROR:  invalid option "nonexistent"
12443HINT:  There are no valid options in this context.
12444-- ===================================================================
12445-- test postgres_fdw.application_name GUC
12446-- ===================================================================
12447-- To avoid race conditions in checking the remote session's application_name,
12448-- use this view to make the remote session itself read its application_name.
12449CREATE VIEW my_application_name AS
12450  SELECT application_name FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE pid = pg_backend_pid();
12451CREATE FOREIGN TABLE remote_application_name (application_name text)
12452  SERVER loopback2
12453  OPTIONS (schema_name 'public', table_name 'my_application_name');
12454SELECT count(*) FROM remote_application_name;
12455 count 
12456-------
12457     1
12458(1 row)
12459
12460-- Specify escape sequences in application_name option of a server
12461-- object so as to test that they are replaced with status information
12462-- expectedly.  Note that we are also relying on ALTER SERVER to force
12463-- the remote session to be restarted with its new application name.
12464--
12465-- Since pg_stat_activity.application_name may be truncated to less than
12466-- NAMEDATALEN characters, note that substring() needs to be used
12467-- at the condition of test query to make sure that the string consisting
12468-- of database name and process ID is also less than that.
12469ALTER SERVER loopback2 OPTIONS (application_name 'fdw_%d%p');
12470SELECT count(*) FROM remote_application_name
12471  WHERE application_name =
12472    substring('fdw_' || current_database() || pg_backend_pid() for
12473      current_setting('max_identifier_length')::int);
12474 count 
12475-------
12476     1
12477(1 row)
12478
12479-- postgres_fdw.application_name overrides application_name option
12480-- of a server object if both settings are present.
12481ALTER SERVER loopback2 OPTIONS (SET application_name 'fdw_wrong');
12482SET postgres_fdw.application_name TO 'fdw_%a%u%%';
12483SELECT count(*) FROM remote_application_name
12484  WHERE application_name =
12485    substring('fdw_' || current_setting('application_name') ||
12486      CURRENT_USER || '%' for current_setting('max_identifier_length')::int);
12487 count 
12488-------
12489     1
12490(1 row)
12491
12492RESET postgres_fdw.application_name;
12493-- Test %c (session ID) and %C (cluster name) escape sequences.
12494ALTER SERVER loopback2 OPTIONS (SET application_name 'fdw_%C%c');
12495SELECT count(*) FROM remote_application_name
12496  WHERE application_name =
12497    substring('fdw_' || current_setting('cluster_name') ||
12498      to_hex(trunc(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (SELECT backend_start FROM
12499      pg_stat_get_activity(pg_backend_pid()))))::integer) || '.' ||
12500      to_hex(pg_backend_pid())
12501      for current_setting('max_identifier_length')::int);
12502 count 
12503-------
12504     1
12505(1 row)
12506
12507-- Clean up.
12508DROP FOREIGN TABLE remote_application_name;
12509DROP VIEW my_application_name;
12510-- ===================================================================
12511-- test parallel commit and parallel abort
12512-- ===================================================================
12513ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (ADD parallel_commit 'true');
12514ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (ADD parallel_abort 'true');
12515ALTER SERVER loopback2 OPTIONS (ADD parallel_commit 'true');
12516ALTER SERVER loopback2 OPTIONS (ADD parallel_abort 'true');
12517CREATE TABLE ploc1 (f1 int, f2 text);
12518CREATE FOREIGN TABLE prem1 (f1 int, f2 text)
12519  SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'ploc1');
12520CREATE TABLE ploc2 (f1 int, f2 text);
12521CREATE FOREIGN TABLE prem2 (f1 int, f2 text)
12522  SERVER loopback2 OPTIONS (table_name 'ploc2');
12523BEGIN;
12524INSERT INTO prem1 VALUES (101, 'foo');
12525INSERT INTO prem2 VALUES (201, 'bar');
12526COMMIT;
12527SELECT * FROM prem1;
12528 f1  | f2  
12529-----+-----
12530 101 | foo
12531(1 row)
12532
12533SELECT * FROM prem2;
12534 f1  | f2  
12535-----+-----
12536 201 | bar
12537(1 row)
12538
12539BEGIN;
12540SAVEPOINT s;
12541INSERT INTO prem1 VALUES (102, 'foofoo');
12542INSERT INTO prem2 VALUES (202, 'barbar');
12543RELEASE SAVEPOINT s;
12544COMMIT;
12545SELECT * FROM prem1;
12546 f1  |   f2   
12547-----+--------
12548 101 | foo
12549 102 | foofoo
12550(2 rows)
12551
12552SELECT * FROM prem2;
12553 f1  |   f2   
12554-----+--------
12555 201 | bar
12556 202 | barbar
12557(2 rows)
12558
12559-- This tests executing DEALLOCATE ALL against foreign servers in parallel
12560-- during pre-commit
12561BEGIN;
12562SAVEPOINT s;
12563INSERT INTO prem1 VALUES (103, 'baz');
12564INSERT INTO prem2 VALUES (203, 'qux');
12565ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT s;
12566RELEASE SAVEPOINT s;
12567INSERT INTO prem1 VALUES (104, 'bazbaz');
12568INSERT INTO prem2 VALUES (204, 'quxqux');
12569COMMIT;
12570SELECT * FROM prem1;
12571 f1  |   f2   
12572-----+--------
12573 101 | foo
12574 102 | foofoo
12575 104 | bazbaz
12576(3 rows)
12577
12578SELECT * FROM prem2;
12579 f1  |   f2   
12580-----+--------
12581 201 | bar
12582 202 | barbar
12583 204 | quxqux
12584(3 rows)
12585
12586BEGIN;
12587INSERT INTO prem1 VALUES (105, 'test1');
12588INSERT INTO prem2 VALUES (205, 'test2');
12589ABORT;
12590SELECT * FROM prem1;
12591 f1  |   f2   
12592-----+--------
12593 101 | foo
12594 102 | foofoo
12595 104 | bazbaz
12596(3 rows)
12597
12598SELECT * FROM prem2;
12599 f1  |   f2   
12600-----+--------
12601 201 | bar
12602 202 | barbar
12603 204 | quxqux
12604(3 rows)
12605
12606-- This tests executing DEALLOCATE ALL against foreign servers in parallel
12607-- during post-abort
12608BEGIN;
12609SAVEPOINT s;
12610INSERT INTO prem1 VALUES (105, 'test1');
12611INSERT INTO prem2 VALUES (205, 'test2');
12612ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT s;
12613RELEASE SAVEPOINT s;
12614INSERT INTO prem1 VALUES (105, 'test1');
12615INSERT INTO prem2 VALUES (205, 'test2');
12616ABORT;
12617SELECT * FROM prem1;
12618 f1  |   f2   
12619-----+--------
12620 101 | foo
12621 102 | foofoo
12622 104 | bazbaz
12623(3 rows)
12624
12625SELECT * FROM prem2;
12626 f1  |   f2   
12627-----+--------
12628 201 | bar
12629 202 | barbar
12630 204 | quxqux
12631(3 rows)
12632
12633ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (DROP parallel_commit);
12634ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (DROP parallel_abort);
12635ALTER SERVER loopback2 OPTIONS (DROP parallel_commit);
12636ALTER SERVER loopback2 OPTIONS (DROP parallel_abort);
12637-- ===================================================================
12638-- test for ANALYZE sampling
12639-- ===================================================================
12640CREATE TABLE analyze_table (id int, a text, b bigint);
12641CREATE FOREIGN TABLE analyze_ftable (id int, a text, b bigint)
12642       SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'analyze_table');
12643INSERT INTO analyze_table (SELECT x FROM generate_series(1,1000) x);
12644ANALYZE analyze_table;
12645SET default_statistics_target = 10;
12646ANALYZE analyze_table;
12647ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (analyze_sampling 'invalid');
12648ERROR:  invalid value for string option "analyze_sampling": invalid
12649ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (analyze_sampling 'auto');
12650ANALYZE analyze_ftable;
12651ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (SET analyze_sampling 'system');
12652ANALYZE analyze_ftable;
12653ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (SET analyze_sampling 'bernoulli');
12654ANALYZE analyze_ftable;
12655ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (SET analyze_sampling 'random');
12656ANALYZE analyze_ftable;
12657ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (SET analyze_sampling 'off');
12658ANALYZE analyze_ftable;
12659-- cleanup
12660DROP FOREIGN TABLE analyze_ftable;
12661DROP TABLE analyze_table;
12662-- ===================================================================
12663-- test for postgres_fdw_get_connections function with check_conn = true
12664-- ===================================================================
12665-- Disable debug_discard_caches in order to manage remote connections
12666SET debug_discard_caches TO '0';
12667-- The text of the error might vary across platforms, so only show SQLSTATE.
12668\set VERBOSITY sqlstate
12669SELECT 1 FROM postgres_fdw_disconnect_all();
12670 ?column? 
12671----------
12672        1
12673(1 row)
12674
12675ALTER SERVER loopback OPTIONS (SET application_name 'fdw_conn_check');
12676SELECT 1 FROM ft1 LIMIT 1;
12677 ?column? 
12678----------
12679        1
12680(1 row)
12681
12682-- Since the remote server is still connected, "closed" should be FALSE,
12683-- or NULL if the connection status check is not available.
12684-- In this test, the remote backend handling this connection should have
12685-- application_name set to "fdw_conn_check", so remote_backend_pid should
12686-- match the PID from the pg_stat_activity entry with that application_name.
12687SELECT server_name,
12688  CASE WHEN closed IS NOT true THEN false ELSE true END AS closed,
12689  remote_backend_pid = (SELECT pid FROM pg_stat_activity
12690  WHERE application_name = 'fdw_conn_check') AS remote_backend_pid
12691  FROM postgres_fdw_get_connections(true);
12692 server_name | closed | remote_backend_pid 
12693-------------+--------+--------------------
12694 loopback    | f      | t
12695(1 row)
12696
12697-- After terminating the remote backend, since the connection is closed,
12698-- "closed" should be TRUE, or NULL if the connection status check
12699-- is not available. Despite the termination, remote_backend_pid should
12700-- still show the non-zero PID of the terminated remote backend.
12701DO $$ BEGIN
12702PERFORM pg_terminate_backend(pid, 180000) FROM pg_stat_activity
12703  WHERE application_name = 'fdw_conn_check';
12704END $$;
12705SELECT server_name,
12706  CASE WHEN closed IS NOT false THEN true ELSE false END AS closed,
12707  remote_backend_pid <> 0 AS remote_backend_pid
12708  FROM postgres_fdw_get_connections(true);
12709 server_name | closed | remote_backend_pid 
12710-------------+--------+--------------------
12711 loopback    | t      | t
12712(1 row)
12713
12714-- Clean up
12715\set VERBOSITY default
12716RESET debug_discard_caches;
12717-- ===================================================================
12718-- test cleanup of failed connections on abort
12719-- ===================================================================
12720CREATE VIEW my_backend_pid (pid) AS SELECT pg_backend_pid();
12721CREATE FOREIGN TABLE remote_backend_pid (pid int)
12722  SERVER loopback OPTIONS (table_name 'my_backend_pid');
12723CREATE FUNCTION wait_for_backend_termination(int) RETURNS void AS $$
12724  BEGIN
12725    WHILE (SELECT count(*) FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE pid = $1) > 0
12726    LOOP
12727      PERFORM pg_stat_clear_snapshot();
12728    END LOOP;
12729  END
12730$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
12731SET client_min_messages = 'ERROR';
12732BEGIN;
12733DECLARE c CURSOR FOR SELECT * FROM ft1 ORDER BY c1;
12734SAVEPOINT s;
12735SELECT pid AS remote_pid FROM remote_backend_pid \gset
12736SELECT pg_terminate_backend(:remote_pid);
12737 pg_terminate_backend 
12738----------------------
12739 t
12740(1 row)
12741
12742SELECT wait_for_backend_termination(:remote_pid);
12743 wait_for_backend_termination 
12744------------------------------
12745 
12746(1 row)
12747
12748ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT s;
12749SELECT pid FROM remote_backend_pid;
12750ERROR:  connection to server "loopback" cannot be used due to abort cleanup failure
12751ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT s;
12752RELEASE SAVEPOINT s;
12753FETCH c;
12754ERROR:  no connection to the server
12755CONTEXT:  remote SQL command: DECLARE c1 CURSOR FOR
12756SELECT "C 1", c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7, c8 FROM "S 1"."T 1" ORDER BY "C 1" ASC NULLS LAST
12757ABORT;
12758BEGIN;
12759DECLARE c CURSOR FOR SELECT * FROM ft1 ORDER BY c1;
12760FETCH c;
12761 c1 | c2 |        c3         |              c4              |            c5            | c6 |     c7     | c8  
12762----+----+-------------------+------------------------------+--------------------------+----+------------+-----
12763  1 |  2 | 00001_trig_update | Fri Jan 02 00:00:00 1970 PST | Fri Jan 02 00:00:00 1970 | 1  | 1          | foo
12764(1 row)
12765
12766SAVEPOINT s;
12767SELECT pid AS remote_pid FROM remote_backend_pid \gset
12768SELECT pg_terminate_backend(:remote_pid);
12769 pg_terminate_backend 
12770----------------------
12771 t
12772(1 row)
12773
12774SELECT wait_for_backend_termination(:remote_pid);
12775 wait_for_backend_termination 
12776------------------------------
12777 
12778(1 row)
12779
12780ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT s;
12781SELECT pid FROM remote_backend_pid;
12782ERROR:  connection to server "loopback" cannot be used due to abort cleanup failure
12783ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT s;
12784RELEASE SAVEPOINT s;
12785CLOSE c;
12786ERROR:  no connection to the server
12787CONTEXT:  remote SQL command: CLOSE c1
12788ABORT;
12789RESET client_min_messages;
12790DROP FUNCTION wait_for_backend_termination(int);
12791DROP FOREIGN TABLE remote_backend_pid;
12792DROP VIEW my_backend_pid;
